I’m 5’9, 165 lbs apple shaped. No waist and skinny butt and legs. The waist goes right up to or slightly below my belly button. I do not find them see through but with the white I obviously wear nude or white underpants. I wouldn’t pay full price for these, but am happy with my purchase. If they shrink or do anything weird in the wash I will update. I try to wear jeans a bunch before washing. They are so stretchy that they are extremely comfortable but do not sag during the day!

Very comfortable...but they stink! I think I’m giving these a 3.5, I went back and forth with the star rating. These are very comfortable pants. I wasn’t sure whether to go with the 0 or the 2, and the short or the regular. I’m 5’3.5” and my go-to Jean is a size 1 in Celebrity Pink. I ordered the Size 0 Regular. They seemed a tad long for me (I think I like the shorter length on me in tighter jeans) but they were perfect with rolled cuffs. They are definitely more of a relaxed look than I am used to. The legs were looser. I liked the rise and it was high enough to contain everything but I don’t think it gave me a flat butt the way some high-waist jeans do. Great lighter color. I think I would have preferred the shorter length, but with the looseness of the pants I’m not sure that would’ve looked better. So I’m ultimately glad I went with the 0R. If you are on the smaller side of a size 0, you may find these too big, but that’s what free returns are for. The thing is these pants STINK. They smell so bad. It’s like formaldehyde meets moth balls. I thought after a wash the smell would be gone but nope! These still wreaked after a wash. I’m not going to return them and hope that a few more washes will get the smell out, but if you’re sensitive to that, I’d skip these pants.

Satisfaction varies whether you want skin tight or not I like ankle skinny jeans that are not skin tight. In Amazon Essentials Bermuda shorts I wear size 4, generally I wear a six. I do not like my clothes tight. Based on reviews I sized up to an 8. The jeans have lots of stretch and if you want leggings look these pants would be great. For me, they are so so. I did keep them as I wanted some tan jeans and they had been hard to find. I am 5’1” and weigh 100#. My perfect ankle skinny jeans are Calvin Klein Ankle Skinny in size 8, which are a little looser thru the legs and ankle, but I don’t think they make any more. Hope the review helps.

The pants aren’t see through on the leg (they are somewhat on the top, but that’s mostly cover by shirts), but that hem stands out. It wasn’t bad enough that I wouldn’t wear them though and almost could be considered a styling feature depending on how you look at it.

Super inconsistent sizing! AWFUL quality fabric. UPDATE: ugh. just look at the new photos. Changing to 1-star. Do NOT recommend. After a few months, I’ve found that these jeans are total garbage. They are droopy and unflattering, and the material quality is extremely poor. Even freshly washed, they never un-bag. The elastic in the fabric buckles out or pulls in weird looking strips in multiple places, and it doesn’t even smooth out after putting them on. TERRIBLE! Wish I never bought them, even at my insanely cheap price. Overall: Meh. I would have returned these if they were at what appears to be the very reasonable sounding full price. - Material is very stretchy, but a bit thin. Nice for summer, but you can catch some VPL even through the dark wash. - Inseam length runs short. I wear flats and almost always have to get short lengths for a 30" inseam, but the short of these hit in an awkward spot above my ankle bone. - The listing didn't specify, but these are pretty high cut. They hit right at my belly button. - Butt pockets are higher than I expected, but not too 90s looking. I bought a pair of these in 2 short. Initial fit seemed just okay, but for the very low price I decided to grab a second identical pair in case I ended up loving them after a few wears. Turns out... They are clearance priced for a reason! In the photos, you can see two pairs of the same jean- same exact size, same exact color, but cut at least a size differently. The waists have about 1-2" difference in diameter! Luckily, the material is stretchy enough that the cut too small jeans are just good enough. And the cut is high enough that cut large ones are also just good enough. Neither are great, but for the price I found them at, I'll keep them while I wait for my normal jeans to go on sale (AE jeggings).

These came just above my ankle bones and are a bit tighter than I'd like but they're still super comfy and stretchy and I would suspect they may stretch out after wearing a bit. I think they're super cute though and are really comfy!

Fit small on me, should’ve ordered a size or two up. So I have to laugh because a lot of the other reviews are complaining about the jeans being too big or too short and I am having the exact opposite experience. I’m 5’3” and 175lbs. I have no butt and awkward shaped hips I’ve been told. I typically wear a size 10-12 and when I’m able to I buy “short” size. These didn’t have a short size so I followed the sizing chart which said if I wear a size 12 I should get a 31. I got a 31 and I could honestly barely button them. They would be okay as standing jeans but sitting in these would be another story for me. They are maybe an inch too long as well. I really needed probably a size 33 in the waist which is crazy because they say that’s a size 16! They also come up higher than my belly button although the model in the photo has them coming up below her belly button. I’m not sure if maybe they are hitting my waist too high because I am short or what’s happening. Maybe they need to sit lower on my waist (if they fit) but then they would be extremely too long. I love the color, I honestly like the way the jeans look on me and I felt like the bagginess wasn’t too much for me. They just didn’t fit me and either came up too high on my body shape or something. Good quality denim just a bad fit for my personal body type

Don't hesitate to get these! I really wish these jeans came in petite sizes. I'm 5 foot 3 and 110 pounds. I knew they would be large on me, but they are so stinkin' cute! The size 4 will work, but I'll need to find the right belt to hold them in place without bunching up the waistline. I wanted a slightly baggy fit anyway. They are also a little longer than I'd prefer, but they don't drag on the ground when I wear shoes. I have always been a fan of the button fly, and this one looks really nice. It's high waisted, so it could be long in the crotch if you decide to size up like I did. There is plenty of pocket space in the front and back. The rear end on my pair has a slightly faded look, but it's not dramatic. I know I've said some critical things about these jeans, but all of the problems I have can be attributed to the fact that a smaller size would fit me better. The jeans themselves look fantastic, very fashionable! If they can look this good on someone who doesn't exactly fit them, imagine how good they will look on a person who does!

Stretchy, soft and comfortable. These wide leg, high waisted jeans are very comfortable. They fit me well, except because of my shortness (5' 4") they are not really cropped on me. More like flood pants, but I am okay with that. I do not have to worry about the bottoms getting wet or my lower legs getting too cold, lol. They have three buttons that are quite stiff to unbutton when you get them but are fine after the first time. The zipper works well. There are four functional pockets, two in front and two in back. The pockets are a little smallish, but would hold a tissue or car keys. As you can see in one of my pictures, if you are apple shaped like I am, the white of the inside of the pocket (on the front) will show, despite the fit being not too tight. Not a problem for me as I most always like to wear shirts / blouses that are long enough to cover at least most of my bottom. Not sure how the pocket issue could have been prevented in the designing of them, but there probably is a way. These jeans seem to be mostly well made. The only thing I noticed really was that the buttons are sort of the cheap type that wiggle and rattle. Not sure what the durability would be of those. Aside from that, I can recommend these jeans and I am happy with them. The reason I only gave four stars was for the buttons and the white of the pocket showing. If anything comes up about those buttons on mine, I will update my review.

Ridiculously wide legs, inseam shorter than given on size chart I ordered these in the “Blue” color in size Medium. I typically wear a size 8 in pants, but the size chart could have had me in a Large instead. However, the listing says the model is “wearing size Medium, Hips 40 / Height 5’3” / Weight 136. I’m about an inch and half taller, weigh about the same, and hips are maybe an inch smaller. The Medium fits me very well and is comfortable. The inseam is at least an inch shorter than the stated 26.38” (I measured it at about 25”). The fabric has a nice weight and is soft and stretchy. The waistband is beautiful and also comfortable. It seems like it would retain its size and shape, however, I have not worn them long enough to test this theory. There are no belt loops to hold them up should they stretch out, or if they would be too loose on you. The buttons seem a little loose, but don’t show any indication of coming off.Now, I know these are advertised as wide leg, but they have W-I-D-E legs! Since they are denim which has structure, If I stand with my feet about hip width apart (a natural stance), the legs of these jeans cross over each other, which looks rather ridiculous. The seams go crooked and it looks like I'm not standing straight even though I am. I have to stand with my legs spread strangely apart for them to look as they do on the model. If only I could slim down the legs, these would be attractive, but as they are, I just can’t see myself ever wearing them, not even just around the house, as the legs crash together with every step. I’ve included photos of how they look on me (5’4.5”, 30 waist, 39 hip, about 29 inseam), and also some with a measuring tape to show the actual measurements of the size Medium. I hope this helps in your decision.

Great fit! I got these for my daughter’s birthday. She is 5’6”, about 125 lbs and usually wears a small. These fit well. They are stretchy and have some give. The length hits her about 2 inches above her ankle. The jeans are flattering and comfortable. They are a mid weight Jean.

Perfect fit! Super cute, cropped jeans! I love the look, I love the fit! Runs true to size chart, I ordered a size small. For reference I’m 5’7 approximately 135lbs, I have thick hips and thighs with a smaller waist. Denim is thin, lightweight and comfortable to wear! I actually think these look better in person than the listing photos! 5-stars, great quality and well made! Highly recommend!

Cute jeans :) Hey there, so I don't usually buy clothes online because they simply don't fit; but I have learned to buy larger then I typically would need and it works out. In this case I ordered XL and they are a very comfortable fit. I am 5 ft 6 in and about 170 lb; I'm a little "hippy" at 41" and that's where jeans usually don't work for me. But these are a little loose, which I like, and stretchy; so I have mobility, which is another important factor. I did immediately notice that the open stars on the jeans look like little stickers you would just peel and stick in your notebook, but I have washed and dried them and they stayed on. I would imagine they won't last forever, but they would look fine without them, or I could put something else in their place. The material underneath the holes is like a microfiber, so it's thin, breathable and stretchy. I will be ordering some of the other designs for sure.

Amazing and Comfortable! Not Stylish... on me...BUT I love them. I bought these for work. I love them for work! I needed 100% cotton jeans and these did the trick. They’re not super flattering but boy are they comfortable! So great for lounging, running errands, they’d have FANTASTIC for gardening, and they are great contract/construction work. I wore them when I visited power plants for work and I will wear them again for gardening and other outdoor adventures when it’s hot out and I need long pants. I love these pants!! I wish they were a little more stylish so I could wear them more often... I mean... I could... but I don’t have that lien or courage... yet. BUT, I will wear these every opportunity I get and maybe I can find a way to make them stylish. I think it’s my body. Some women could definitely make these stylish. I’m a little shorter and more petite. I think for these to be stylish you probably need longer legs. I have to to roll these at the bottom and I look like a little girl. I also got the smallest size they offered and they get a little roomy... they may be made to be that way though. Anyway, I seriously do love these. Comfiest and most breathable jeans that I have ever worn. I want MORE 100% cotton jeans. Please!! They truly are amazing. GET THEM. They may look great on you, just not as much on me. If the way don’t look “cute,” keep them for fun and rugged activities/gardening. P.S. I got a size 4. This was the lowest offered when I bought them. They stay up because of where they sit on my hips though. I’m something like a 27in waist, 34-35inch hips?? Waist size doesn’t matter as much for these... or they didn’t for me... I’m 5’3”

Run a bit big I love levis! I have some shorter 501’s that I’ve had for a few years but I am at the age where longer shorts are definitely more appropriate for most events. I tried a size 25 based off the size chart and found them way too big, so I exchanged for a 24 and they are perfect. I line dry most of my clothes so shrinkage is unlikely, but if they do happen to shrink, I still have some space all around to account for that without being too baggy. *If you dry these, they will shrink.* 501’s are not pre shrunk because they are designed to be “broke in” by sitting in a tub of water with the jeans on, and wearing them until they dry. They are not meant to be washed every wear or even every two wears, so keep that in mind! I’m gonna put my measurements at the bottom of this so hopefully it will help some pick the correct size. Anyways, get these, you’ll love them! I find them very flattering on everyone I see wearing them:) • My measurements in inches: Waist-25 Hip-31.5 Widest point of hips(butt)-35 Thigh at widest-20.5

I line dry most of my clothes so shrinkage is unlikely, but if they do happen to shrink, I still have some space all around to account for that without being too baggy. *If you dry these, they will shrink.* 501’s are not pre shrunk because they are designed to be “broke in” by sitting in a tub of water with the jeans on, and wearing them until they dry. They are not meant to be washed every wear or even every two wears, so keep that in mind! I’m gonna put my measurements at the bottom of this so hopefully it will help some pick the correct size. Anyways, get these, you’ll love them! I find them very flattering on everyone I see wearing them:) • My measurements in inches: Waist-25 Hip-31.5 Widest point of hips(butt)-35 Thigh at widest-20.5

I was looking for fleece-lined leggings for when I move to the mountains this winter, and I’ll be spending a ton of time outdoors through my work, but also working in a very casual-dress office environment as well. I’ve lived in the area before, and I needed to stock up on pieces I can layer. I seriously just ordered three more pairs of these leggings, because they’re on sale, and the cabin I’ll be living in has access to a washer/dryer, but our buildings operate on solar power, and to conserve our energy I’ll likely drive to the laundromat every few weeks, and these are the most comfortable, attractive, versatile leggings I own. I can layer them with my other thermal tights/leggings for extra warmth if needed in the office, or layer them under ski-pants or thicker hiking pants outside. Also, I just wore them to lunch with a friend today, they look fantastic, so I just wore them with a long-sleeved more dressy top and they were great on their own. I am 5’4” and 128-130 lbs, and curvy hourglass shape, more curvy on the top though. My regular bottom half measurements are included in the photos I’ve attached. The mediums fit very well, even though most of my leggings are smalls, I wanted something looser and able to layer over another pair of fleece leggings for cold days - and these leggings are soft and so stretchy that I can comfortably wear them alone or layer below with another thin thermal layer. I love that they’re lined leggings, but especially since I sized up a little, they aren’t inappropriately clingy. The waistband is soft, stretchy, and not constricting at all. The waistband on the medium is a little loose on me, but not in a way that they’re baggy or would fall down, or that anyone would notice. Im curled up on the couch writing this right now, and regardless of how I sit or lay down, they’re comfortable and flexible. The leggings are opaque and not sheer at all. They’ll provide a great “blue jean” looking cover that I can wear my super stretchy fleece lined leggings under if needed. I just bought another pair of the blue, and two pairs of the black. Thank you to the poster that mentioned piling. Because of it, I washed these the same way I wash my more traditional looking fleece lined leggings - in mesh laundry bags on a cold delicate cycle and then hang to dry. I’ll update my review if there’s any issue with piling with this laundry routine, but I haven’t had any yet.

Very comfy... Ok... first, I gotta say: these pants are super cute and comfy. I have always had a harder time shopping for pants, because of my extremely curvy figure. Typically, in stretch pants, I wear a size 2x to 3x depending on the brand. In jeans I wear a size 24. So, I purchased these in 4x. They are just right (provided they don't shrink in the dryer!). I cannot comment on durability, as I only got them today and only tried them on to see if they fit and take pics for your review. If anything changes I'll update. However, right now I do recommend these and now I think I want to get a couple other colors, as well. Update: they did not shrink in the dryer on standard low heat. The only thing that I don't like is the elastic bad twists, however they still seem to lay flat once I get them pulled up. So far I still like them and will probably order other colors in the near future.

These are sure to become my favorite pants! They are so comfy and cool looking! People have thought I was wearing a skirt rather than pants. I am 5ft 4in and was worried that they would be dragging on the floor, as is often the case. These fall perfectly to the tops of my feet. Absolutely a perfect length. Other reviewers have mentioned that the elastic waistline does not have a lot of stretch and I would agree with that. They do stretch, but not drastically. They are perfect for me with an around 35in waistline. For reference, I generally wear a 14-16, or XL, and am around 175lbs with a curvy shape, but not a lot of definition in the waistline. I want these in all available colors. Love them!

Decent Jeans For reference: I am 5'8", 190ish lbs, pear shape with a meno-belly, 31" inseam, 37" waist, 45" hips. I typically wear 14/16 depending on the style and brand. I did look at their size chart and although my measurements did not match up I decided to try size 14 Tall. After washing on cold and drying on low heat I can say they fit. The inseam on the pair I received is 30", which works fine for me. These are snug around the waist but loosen up as you wear them. A 16 would have been too big/baggy, so with this jean I believe I am between the two sizes. There is a good weight to these but not so heavy they cannot be worn. Will be good Fall and Winter jeans, but too heavy for Summer. These are high waisted and indeed wide legged as described. These may be tummy controlled for smaller tummies, but without additional help from shapewear undergarments these do not help the meno-belly. Not a complaint, just an fyi. Overall I find these to be decent quality jeans for a reasonable price.

Not a style for me but very nice quality The denim is really nice weight in these high waist jeans. No visible panty lines, they’re not so heavy that they are hot to wear and have just enough stretch for them to keep their shape. The seams and hems are uniformly stitched and sturdy also. The legs are very wide, as shown, but I don’t feel that they have a baggy fit elsewhere. Unfortunately, on my body shape and height I don’t find them to be at all flattering I don’t know if it is the back pocket placement, the leg length or the snug fit of the jeans from the waist to the hips. (I’m 5’4” tall and wearing a size 10.) Even with a 2”heel I felt I looked short and squat. Not a look I can successfully carry off. On a more slender, taller frame they would be very cute.

Definitely size up! Pay CLOSE attention to the size chart! I think the size chart might be pretty accurate. I am 5'4" and about 200 pounds. I usually wear around a size 14 but I got the 16 just in case because of the measurements. These are still too small and much tighter than I like. The only reason they even still fit is because of how stretchy they are. So don't expect the sizes to match what you are used to. Check the size chart. If these were the right size for me I think I would like them ok. The stretchy material should be very comfortable. The length is good for me. The zippers is not smooth and takes some effort. It gets caught up a lot. The pockets are not very deep and the fabric feels like it won't last very long. The size on the pants says 32 everywhere. Nowhere on the pants does it have the size 16. Only on the plastic wrapping does it say size 16. So it isn't surprising that this doesn't match closer to actual US sizes. Overall it is an ok pair of pants. Just ok though.

Very nice pair of jeans I'm 165 lbs and 5'7, and I carry my weight in my mid-section. I bought these just to see; had zero expectations because the reviews were mixed. Very nice belt I was impressed with this belt based on the price I paid. It is entitled a “leather belt” but down further in the description it states “faux leather” so truly not leather but I wasn’t expecting it to be for $15.00. Still it’s false advertising, hence my 4 stars. Besides that the rest is all positive for the belt I paid for. I’m a 37” waist & went with a large & it wasn’t too large for me. There are 6 holes to the belt & I use hole #5 to buckle it. The material is nice & smooth. There isn’t a flaw to it. It’s 1” wide & the buckle is 1-1/2” wide. The buckle is a nice silver which is shiny but not too shiny. It does come with instructions-per my pictures- as well as a little gadget to use in case the belt is too long. The gadget allows you to unscrew the buckle & cut the belt to shorten it. I’d say the sizing they provide is accurate though. I could’ve gone with a medium. Overall a great belt to wear with jeans or dress pants. A nice color black. I’m happy with this belt, it fits comfortably & I feel it was well worth what I paid. I definitely recommend it.

Unsure The positives: The stretch is there! Makes my butt look nice and isn’t uncomfortable to wear like some jeans are. They fit perfect in the waist ( I’m 5’9 170 pounds) and I got a size medium. The material is very nice. Not itchy. The negatives: The color is not even close to the photo shown. They are more grey blue than the blue I was expecting. The bottom of the pant legs are baggier than I thought but again the photo is of the bottoms are rolled. When I attempt to roll them they just aren’t looking at that cute. All in all I am thinking about returning they just aren’t what I am looking for color wise. Which I hate saying because these are super comfortable!

So comfortable and flattering! These are incredibly stretchy and comfortable. I’m 140lbs and 5’6 and ordered a medium (what I’m wearing in pics). However, I just ordered a Small to replace them because they stretch out the more you walk around in them. Love the super high waist and flattering look. I’ve always been concerned about my midsection and these hide it well!

Super cozy & cute jeans! These jeans are very comfortable. They are stretchy so I could’ve sized down, but my regular size works perfect also. I love the length on these (I’m 5’3”) and how high rise they are. The back pockets are adorable. Perfect fall staple!!

Super cozy & cute jeans! These jeans are very comfortable. They are stretchy so I could’ve sized down, but my regular size works perfect also. I love the length on these (I’m 5’3”) and how high rise they are. The back pockets are adorable. Perfect fall staple!!

Super cozy & cute jeans! These jeans are very comfortable. They are stretchy so I could’ve sized down, but my regular size works perfect also. I love the length on these (I’m 5’3”) and how high rise they are. The back pockets are adorable. Perfect fall staple!!

Nice length crop, light summer weight denim These are a nice light pair of summer weight denim pants. I think they're meant to be crops, but I am 5'3"and these came down to my ankles (which I prefer anyway). The material is generally quite soft but some of the seams are very rough, especially around the waist area. It's like they chose to burn the thread ends rather than tie them off. I would make sure to always tuck in my shirt with these or wear substantial undergarments that come up to the waist (at least) to protect your skin. The thread roughness aside, I like the stretch of these pants. I wish they were a bit more structured in the midsection. Since they are so very high waisted, one would hope they would provide some tummy control, but these just move and stretch with you, which makes them incredibly comfortable. The green is very light and makes for easy pairing with many colors of tops and shoes. I normally wear an 8 or 29 and these pants in a large fit me well.

New favorite pants! These are the best pants! I love the fabric, the fit, and I'll be ordering more asap. I got the size M in the color white and they fit me well. I am typically size 4-6, 29" waist, 41" hips, 25" inseam, 5'0" tall. These fit me perfectly and have plenty of stretch so I could have possibly sized down to the S. The white jeans are not transparent or see through. They wash and dry well. I cannot say enough good things about these pants, especially considering how hard jeans are to find when you are petite. Can't wait to get the other colors! Worth every penny!

Soft and Versatile I bought a medium, based on reviews. I weigh about 120 and am about 5' 5" - or a little under. The jeggings are a little loose, but a small would have been too small. If need be, I will sew in a tuck or two. Generally speaking - these fit as expected. The rise is 10". I will trim the bottoms to fit my exact height, and won't bother hemming them. They have enough polyester in them I don't think there will unravel. / My pair: the waist-band twists in the wash, so I had to sew a few stitches to get it stay in place. Definitely a down-side.

Comfortable I forgot that I was wearing denim. This felt more like tights or yoga pants for me. With it being black, you can't really tell that its denim either. It's stretchy material and hugs your body just right. The length is just right for 5'6" height.

Sparkly Belt This belt is super cute. It's very sparkly and stylish. The rhinestones on the buckle are a great touch! The belt is the perfect width to wear with jeans. It is thick and of great quality. My only complaint is with the sizing. I am a waist 28 so I ordered a size small which is supposed to fit waist 26-34. The medium is 32-40. This belt barely fits; I can only get it on the first hole and it feels tight. There is no way that this would fit someone with a 34 waist. The size chart is way off. I wish I had ordered a medium, but I didn't know that the chart was incorrect. I would recommend sizing up from whatever size you are on the size chart. I wish I had known that before ordering it. Overall, I do like this belt, but I will have limited use of it because of the tight fit.

Gorgeous band, but buckle's not subtle This silver "sequin" belt has a gorgeous band. It's a nice thick quality leather covered in multi-shades of silver glitter (that doesn't shed), outlined in silver beading ( not sequins). I adore the belt band, just wish the buckle wasn't 3 " wide and covered in VERY large rhinestones that seem a lil cheesy for an older woman like me. I just wear my shirt untucked in the front to camoflage the buckle, and got LOTS of compliments from girlfriends wearing it as slightly more demure bling. I have a 30" waist and size 26-34 fits me perfectly (with 2 belt-holes to spare if I gainore flab), and the belt "tail" lies nice and flat against me. I do luv this fun belt!
Perfect fit For reference I am 5ft and 95lbs I bought the black in the XS size tho I suspect a S also would’ve fit. Cute finish and great vintage silhouette!!

Great bell bottom jeans for short person These jeans are amazing. I have bought 3 pairs. When skinny jeans came around they were not making boot cut flair or bell bottoms anymore. Finally the style is coming back around. I have been having trouble finding bell bottoms for someone is short as I. All others drag on the floor. I'm 5ft even and weigh 130 LBS. I order a medium. They are a little big on me so I do need a belt. But I'm worried if I get a small I will not be able to get them over my bum. I have junk in the trunk.I have only found one other brand of bell bottom jeans that are made for short people. The material is great for me. I do not have a gap in the back either. Highly recommend.

High hopes but disappointed These are really comfy and length is good and I love that they’re low rise because that’s hard to find with the flares but they’re very baggy. I hate jeans that are loose around my knees and that’s what these are like. I’m 5’3 and 151 pounds, i got a large but feel like I could have worn a medium. But I feel like even with a medium, they’d still be baggy.

GREAT FIT!!! Loved the fit! Was worried they would be baggy but no. They fit perfectly as I am usually size 3-5. And 5'4" weighing aprox 125. I ordered a small and am excited to wear them for Thanksgiving:) To match my 3 year old princess! I really wanted the darker ones but like the lighter ones too. Overall am pretty impressed:)

good quality I’m always a 6 in stretchy jeans and I got a 6 in these; being super high waisted they’re a little snug around the waist (I’m apple shaped). A size higher would be too loose on my skinny butt and legs though, and they’re not uncomfortable or anything. They do have stretch. Heavy denim. No warning label about washing them before wearing due to dyes used. I got them for $10 what a bargain! I like the cut of the legs, they’re cute, it’s a nice happy medium bw skinny and super loose. The black is like a grey black. 5’9, 165 carry weight up top.

Exceeded my expectations I bought size 4. I'm 5'6", 137lb, thicker in the thighs and smaller in the waist. These jeans were much better than I expected and the color in the picture looks darker than in person. The material is the perfect thickness and offers mild smoothing of bumps and lumps and some stretch for comfort. These were more flattering on my butt than any looser for jean I've tried.

Size down but shockingly good!! I am really impressed with these jeans! I am truly shocked at how much I like them! I did cut the bottom as they were slightly longer than I like, but now they are perfect! I already purchased the other two colors! Some thoughts below: - Runs large. I usually wear a size 6 or 28 in jeans and I went with a 4 in these and they are PERFECT! I am 5'7" and 135lbs for reference. - The length is a bit longer than I like so I cut about 1.5" off the bottom. The photo in heels is full length and the other two photos are cut. I might cut a bit more off but have not decided yet. Even with the shorter length, they still look cute with heels or flats! - The material is somewhat soft but still holds its shape. It has a bit of stretch to it, but not enough that its going to stretch out after one wear. - Nice and high waisted which is what I think makes the wider leg work. - These are tight through the thigh but wide through the calf, they're perfect! Overall, I would size down one size, especially if you are between sizes. Expect the length to be around 29" inseam but cutting them is super easy if you want them shorter and they still look cute! Definitely recommend.

These are perfect for petite ppl with some curves! I’m only 5’1 petite and 126 pounds. I have a little bit bigger of a bottom though and it’s hard to find jeans that are not too long but also fit my waist and curve’s comfortably. I have bought $200 jeans and many other high end ones that never really meet my expectations. So I wasn’t too hopeful on these either but to my surprise they’re my favorite jeans now! The material is so soft, they have the perfect amount of stretch in them they hug my curves nicely, the length is perfect, and the button is sewn on super strong! The color is exactly as it appears online and the size chart was super accurate. The threading is nice too. I did wash them on normal cycle, cool water, and dried the n medium heat, and I had no issues with shrinking, fading, or loose threads after. I want more of them in other colors, these are a shorter girl dream jean! The stretch is great because it would also work for those with more curves than me also! Definitely recommend!

Everything! Wow! I kept hearing all the hype and initially ordered these in the lighter color(Crakleton). I am a curvy 5’ 7”, 190 pounds, size 14. I felt they were a little to snug for comfort. I was offered an exchange to my delight, and got the darker Blue Laguna-Waterless color. Great customer service. As we all know different washes/colors make a difference in the fit of denim.These are living up to the hype and are all they claim to be! Perfect length, perfect fit! If you are on the fence, just try them. All for under $30. Who could ask for more?

Very cute shorts with a LOT of stretch!! Cute shorts and they cover up nicely which I really like!! They are VERY stretchy though! Almost too much cause I would have preferred a little more...um...tightness around the belly, but I should have gone down a size I guess?!? Other than that though, I really like these! VERY comfortable on!! For reference, I'm apx. 5'7" & 145lbs with an apx. 30" waist and 40" hips and this is the large. According to their size chart, this is what I should have ordered, but with the stretch, I wished I'd gone with the Med.! Hope this will help you chose more wisely at least! But, I like em and will still wear em cause they are super comfy on!! 3 buttons at the waist and a small zipper. Seem well made and there is material under the 'frayed' part so you don't have to worry about anything showing that you may not want to show! LOL They seem reasonably priced and I would recommend! Just come cute shorts!! Hope this helps! Thanks much!

Chubby girl friendly! I hardly ever write product reviews, but as a chubby girl sometimes clothing items are impossible to buy based on size charts and reviews from people who do not have the same body type. I’m 5’3” and about 220 with a mom belly from having 2 kids back to back. They hit me at the perfect length and could be easily adjusted based on shoe type. These overalls are such a nice material of denim and have a crazy amount of stretch. The distressed look is super cute and the pockets actually function, since sometimes they don’t always have real pockets. All the buttons seem really well attached and stitching is great. They are incredibly comfortable and will work perfect for all seasons!

Love these overalls!! I am 5.0 ft and about 132 pounds. I’m short and curvy in the bottom. (Thicker). It’s very hard to find pants that fit me length wise and also stretchy in the waist, butt and thigh area. I took a chance and bought a small in these overalls. Let me tell you….. they fit me in all the right places. Not too long. I rolled them up a bit to give me a 3/4 look. They aren’t too tight and fit comfortable on my waist, butt and thighs. I will most likely order more pairs. I love them so much. I bought for a scarecrow costume. They do have a slight smell, but nothing too overwhelming.

Burnt Orange color 5'3 117 pounds Size XS I love the way this fits. I am petite 5'3 117 pounds long torso short legs. Its hard for me to find and jeans or shorts to fit me. Its always too long or too wide.. These were PERFECT! They are great for fall. 😍

I’m honestly shocked I am beyond thrilled with how these overalls fit. I am normally a size 12 in pants and I bought these in an XL. They fit perfectly. Not too tight, not too loose. I feel like they flatter my belly and don’t give me a diaper butt. Good coverage all around. I’m 5’7” and with one roll, these hit right at my ankle. The fabric is stretchy and soft. I’m shocked they fit and actually make me feel good about my body! I want more colors now!
Fits perfectly! Love these overalls! I got a size large. I’m 5’6, 163lbs. The material is so soft. I wore them casually but could easily dress up.

THANK YOU TO ALL THE PEOPLE WHO LEFT REVIEWS i read a few comments about these overalls and i was still skeptic about them like one other customer stated she had been looking for overalls for 15years ive also been looking for about the same years if not more ive always had trouble finding clothes n my size womens pants never worked for me im 5ft 7in currently weighing in at 181lbs so i looked read looked read and looked and on this product i ordered xl hoping they werent too big considering im not an xl my usual pants size are either too big in the waist and just right in the length or vise versa but these are WOW i slipped these on and didnt want to take them off they’re so comfortable and roomy they stretch im glad i got the xl cause a large wouldve been too short in the length now if you’re my size more or less i suggest you get xl for comfort and length the crotch area is a bit low but i just might buy more n other colors truly one of the BEST things ive bought from amazon and ive had a lot of disappointments from things i’ve purchased but not these i urge anyone who wants these to try them out you wont be disappointed unless you get the wrong size again thank you

Cute but poor quality control In general these are cute & fit me well. I ordered “E Deep Blue” in both Med & Large. I’m 5’2” & 164lbs, typically size 8-10 & the Med fit me best. I didn’t notice much difference in the Large. The hip area was a bit more roomy, and I noticed the extra fabric in the crotch area in the Large, but that wasn’t an issue with the Medium. As you can see from the pics, the colors are very inconsistent! I ordered the SAME color/style in 2 sizes but they looked quite different. (Large is darker on left.) Second pic is of labels. I think they are the correct items since the only difference is the last 2 digits under the bar code, which I’m assuming may represent size? I still recommend giving these a try, but order more than one like I did & return what doesn’t work.

Love these overalls but… (Before I start, I’m 5ft 4, 138lbs, Size Medium, Size 9, 29 waist of this helps at all?) The first 2 photos is what the problem is for most people — the extra material at the crotch region. I somewhat don’t mind this because if I’m on the time of the month, these are great for the wiggle room if I’m bloated. But it can look unflattering! The 3rd photo is length. They’re perfect, but I roll may cuffs by habit anyways, so, that’s where the 4th photo kicks in. I like the cuff look better on me. The last photo is another angle focusing on the material in that region. I am planning to buy another in a small (same color) and perhaps that will be better so it won’t have the stretchy pouch there. I’ve read some reviews where alot of women are saying these aren’t stretchy? Perhaps it differs for color/style? Because these are STRETCHY. Best way to describe these overalls: The material for Jeggings. They look like Jean material but stretchy asf. Are these worth $40+? No. I wish they was alittle cheaper. Luckily for me, a flash sale popped up and I got them less than $40.

The smell is awful They have an awful smell (I think from the fabric dye) I've washed them 10 times with different products and hung them outside in the sun to try and get rid of the smell but they still smell like fireworks. The fit is okay. The front section around the waist is a bit weird as if it's been stretched out and makes that midsection look bigger than it is. I ordered a small I'm 5'2" 133 lbs.

Runs large, nice stretch to material Well I ordered another brand and also these and omg these were so much more comfy! They run large so I bought an XS I’m 5’3” and 128 lbs and they fit shapely, but they stretch so they are so comfy when they fit shapely. I’ll have to cut the length no big deal. Loose and comfortable I really Like these overalls. They are really soft but sturdy denim. The legs are roomy and not tight at the bottom, more of a flair than a straight leg. I’m short and kinda square 5’3” 155 pounds and a large fit me just right except I have to pull the straps as tight up as they would go, which I didn’t love. I like the dark blue color. As comfy as leggings, but thicker & dressier (and no cameltoe) As w/ seemingly many others, I bought my first pairs @ CVS (got 3). I don’t normally shop for clothes there, but they were a serendipitous discovery, and having a hard-to-fit body where I don’t often find items that I can wear off-the-rack (I’m an endomorph, or “apple” type, where my waist is short & thick), I wanted to look for more pairs, solids specifically (black, heather grey & indigo w/ gold threading), to have as wardrobe basics. I was THRILLED to find them on Amazon (and they do have their own website, as well), and bought the pairs that I was looking for, after reading reviews from customers who ended up becoming Lildy fans in the same manner. These jeggings are SO comfy that you can literally wear them 24/7, and even for activities that require a broad range of movement (splits, high kicks). I wear them to sleep, because I’m at that stage in my life where I’m too lazy to change in & out of clothes constantly (just turned 47). I have a “uniform” that works for me (wifebeater & jeggings), and it’s nice to be able to simply add accessories, outerwear & shoes, and voila….I’m dressed to go outside. 👌 Prior to finding the Lildy jeggings @ CVS, I was wearing leggings, but would wear a mini over them solely for the coverage to hide the cameltoe. Now, I don’t have to do that because the material for the jeggings is thicker. I save myself from needing to put on an extra garment that constantly shifts from movement, and requires additional labor to adjust & launder. Dressing is much easier, and I effortlessly look pulled together. I always wore bootcut or flared pants (they were more flattering for my silhouette), but these jeggings fit so well that I feel confident about how I look in them (can’t say the same for the ones from Walmart), and unlike w/ flared or bootcut bottoms, these tuck into knee-hi boots w/o any of that extra fabric, and make looking cute such a non-job. Highly recommended, if you feel that the cut will work for your body shape. Great value for the price—you’ll get hooked & end up coming back for more colors & patterns. (For reference, I’m just under 5’2”, ~110 lbs., 28” waist & 28” inseam.) UPDATE: (May 2022) I’m now on my SEVENTH pair of Lildy jeggings! 👖 Just got the olive green solid color. Made just like the rest. I LIVE in these—you can exercise & sleep in these jeggings, and it’s nice that they look like “real pants.” 😆 The grey ones look fairly refined, almost like twill pants, and you just pull’em up like they’re you’re favorite pair of leggings, but might be fine for a “casual nice” work dress code. 🥰

Figure Flattering but Cut is too Long for My Frame The - Sidefeel Women's Jeans 2024 High Waist Bootcut Jeans Fashion Stretchy Jeans for Women – are very figure flattering for my fuller figure frame however, the cut of the pants through the legs and torso is too long for my short in height stature. Bigger than bootcut, BIG legs on these jeans. I'm 5'4", 147 lbs, 29/39 and chose a size 12 in these jeans. Overall the fit is great, these are very comfortable to wear, and I like the Bluing color of these jeans, but I feel that the leg type is misrepresented. The legs of these jeans are more of a stovepipe design, not nearly as fitted and shaped as the images suggest, and I don't like how huge and stumpy they make my already thick legs look. I didn't expect a close fitting pair of jeans, but neither was I expecting something so loose and baggy on me. These remind me somewhat of a certain brand of oversized leg jeans from the 90s, and I only wore those when I was a size 4-5 to avoid looking like a wall of denim. These make me look stumpy and stubby, and as much as I like how they move and feel I just don't like the look of them on. The legs of these jeans look short in the images, but mine do line up as described in the size chart, just shy of 30". These are about an inch too long for me, but I'm used to that. The material is quite stretchy, and very comfortable to move around in. I can do squats, take long strides, and stretch my legs out as much as I can in these jeans without discomfort. The waist is very comfortable and flattens my problem abdomen without digging in, and these jeans look great under close fitting tops. The backseam doesn't ride up my generously sized posterior, and the seat of these is somewhat flattering on my large backside. The front pockets are wide and deep enough for an earbuds case, but won't fit any of my phones. There are no side pockets, just front and back ones. I did have an issue with the stitching, however. There are many areas where the machine spat out knots of thread into rats nests, there's spots where the stitching was attempted to be backstitched but veered off, gaps in the stitch lines, and loose sections of threads that hang in a gap while being stitched down on either side. The four attached images are a sampling of the problems that I found in the stitching. I am not fully satisfied with the construction quality of these jeans. Overall I like the jeans enough to wear them camping or to work outdoors in cooler weather, but I'm not happy with appearance of them on and how rough the stitching is on these pants. Trendy jeans for all the seasons Unheard of, jeans that fit the first try! Most of my jeans are size 6, I figured M 8/10 would be a safe bet knowing these jeans are 71% cotton, another plus. I had my doubts when I saw the tube shaped package they arrived in. I tried them on pre and post laundering. Washed in cold and hung to dry, as per directions. Nice white stretchy pants I’m not sure about these pants. I like the bright whiteness of them and I like that there’s pockets. My issue with them is that they’re not as thick as I would of hoped to have had them, now they are not see-through however, because they’re not that thick they don’t do everything you want, leggings or yoga pants to do. Hide “things”! It’s hard to tell in these pictures with great lighting(I am using a ring light), but they’re not hiding everything you would want to hide. Ss a matter of fact, in some bad lighting, or maybe even a little bit of shadowing, it kind of makes your legs look even worse than what they probably are. Delivery takes a long time Starting with the dislikes, this skirt took over a month to arrive. The original order got lost in the mail so they sent me a new one. The skirt was packaged inside a vacuum sealed plastic bag. Upon opening it, there was a slight odor to the skirt material itself. The color of the fabric was vibrant. I ordered a small. I am 5’6” and 130lbs. I was able to walk my normal strides and pace comfortably. The skirt did not place any restrictions to my legs walking. The buttons are all functional, you can undo as many as you want or none at all. Overall, I wanted a nostalgic fashion piece, and this Jean skirt was it. My teen daughters both really liked it on me and want to borrow it themselves. Despite the delivery time issues, it was an okay purchase over all. But I wouldn’t buy from this company again.

Length shrinks A LOT I am not a tall person by any stretch of the imagination -- I'm 5'3" on a good day. I ALWAYS have to order short/petite pants or hem up regular pants by several inches. Having seen the reviews on these saying that they shrink, I went ahead and bought the regular length ones. Goodness, am I happy I erred on the side of caution. They started with a 29.5" inseam (see photo). After two or three wash and dries, they've shrunk 2" up to a 27.5" inseam. They're nearly too short for even me, but they'll work. That aside, they fabric is thick, soft, and high quality. It's perfect for work, lays flat, doesn't wrinkle, and it gives a nice silhouette for curvy hips/bum. They're also pull-ons for extra comfort, and they have front pockets. I really do like them and I'll be getting several more pairs for work, but I feel bad for anyone taller than me.

Nice, but not for my body type These pants are the “pull-on formal” type with no fly/ button, a thick elastic waist and detailing like overstitched pockets and belt hooks to make them work-appropriate. The fabric is of pretty decent thickness but if I kept them I’d probably need shapewear to cover the vpl and lumps and bumps. I ordered them in long, as the current fashion for flood pants doesn’t suit me, and they would be just about right with a modest heel (I’m 5’6” and somewhat short waisted). I have a small waist and big booty, but I was hoping they would work for me due to the stretch and elastic waist but they gaped really badly at the back. If I went down a size they would probably fit better at the waist but I wouldn’t want them to be any tighter on the hips for work pants. I returned them because of this but think the quality and style are good.

Very comfortable! Excellent quality Not sure why these pants got low reviews. But these Lee pants are legit comfortable. My measurements: waist 36 inch, hip 44 inch. Weight: 170, height: 5’6” I opted for the size 14 regular. I’m Somewhat curvier in the back. I did order 16, but it was too big, especially around the waist. The 14 is not too tight, or too loose. Inseam is perfect for my height. These are pull on with no zipper to deal with. Two side pockets that are deep. Back pockets are fake (cannot access). Material is a lil on the heavier/thicker side but soft and quality. Good stretch throughout. Try it out! I ended ordering more colors after this one.

Ok, but not perfect These were essentially what I was looking for, a lightweight, comfy, denim pull on but not quite a home run. FABRIC and STRETCH The fabric is NOT stretchy, only the waistband has stretch. Of course there will be color variation, but the pics show a more weathered, distressed look than the mine. SIZING and FIT: I tried both XL and 2X and there's not a whole lot of difference between the two as far as I can tell, but there's more room in the hip and thigh in the 2X. The fit isn't super flattering, especially from the back, but that could be because I have a relatively big, round, bum so it isn't as gathered back there as it is in the model pics. I think I'd probably need a 3X (which they don't have) to get that cute extra baggy look. OVERALL: For reference, I usually wear about a size 16 in pants. I'm plus sized (50-44-48) with a large bust and bum but no hips. I carry most of my weight in my belly. So while these will fit a plus sized body, they fit more like a regular relaxed jean than a baggy/slouchy jean. If I keep them, I will probably go with the smaller size (XL) because they're slightly more flattering as a relaxed jean, but if I could, I'd prefer to size up to get the slouchy, oversized look like the model.

Legs much baggier than most bout cut jeans I’m 5’5” 160lbs, but these jeans and size 10. It’s perfect throughout the waist, but, hips… Slightly baggy in the thighs and super big below the knee. Maybe these jeans fit better on thinner girls. I felt like an elephant wearing them. So if you’re my size… You might want to pass on these🤦🏼‍♀️🙃

Sizing on chart is correct For accuracy sake, I posted the sizes of the actual belts, along with a measuring tape for the small and the medium. These belts are lovely and with the Amazon prime prices, they would be a great purchase. So, depending on how high or low you wear your pants and your belt needs, I hope this post helps. I also ordered a large because of the reviews, and I immediately returned it because it was way way too long. It was again, accurate with what the guide posted on the website. I kept the medium size, which fit both my 31 inch waist pants and my 34 inch midrise pants.

WAAAAAY TO SMALL!!!!! I purchased a Medium because the last belt I bought on Amazon was a medium and it fit perfectly . My waist is a 36 , and this belt is waaay to small . I measured the belt with a tape measure . It measured 38 inches from buckle to end but the measurement from the buckle to the first hole is 30 and to the last hole is 34, therefore this belt should be for no larger than a 34 waist . Too stiff and tiny This is a tiny little belt. It’s real leather with a brass colored metal buckle. I didn’t particularly care for it myself. It’s too narrow for my taste and too thick. Port is the new neutral This dark port color is a lot more versatile than you might think. I love this for a neutral alternative to black, brown or grey. It adds a pop of color without competing with the rest of your outfit. The design is a straightforward classic with a plain finish to the good quality leather. It's relatively sturdy but pliable and softens with wear. I anticipate it will last a good long while. I like the bonded finish on the edges too. the squarish buckle is like a tarnished brass for a lived in look. The tail end is squared with subtly rounded corners. This covers up the embossed Timberland logo when wearing. The medium size I ordered fits me well. I have a 31" waist and 41" hips and I can wear this on the second hole with midrise jeans, giving the belt a measurement of 34". There are five holes in this belt at one inch intervals measuring a span of 31" to 35" from tang to hole. The width is neither wide, nor skinny — 7/8" or 22mm, very workable and will fit through most belt carriers. I love it. Excellent quality with great workmanship. I wear this often!

These are capris... and I'm short! ✅Likes: ○ The color/wash is not too bad. Although I hate the fit of these (more to follow) but the wash is better than some other barrel cheap jeans I've gotten. If you're looking for the "barrel jeans via CAPRI" these may be up your alley, more gen-x/millenial does trendy than the ACTUAL barrel jean trend which bows out more and is BAGGY+full length. And no, I'm not tall. In fact, I'm barely average, a mere ~5'2" with short legs so... My previous go-to barrel jeans are a flat 26.5" in all sizes and that's MUCH better IMO... I ordered a 6 but tbh if I knew these were supposed to fit capri length I would try a maximum size 14 just so they sat lower (my reg size is M 26" waist, 39" hips) ● Not just short/capri style but they are AT LEAST AN INCH SHORTER THAN THE CHART so all I can say is size up IMO! 0.5-0.7" variation was incorrect in my case, tba if it's an accident but I think you should expect 1"+ so size up. ● Cheap construction, SUPER cheap zipper, 4 odd thick felt patches inside, super tight button hole, thick (but hollow) button, uncomfortable plastic thread ends everywhere, slippery synthetic pockets, etc ● Says "non stretch" yet there is spandex so yeah, they're somewhat stretchy and elastic will wear down

Inconsistent Size Chart I’m 5’7”, 129 lbs, 29” waist, 38” at the widest part of my booty. Size charts always seem to be so misleading. I can never rely on them. I read reviews trying to figure out what size to order. Several reviews said the size chart was pretty accurate, which meant I would have needed to order a large. I’m usually a small, but in considering the reviews I ordered a large and a medium and figured I’d return whichever didn’t fit. I ended up ordering a small after they arrive because the medium was too big. In the photos, I’m wearing a small, and you can see the pants could easily fit someone 2-3 inches wider than I am. They stretch big time. I’ll probably keep the small but not sure because the length is right at where I would want it to be and after washing/drying, it seems like this fabric would shrink quite a bit and then be too short. Either way, I would order a size down or at the very least your regular size when ordering these.

Awesome pair of jeggings! Runs a bit large. Based on other buyer reviews, I decided to purchase these in a size large. I'm 5'6 and 170 lbs. Typically I wear jeans that have a low rise because they're a better fit for my body type (extra weight in the tummy area.) High and mid-rise pants tend to cut into my belly fat and it creates these very unsightly lumps on my sides. Getting to the point, I wish I had gone with the medium instead of the large. They're form fitting but in a looser way. I like how they fit throughout the legs but I would prefer them a tad more fitting. Most jeggings (even the super stretchy ones) are too tight in the calves and ankles for me to wear comfortably. These are nice, smooth denim and very, very stretchy. They're perfect for someone like me who usually has trouble fitting into jeggings. There is a super comfy waistband that is equally stretchy (FYI: no belt loops or front pockets on these pants.) It's a higher waist but they're really easy to roll down, which is what I prefer. Even with them rolled down the waistband is still very big on me. I haven't washed them yet so I really hope they shrink some. If I were judging these on comfort level alone they would absolutely get 5 stars! They are literally just as comfortable as cotton leggings. The denim is very soft, not thick and rough like traditional jeans. I'm definitely ordering another pair in a smaller size! Highly recommend!!

Tummy control - no. Material - good. I’m 5’5” 170lbs I bought a 12. I would say these fit just right however I like my pants with a little more room I could have sized up. soft material, nice wash color, that's it nice material but not very flattering on my body type. it squishes my butt (which is 45" so the opposite of flat lol) and it's far too short for me (5'10) despite getting the long length. it also runs small; i sized up and it fit pretty snug. it doesn't look like the wide, bell bottoms you see in the pics. i like the wash and the material, which is soft and seems breathable, but it falls flat otherwise. the pockets are also small.

Ideal for tall women One of the appeals to this style is the wide legs. For women like me, with rather thick thighs, these pants are liberating and offer much comfort. These did not disappoint in that aspect. But overall, the length was a huge drawback (reduce a star). They were like 5 inches too long. I attempted to roll the waist band to reduce the length but they were still too long and doing so caused the pants to bunch in the rear end. I attempted to roll them from the bottom at no avail; the cuff would not stay. Nevertheless, the feel was super soft with rather shallow pockets for such a relaxed style (reduce a star).I did order my normal size 12. For reference, I am 5'6" at 175lbs with a waist of 35". I felt like the elastic would help to frame my waist, but I wish I had ordered a smaller size because I did not feel any curve was sightly in these pants.

I’m so impressed! I’m in the process of losing the 30lbs that I gained and I didn’t want I spend $100 or more at the mall to buy a pair of jeans that I will hopefully only wear for a few months. GREAT pants LOVE these pants. I ordered a 5w x 32L and I am 5'4'' and usually between a size 4-6. They had a little extra length if needed but fit fine with my boots. Sooo comfortable! BEST fitting jeans I own! Bootcut, and right length, and stretchy.. Can't go wrong! For reference I ordered a 7, and I'm 4"11, and 115 pounds. Perfect fit, but not to tight.. About an inch below my belly button, which I love!

Amazing I got my jeans, and let me tell you. I absolutely love them. They are very stretchy I wear a size 22 normally. I could probably have went down a size, as you can see in the pic how there is still extra material that bunches. This is the first time ever buying jeans online. I wanted to make sure they fit. I’m 5’9 and they drag to the floor still. I want to go buy more in different colors. Love the heart design on my knee. There wasn’t a heart pattern on the jeans so it was neat it made that. I wouldn’t recommend them to anybody who is shorter cause the bottoms would get ruined. This is actually the first time even making a long review on something I have purchased. I thought it was pricey not going to lie. HOWEVER I am very pleased on the Quality of material the weight of the jeans is not heavy love the pockets. The only wrinkles that you see is because I could’ve went down in size. I would recommend these jeans to anyone. Even though I did not show my face.

Love them!! These are super soft. I love the way they fit. I am 5'9" and weight 205. They are stretchy, which makes them very comfy at work all day. They are just long enought for me to wear flats with. Love the color and the distress. Quailty is very good. Highly recommend.

Cute Pants I rather enjoyed the look and feel of these pants, but I decided to return them because they did not fit the way I was hoping. I am 5'8" 155lbs - I am on the larger end of a medium / smaller end of a large. I was looking for a relaxed/boyfriend fit. And after reading the reviews, I decided on a medium, as some of the reviews said these pants ran on the larger end. I did not find that to be true. They had more of a regular-to-slim type fit - snug'ish on me. I am ordering a size large, and will update my review after I get the new ones. **Update** I received my size large pants. The fit is more to my liking. More roomy, but not frumpy (see pics).

Size chart true for US sizing. Super soft and cute! For comparison: I am 5’5” and I am usually 120 lbs but post holiday I’m rocking 125-130 lbs. I normally wear US size 4 or 27 in pants and size medium bikini bottoms. I have wide hips, some junk in the trunk, and shorter legs. Product: With the extra winter weight hanging on tight, mostly due to my love of chocolate and avoidance of the outdoors when the temperature is under 60, I decided to order medium Grapent jeans. I did not want the pants to be too tight and hinder my couch potato winter lifestyle. That was a mistake. The provided size chart recommended a size small but I have trust issues when it comes to size charts so I went up a size. Not only are the jeans plenty big in the waist, maybe for 8/10/12, but there is quite a bit of stretch in the material. Also, the fabric lost a bit of shape after a day of running errands. I fully regret not getting the recommended small because the material is very soft and stretchy so I think I would love them if they fit better. That’s my bad! The fabric is not a true denim but rather a medium weight cotton/poly blend. There are functional hip pockets as well as back pockets. I especially like the thigh rip because it is a different style than my other ripped jeans. As for length, I had to roll the cuffs to wear with sneakers. I’m normally a 29” inseam and these jeans were probably 30-31”. These waist just qualifies as “high” landing just past my belly button. The rear area was the only fitted part with the legs being looser. Recommendation: These mom jeans are stylish and super comfortable. I recommend going by the size chart in this case and rest easy knowing the fabric has plenty of stretch. The only negative thing I found was the tag instructs to hand wash while the product page advertises machine washable. I am going to machine wash but hang to dry and hope for the best. I really like these jeans and would recommend to a friend!

Love these! These are great jeans if you're a curvy girl like me. If you are hourglass or pear shaped... these jeans check all the boxes....relaxed fit in the hip and thighs and super stretchy. I'm 5'4 and the length is about perfect for me. The funny thing is they are advertised as having a rise of 9.5" (mid rise), however I knew that couldn't be true because the waistband came up above my belly button. So I measured the rise and it was 12"! I wasn't mad about it because I love high rise jeans. The only negative I could find about these jeans is they are quite a thin denim material and I question how long they will last, especially after seeing some of the other reviews. But for the low price I paid I'm sure I'll get my money's worth.

Love these jeans and I don't say that often! I bought these on a whim because the price was right. I love the gray color - it's dark enough to serve as a black without attracting all of the hair but actual black jeans would. For reference, I'm 145 lb 5'5, athletic build, and the size 10 fits the way I like. If I wanted a slightly more standard fit, I would go with my regular size 8, but the 10 gives me room to do anything I might want to do around the house or out. I actually ordered another pair in a more traditional jeans color.

It seemed like I was pulling them up in the back once in a while. Nice for knocking around the house or running to the store.

What a funny joke to call these xxs These are huge! The size they sent was indeed labeled xxs...but what the heck?! I fit the given measurements, but they must all be totally wrong. These are falling off, as you can see I have to hold them on my actual waist where they are supposed to sit. They are nice, but just comically large, I am short, only 4"11, but the rest of my measurements are the same size as the size chart. Also, you can see the white interior of the pockets through the pants, the fabric is not thin but still see through, so dark or white underwear or colored tucked in shirts are a no. They are more of a cream which is what I wanted and expected from the pictures. For the price, not worth it, I've gotten better and more accurately sized things on shein. Also even with heels, the pants are dragging the ground, so def not for short people. But idek, if I could have gone any smaller than an XXS, at this rate, it probably would have had to be an xxxxxs🙄 I wouldnt trust this sizing at all.

Order down one size from your usual jeans - they will loosen over time I wear size 6-8 in jeans. I first ordered these in size 8 and loved the fit and the faded whisker-look along the top. After walking around in them for a few minutes, I felt them sliding down a bit. We all know that jeans will continue to loosen throughout the day so I opted to exchange for size 6, which is what I typically wear in KUT from the Kloth corduroys. The fit was great though the whisker fade was much less prominent in the newly exchanged pair (dang it!) The photo I've included is of the size 8 jeans. I like the mid-rise fit. It feels secure and flattering around my mid section but still hip and stylish. I also like the ripped look but not so ripped that I feel like a teenager or homeless person. I have long legs (34 inseam) and these cropped in the perfect place on my calves. Love them!

If it’s any indication the zipper length was 3 inches. For reference, I am 5‘2“ and about 125 pounds. Athletic build. Size 4 fit snug through the thighs and bug gap at the waist.

Great fit. Poor quality. First, I loved the fit. They were perfect for my body. About 135 lbs, 5'7" and a size 6. Weren't too baggy. Just right!! Unfortunately, they tore in the butt after 5-6 wears. :( I tried patching it but it's a really awkward location. Just wanted to want others.

Get the large, even if you're thin I love these leggings. They are super soft and fleece lined (which is so comfortable). I am 5'2" and 117 lbs (29-30 inch waist) so I bought two pairs in large/xl based on my waist (shown in the photo attached) and I own a pair in small (which is what I originally purchased). Honestly, you would not see a difference in a photo of me wearing either the small or the large size. The large is a little longer in the legs and comes up above my bellybutton, but other than that I don't feel much of a difference. It definitely feels more comfortable pulling it up so it aligns with the top of my legs to look like jeans rather than tights (and I'm short!). I will likely purchase it again in the large size since there's not much of a difference to me in tightness. Although I could see this stretching to fit a heavier person, I wouldn't consider it xl sized. I usually wear small or medium in other stores, and I'd consider this more of a medium size.

Good for the Price I bought these in size S/M but I think I could've gone with a Large very easily. I'm normally a size 6/8 and small in women's clothes, medium in anything"trendy" (like forever 21, H&M, etc.). I'm 5'8”, measurements 34-24-38, and most pants are a little short on me, these were no exception. I was expecting to probably have to exchange these for the larger size, especially right after I took them out of the package. They look like clothes for a ten year old, but these leggings stretch an absurd amount. I would not suggest a S/M for someone who has any extra "love-lumps" going on because I feel like these will role down in that situation. They're high waisted enough to feel really comfortable and they're not the warmest leggings but it's not like I can feel a breeze when I'm wearing these (like some other leggings I have, looking at you Target). Would buy again. My biggest suggestion is that, if you are trim and slim, buy the S/M. If you are anywhere else on the spectrum, buy the Large.

Love these!! I freaking love these stretch pants. They truly look like jeans but wear like a second skin. In my opinion these are spring/fall type stretch pants. Too hot for summer but wont keep you warm in winter. They are kinda long (I'm 5'4" about 170lbs) and they bunch at my ankles and sit high in my waist. BUT- 1. They're perfect to wear with calf boots!! 2. If you want to wear flipfloos or sandals...they easily fold up to look like a cuff. And 3. So, now I'm quite happy with the pants and will wear them frequently.

Flattering Denim Pants, Excellent Fit. I was surprised these “jeggings” are actually real denim and have functional pockets. The only thing that doesn’t qualify them as jeans is a zipper, I suppose. I’m 115lbs, 5’7”, 27”Waist, 35”Hips, 18”Thighs, and got an XS. I have zero curves, but these make it look like I might actually have hips. For once, jeans that hug my skinny thighs instead of being baggy. Normally I would get a Small, but I’m happy I sized down. The length is perfect -hitting just above the floor. The waistband is really tight though, I almost couldn’t pull them up. But it’s made of the same denim the rest of the pants are, which I prefer over a separate stretcher material. No belt-loops though. Overall, very flattering and comfortable denim pants.

Worst fit ever I wasn't expecting top quality for the price, but these were so awful in so many ways that I don't think they're worth $1. The material feels very cheap and there isn't much stretch to the fabric. There's also these very odd puckers of extra fabric on the outside of the hips and on the inseam in the crotch area, which looks ridiculous. I'm 5'4", 140lbs and got the large size capris because I wanted a looser fit, but these were slightly snug in the waist and loose around the thighs, especially with that extra pucker of fabric. Also, these were not capri length on me. I have short legs and a long torso and, as you can see, they go down almost to my ankles. I'd have to roll them up many times to get the length I want, but the fabric isn't stretchy enough and is too snug around the lower legs to do this. Finally, there are back pockets, but the front pockets are fake. And be mindful of the tag that warns about bleeding dye even after washing. Given all of these faults, I wouldn't recommend these to anyone. There's really nothing positive to say about them. Don't waste your money.

Good Value For Quality Slimming Jean There's nothing more timeless than a good pair of basic blue jeans. The Dickies Women's Perfect Shape Denim Jean-Straight Stretch lives up to its name and offers tremendous value for well made durable pants that move with you while staying in place. These Dickies are the type of jean that you'll look forward to wearing if an effortless style and fit are what you're after. They fit loosely without slippage and flatter the form with its straightforward sleek design. My wife wears size 8-10 and the 10RG fits her 5'5 ½" frame just fine, albeit a little loosely around the crotch and derrière. That's fine with her given the comfort it provides and the classic style it affords. The stitching is solid and the quality button and zipper are signature Dickies. The material feels durable yet it's not too thick or bulky. The stretchy material infused in the cotton fabric perfectly accommodates an active lifestyle. The pockets are large and practical and can be used to store personal effects like wallet or phone. The length is versatile, working with both flats and heels. My wife loves boots, so these work well as a boot cut with it's stretchy design. But as the Florida weather warms up, sandals will be her go-to footwear and these look great with open-toe shoes. The Jean-Straight Stretch stretches the form and provides a slimming effect that works great if you choose to tuck in a shirt, but it's form fitting enough for untucked longer lengths as well. ~ Kort

Shocked at how much I like these I had super low expectations for random brand Amazon jeans but wow, I am impressed! They have their flaws for sure, but they are WAYYYY better than expected and super comfortable. Some thoughts: - Run a bit large. I am 5'7" and 140lbs. Great! I really like these jeans! I'm 5'2", 120lbs, 28" waist/34" hip and ordered a size 27 in Blue-4. The lay-flat measurements are as follow: 13.5" waist, 10.5" rise, 25" inseam (so pretty close to what the size chart says). They're made of a soft, stretchy material - I usually prefer non-stretch denim, but I like these because they don't feel thin or cheap, and they're sturdy enough to hold their shape. The online photos make them look like they're made of all cotton and have a button fly, though neither are true in real life. However, I still found them to be very comfortable and flattering, and they're not too distressed, which I like. They're more of a skinny/straight leg fit than I expected, but the waist fits perfectly so I don't think they're too small. All in all, I think they're a very versatile style of jeans, and I'll enjoy wearing them. Please note: be careful when washing these. I threw them in the dryer in hopes that they'd shrink a little bit and get a little tighter, but they shrank vertically instead! I lost at least half an inch off the length of the legs. Not a big deal when you're short like me, but someone taller might have an issue with it.

Finally the style I wanted! Been wanting some cropped relax fit jeans and looked EVERYWHERE. I’m 5’7” 140 lbs and a size 26 fit me perfect. Good quality. I will say they are lighter than what the picture showed.

Nicer than expected These Lee pull ons are a lot nicer than I expected. They are a good fit with some give and don't seem to be the type of jeans that stretch out after 10 minutes. I washed them per the instructions then dried them and they did shrink a bit in the length, so be mindful. This was a welcomed surprise, because they were a little long. Now they're perfect. I am 5'3" and 145-150 pounds with curves and a belly. I feel like they're flattering to my shape. I'd buy again.***Update*** These jeans really didn't shrink that much in the length, less than I originally thought. I usually wear a bit of heel or tennis shoes with these. Even so, I love these jeans. They still look and fit great.

Great accent pieces I’m surprised by the quality of these belts. They are well made for the price point, and the simplicity of the buckle elevates any outfit. The belts were packaged with care. Each belt had a sleeve and the buckles had protective tape on them. They also came in a nice box. I got the large size and I am 170 lbs and carry weight around my belly. When I use the belt on my pants, I could button it on the 3rd to last notch. When I use it for an accent piece with my dresses, I can get it to the last notch because it sits higher on my waist. I could go for a smaller size, but I don’t like the look of the extra holes showing on the belt. With the larger size, most of the notches are hidden and makes the belt look more tailored and streamlined. The extra length of the belt that goes around the waist underneath doesn’t give much bulk and is still comfortable. So it’s up to personal preference whether you want to go with size or look. Both the brown and black are great colors to work with any outfit. Whether to pull the look together or to break up sections. I’d purchase it again in a different color.

The buckle was very obviously rose gold not yellow gold as shown in the image. See comparison to my yellow gold ring. Also the cream/white colored belt was more tan/beige than white. Quality is a bit questionable but nice for the price. I got the large and I have a 34” waist so it’s true to size.

I never say this but I love the bell bottoms. I don't like bell bottoms on me because they somehow make me look, short, stumpy, and is historically a shape that is not so flattering on my body. These are however different. I love them. It's probably because they are cropped. I've gone through life staying away from that shape but these jeans changed it for me. The Jean material itself has a nice thickness and stretch to it. There is an odor and a chemical smell that's gives me skin reactions, so I will not be wearing these until I put them in 2 very hot machine washes. I ordered in a size 2 and it fits perfectly. For reference, I am 5ft 2 in, 120-125 lbs, athletic build, and size small/x-small, 0-2, 25-26 inch jeans fit me best. So, do order true to size. All the stitching and sewing was done well with no open seams, overlapping of material or loose hanging thread. Get these jeans! They are so flattering on.
