Halloween vibes These jeans are cute, but they look like pants to a sexy Frankenstein or Hulk costume. The faux distressed hem doesn't resemble normal wear and tear: it's too symmetrical. I'm going to have to either hem them and make capris or cut them to (ironically) look less deliberate. But the cotton/spandex blend makes the fabric stretchy, soft and comfortable, and the pants have five pockets (including the fob) and belt loops like regular jeans. They're a bit loose on me, so I'd have to say they run large. For reference, I'm 5'8", 120 lbs, 26 waist, 35 hip, 33 inseam (not relevant here) and ordered a 4. They're borderline low riders but don't have a short rise, which is good. In other words, not a Junior's fit, more women's (but not matronly).

I love these jeans! Comfort level = A+ First time ever that I have ordered a pair of jeans online. I have always been one to go and try them on in the store before buying, but with the stretch waistband and spandex in them, I decided to take a chance. I am 5'4" and 180-ish so I ordered the X-Large. I have a problem with so many in the pants line being too long due to my height. Figured with these it might work out to be just about right since they are supposed to be cropped. I was right! They are nearly a normal length on me, and I do not mind that it alters what the style was meant to be at all, lol. I dress for comfort, not fashion. These jeans are very soft and have a forgiving stretch that makes them super comfy to wear. I was surprised at how thick and heavy in weight they are. They will be nice and toasty warm when cooler weather gets here and in the winter. First pair of jeans too that I have ever been able to tuck in a shirt and did not have a spare tire hangover at the top of the jeans. These jeans are well made and look like they will last a long time. I can highly recommend these jeans. If I see a good sale on them, I will be grabbing a second pair, I like them that much! Very happy I took the chance on them.

Comfy but front pockets make them poofy My stats: 5' tall, 114 pounds, 29 1/2 inch waist, 36 inch hips, 22 inch inseam; 60 years old, carry my weight in my abdomen and thighs. I purchased size small/short. I have to fold up the bottoms a couple of inches, which I usually have to do, so not a problem there. While they are comfortable and I wear them a lot, I am not totally satisfied with them and would try another brand/style rather than purchasing them again. On the plus side, they are very stretchy yet are heavy enough to look like jeans. The back pockets are nice. I washed them in cold water and dried on medium heat and they did not shrink on me. What I don't like: Mainly the front pockets, which are deep and they allow too much fabric in front of them, such that the abdomen area looks poofy and the thighs look wrinkly. I was hoping for something a little more flattering. While I do not wear nor want to wear them with a shirt tucked in, I am attaching photos of the pants with my shirt tucked in so you can see the waistline, which also feels a little too thick for my tastes. An additional negative: It gives me a slight camel toe, of which I am constantly aware, though it's not so bad that I won't wear them. I am also attaching a photo of me in a pair of pocketless exercise pants so you can see what I look like without pockets poofing out.

I purchased size small/short. I have to fold up the bottoms a couple of inches, which I usually have to do, so not a problem there. While they are comfortable and I wear them a lot, I am not totally satisfied with them and would try another brand/style rather than purchasing them again. On the plus side, they are very stretchy yet are heavy enough to look like jeans. The back pockets are nice. I washed them in cold water and dried on medium heat and they did not shrink on me. What I don't like: Mainly the front pockets, which are deep and they allow too much fabric in front of them, such that the abdomen area looks poofy and the thighs look wrinkly. I was hoping for something a little more flattering. While I do not wear nor want to wear them with a shirt tucked in, I am attaching photos of the pants with my shirt tucked in so you can see the waistline, which also feels a little too thick for my tastes. An additional negative: It gives me a slight camel toe, of which I am constantly aware, though it's not so bad that I won't wear them. I am also attaching a photo of me in a pair of pocketless exercise pants so you can see what I look like without pockets poofing out.

Long enough skinny jeans - FINALLY I'm 5'10" and 146 lbs. I got size Large Long and I'm super happy with the length. They even bunch just a little around my ankles which I love and can never seem to find, being tall. I don't have to constantly be pushing at them to keep them from riding up to short, the length is amazing. I love how the pants fit, they are flattering but not too tight. There is good give in them and they feel like real jeans, not leggings, but are still comfy. I have bigger hips and a small waist and there is just the slightest gap in the back where the pants don't hug the way I would like them to ideally, but I usually wear looser tops so it doesn't show at all, and its not so uncomfortable that I won't get used to it. I ordered the dark wash and they weren't exactly the color that is shown, and at first I was a little disappointed but the more I've worn the pants the more they are growing on me and I can see them becoming my go-tos.

Definitely warm! I am impressed with these jeans! The color is great, and the fit is size accurate according to the sizing chart. They definitely are high waisted. The pants have a nice but slight stretch. The inside of the legs are super soft and the pants definitely keep you nice and toasty. I definitely recommend if you are going to be outside in the cold, say, for a night football game. They may be a little too toasty in a crowded place with heat, unless you just are cold natured. The pants are an average length (they were long on me as I’m 4”11) but will pair nicely with boots. You can’t really roll them up at the ankle as the inside lining shows. I do wish they left a little room without lining so they can be rolled.

Almost Too Perfect. I don’t think anyone is loving the elastic band on these pants, but that is my only complaint. I like my jeans tight around my butt/thighs, which is hard because I don’t much have a butt or thighs. But I love this fit and they feel like wearing comfy sweatpants. I’m 5’7”, 115lbs, 27”Waist, 34”Hips, 18” Thighs and got an XS. There’s a bit of a loose area in front, but it doesn’t look weird and makes space for more curvy figures. The fleece does add a scotch of bulk, but I need all the bulk I can get on my skinny legs. The cut is tighter in the lower legs than the seat and thighs. Probably not great if you’ve got larger or athletic calves, but the stretch is fantastic. I put these in the dryer on low to tighten up just a little, no problems. The fleece isn’t terribly thick like another pair I have, but is still warm and super soft. The blue is a nice wash on the less faded side than some. I very much recommend these fleece jeans to pretty much anybody.

Love them I love these way these feel and fit. The cotton is really soft, seams appear solid. The color is bright and they are just comfy. I really could lounge in them, feel like pjs. I'm 5ft 200lbs and bought a size large. Although I liked and wanted the relaxed feel, I could've purchased a small and would still comfortably fit in them. I have a lot of wiggle room and stretch, even in the waistband, which has a drawstring. I would knot the ends to prevent string from going in hole when washed. The waistband stretches another 5-6 inches out for me and my belly is 41 inches. I prefer the gathering at the ankles because tight fits around the ankle aren't flattering on me but with that being said a tall person can rock these maybe up to 5'5 or 5'6. I prefer the roominess of the large as it hides my fupa. I bought four designs and only have to return one as the red plaid has a hole in the right pocket. Oh, it has pockets! Not shallow but not deep enough to hold cell phone unless you had a tighter fit, like if I had gotten the small the phone would fit snug. They are not transparent so no worry about underwear showing. They are lightweight. I'll update the breathability later as I live in Florida and it gets hot. They have found a repeat customer. I'll update after I do my first wash to see how it holds up. Good value for the money.

UPDATE: After receiving the new pair in size Small I can honestly say that these are my favorite joggers ever (I'm going to order more in different colors and prints and I won't break the bank doing so :)). The fit is great : not tight, not baggy, the fabric is thin, buttery soft and washes well (the pants kept their shape after a few washes). The waist band is very elastic and the drawstring is easily removable if that's your preference. The one thing I would mention about these pants is that on my frame (5'4") they sit above my belly button, which would make them high-waisted; if you're much taller than me, that may not be the case for you.

Thrilled I took a chance on this product. I was skeptical about less than 15 dollar pants, not gonna lie, but these are better than I hoped. Pardon the mess but I wanted to show the length and my room is a wreck. I’m 5’7 and 130 lbs and took a chance on size small, and they fit like a glove. Waistband feels secure but has some give, and length(which I was most worried about) is just right, resting on my ankle bone. There is a good bit of stretch too, so I can sit or lay however and they don’t feel stressed or pulled too tight. I do wish there was a little more slouch in the calves above the bands, but purely for aesthetic reasons. They’re also really, really soft. Thin but the kind of soft that’s inherently warm. I’ve ordered two more!

COMFORT TO THE MAX MY DUDES Just wanted to give a little point of reference for dimensions, I'm 5'8" and weigh around 135-140 lbs (likely closer to the 140 side as it's comfort food season and I have little to no shame 😎👉👉). From reading the reviews I went up from my usual medium to a large for a little more of the baggy look. So this is SUPER COZY, but I will warn the material is pretty thin and doesn't stand much of a chance in the ugly bitterness of below freezing cold, BUT I feel like there's enough space in there to wear another set of leggings if you really wanted to. LOVE the print and there's a million options to choose from, definitely ordering more especially considering the price. May try to go with my regular size just because the bagginess does gang up around the crotchal area a little, which can be fun but not always the mood I'm looking for. Nevertheless 10/10 would buy these baggy knickers again 👌

I'm Very Disappointed in the Pocket Version I have owned the fleece lined legging version of this pant for 3 years and loved them but it irks me to walk in the cold with no pocket for my phone. Shoving it in the front of my pants always cause my shirt to get tangled in it and I looked like a crazy person trying to keep my pace and wrestle it in the front of the pants. Enter the pocketed version. I ordered exact same size. Fit like a glove, right under my belly button, though the pocket is tapered and my phone is tight, I didn't think much of it and went on my walk this morning. I only did 1.75mi because I was so disgusted with how far these pants had fallen while walking that I turned around. I attached pics of before and after walk. I pulled them up once while walking and they resumed hip position again almost immediately. Also, due to the tapered pocket, only the top portion of your phone fits in the top, so you have to push, wiggle, wiggle, push, wiggle wiggle to get it far enough in, so it won't fall out...all while trying to keep pace. Within half a mile, I only push, wiggle, wiggled once and it fell right out and hit the ground...in the dark. Whomever designed this pocket, had zero thought about functionality while walking or running. Sure, the pocket looks sleek but it's awful. For reference, I have the S21 which is only exactly 3 inches wide. Do not buy these pants if you have an S10 or the giant Iphone because they won't fit. The only plus about these pants is that the fabric is very lint and pet hair resistant. I'm 5'5"/160# and ordered the large. I'm super pissed that I just wasted over $60 for fleece leggings that are pure caca

The 1st pair was great, others are not consistent I ordered 1 pair of these to start and thought they were great! They fit true to size and felt very comfortable and stretchy (almost like a jeggings material/feel)! Although I don't normally wear jeans in the "long" length, I ordered it in these since the regular length was listed as an inch or so shorter than most regular length jeans. I am 5'5". The long length on the first pair seemed great. They were a little long, but I would rather that and make a small rolled cuff at the bottom than too short. Because I liked the first pair so much that I just got, I ordered 2 more. None of them are consistent. They are all made of different blends of material and have a different texture. The sizing and styles are not consistent. Each pair is a different length. The design on the back pockets of each is different. It does show this in the product photos, but I didn't expect the blends/textures and lengths to also be different. While each pair does fit (some longer than others), the fits do vary. The front pockets are also different. Two of the pairs have what I would consider "normal" front pockets for women's jeans. The 3rd pair has pockets less than 2 inches deep that they're basically pointless! I love the 1st pair I got but will likely send the other 2 back. Just don't expect them to be consistent.

Flexible & chic I love these Stretchy WERFORU Skinny Elastic Waist Belts with Gold Buckles for accenting dresses, long shirts, dress/work outfits and just overall casual wear. I went with the bigger waist size and they fit perfect for wearing low like a belt or up high under my chest as an accent belt for certain outfits (5'2", 185, measure shy of 43" around my waist/belt line and about 37" just under bust). I love the various colors included in this set and can pair with just about anything. I chose the set that included the white because my previous thin white belt did get a makeup stain from my vanity so I'd recommend putting the lighter colored ones away when not in use. The gold clips are easy to manage and look really chic and I love that it is a symmetrical design when hooked. Price point is high as I have seen sites like Shein and Temu cheaper for similar belts but the elastic on this does feel a tad thicker than previous ones I have purchased from them.

Incredible! Consider buying a size up from your typical size! I usually never buy clothes online because I'll never know if they will fit, but I'm so glad I took a chance with these jeans. Amazon was recommending I go with a size small because I typically wear a size 28 (size 6 or 7 in Old Navy), and I'm so glad I went with a medium based on existing reviews and the minimal difference between size small and medium according to the size chart. I'm a runner, just shy of 5'4'', and my pre-pregnancy weight was around 138-139 pounds. I'm now 18 weeks and my weight has only gone up about 4 pounds, but I was desperate to find some maternity jeans as I was getting increasingly uncomfortable from the pressure resting on my lower abdomen, especially when sitting in a car. I normally wear size 28 (size 6 or 7 in some brands like Old Navy). The inseam length of this size medium is perfect. No area on the jeans feels too tight or too loose. I love how high these sit on the hips because it feels like they aren't going to slip down. I expect there may be some minor shrinkage when I wash them so it may feel a little bit tighter post-wash, but they seem stretchy enough to continue feeling comfortable. I think these will also be awesome postpartum jeans. One thing I was concerned about was that the waistband wouldn't be supportive enough, especially as I get closer to the third trimester, but I simply decided to purchase a separate maternity band for extra support and to wear with workout pants or dresses. Overall, this is a great buy and I recommend for any mamas that are feeling uncomfortable!

Comfortable!! I love these pants! Lately I’ve been living in yoga pants but have yet to take these off from trying them on because they’re so comfy. I’m usually between a size 8-10 (5’7” and 151lbs... big hips) so I got a size medium and there is a little bit of growing room. At almost 12.5 weeks I am not really showing yet so I am very grateful to know that as baby grows and if my butt decides to get bigger that these should still fit comfortably for most if not all of my pregnancy. I highly recommend! The band is perfect. My stomach can breath without feeling like there is something cutting into my uterus. All of that being said, and I’m sure this is in a review out there somewhere, and this may be true of all maternity pants, but the front pockets are not pockets at all.

(But I’m used to that) I’m very pleased with these and am happily donating my old pair of jeans.

**Updated **Update: My fiance actually purchased these for me for Christmas and he had ordered me the Medium and truthfully, the medium fits so much better and the legs still aren't baggy like I was worried about in the first place. These definitely fit more true to size than the Levi maternity shorts do! If you're between sizes I would definitely say you're safe sizing up. I'm 5'4" and pre-pregnancy I weighed about 133 and was wearing either size 6 or 8 AE jeans or 27 in Miss Me and chose to try these in size Small because I had ordered Levi maternity shorts for my last pregnancy and found that even a Small in those ran a little big and I'm glad I went with Small on these instead of Medium. I'm currently 22 weeks pregnant and now up to about 147 and I'm afraid Medium would have been too big and I'm not one for wearing super tight jeans or leggings. I will admit the legs are a little bit tighter than I like but they are stretchy and I still feel like I have room to grow. I'm carrying low and the band snaps under my belly but I'm at a point I can no longer button my normal jeans so these work well and I feel like I should be able to wear these throughout most of the rest of my pregnancy. I would definitely recommend these and if you're on the fence between sizes go for the smaller size otherwise you may end up with baggy "skinny" jeans.

These jeans are very well made and are very good quality.

Nice but a few loose threads I bought these in two different sizes, so I am going to have similar reviews. The fabric on these is very nice, with a soft, smooth finish. They have a good amount of stretch -- better than the PU leather type pants. There was no strange smell to the fabric. The XL and XXL both fit my girlfriend well. She is 5'10" and an average sized American woman. The sizing chart on the product page appears to be accurate. These are very shiny and do a good job of giving the leather look, while still being comfortable and breathable. On the XL, there were some loose threads. I was able to cut them without damaging the integrity of the pants. On the XXL, all of the seams and stitching were perfect. My only other issue, other than the loose threads is that the product description appears to have changed since I ordered these. The product page now refers to these as "jeggings". This are not jeggings, they are leather look leggings. You can see this clearly in the pictures, so I don't know why the page changed. I would recommend these.

I really like these. I’ve always wanted a pair of faux leather stretchy pants. I decided to give a shot. I’m 5’6” tall; my waist is 31 inches and my hips are 44. Cute and comfy while they last I will say first that these look nothing like leather in person, however, they are still cute. I'm 5'4 and a size 18 in womens and I got these in a XXL. They fit me just how I'd like. They go past my ankles and rise about 2-3 inches above my belly button. I haven't had any issues with rolling or anything like that. They're not super thick or super thin, there is no see-through of my derriere and above all they are pretty comfortable. Now with all that said I absolutely hate the pockets on these! They're shaped weird and they look even more weird when you have the leggings on. Whenever I wear these, I wear a top that covers the pockets. I would opt to get the version with no pockets. The other unfortunate thing about these is that I don't see these holding up for a long time which is crazy considering the price. I would say that when purchasing these don't expect them to be an everyday type of legging. Think of these as something to be pulled out semi-occassionally that can be dressed up for the impromptu date night or hanging with the girls when you want to be comfortable and cute.

Surprisingly comfortable My husband got these for me, and honestly I was not super excited. I did not anticipate them being so comfortable and flattering. They are good quality material and well put together. They are flattering (size small - I am 5'6" and 130# for reference). They are now a base piece to my overall wardrobe as they are comfortable and can go with a wide variety of tops.

Decent faux leather leggings I’m 175 lbs and 5’6” and these XL faux leather leggings fit well. They are tight but comfortable so be careful with what type of underwear you decide to wear with them. They never slipped down once during the day and were light and fit so comfortably. The pockets were an amazing addition and I actually was able to use them, which is a game changer for leggings. They don’t look super close to leather, but are enough of a faux leather texture and color that they work really well for a casual day!

Go with your normal size I really love these pants! They fit great and are very comfortable and flattering. The pockets on the back are a nice touch because I love wearing leggings but sometimes wish they looked more like real pants. The back pockets do just that by making them more versatile pants that can be dressed up or down in a lot of styles! I like the shiny black leather-y kind of look, because it just adds a little touch that differs them from other black denim jeans or jeggings I have. The quality seems great so far. I'll update over time if they wear out quicker than expected or something of the sort. The only downside for me personally is that I ordered the wrong size. I normally wear Large for bottoms (5'4" 185lbs). But, I've gained a few pounds recently and wanted a bigger pair for my closet because even some of my large pants are feeling tight lately. I thought I'd go up a size and get an XL in these just to have a pair that fits me good now. It turns out they're a bit bigger than I expected though, because I feel the looseness of them and constantly have to hike them up, whereas I think they would stay up fine if I'd gotten my normal size. So my suggestion is stick to your normal size because you don't want leggings/stretch based pants to be loose!

These feel great on I was hoping for pants that were like thicker faux leather and these were not what I expected. They were thinner and felt like leggings, but with back pockets. Despite the fact that they weren’t what I expected, they fit perfectly and felt amazing while on. They were accidentally thrown in with the regular wash and dry cycles and it didn’t ruin them at all. I don’t normally wash and dry new clothes this way, but was very pleased that they were not shrink or get damaged. For reference, I am 5’4”, 140lbs and the large fit well. I feel like a medium might fit well, also because the stretch is great.
Belt is vastly oversized Bought a small 28-30 for my 30 in waist and it is a good 4 or 5 inches too big.

Super stretchy and comfy! I'm 5'10" and it's hard to find jeans that fit in length and style. I'm thin through the hips and legs. These are so stretchy! They fit like a glove and you can still move! Short is long on 5’2 person. The fit was as expected. I’m 5’2 125lbs with a mom pouch. These fit well everywhere but in the ankles. They are about 3 inches too long and bag. I ordered Size 7 Short. Favourite jeans! I'm 6' so finding jeans that are long enough (and skinny jeans that fit over my big feet) is a struggle, but I am in love with these jeans. They're super flattering, so comfortable, and don't give me a muffin top at all. I bought a bunch of different colours and I love them to pieces, would recommend to any taller ladies out there! I also normally absolutely hate high rise jeans since they cut into my gut, but these fit so well.

Junior low rise, not jeggings (5'9", 34 inseam on boot cut, size 2-4)... These are... odd. The back of the legs is bigger than the front- so it looks like it tapers in. Idk if anyone would notice but it was enough to bug me. These fit like a tall junior LOW RISE imo but probably comfy in the right size. I got a 5 tall because I'm usually a 2-4 but these were tight. More than long enough A+, but lots of cheap sewing errors. Not like jeggings, just like regular jeans are these days. If you have any butt at all, these will not fit. My butt is super small and looks HILARIOUS in these. I'm guessing would likely fit better in a 7. Idk if I want to try again, but I returned these.

Cute, fit well, but poorly sewn I'm 5'3" & 130lbs. I ordered the 9 Short. They fit perfectly, but there is no way I could go down a size. If I gain any weight they'll be too small. Length is good. Overall, they're really cute & the color is as pictured. The downside is quality control. The stitching on the bottom of one of the legs is horribly crooked. On the inside, there is a piece of plastic in the threading that scratched me when I put them on. They will be going back. I'm unsure if I'll try a second pair.

Love love love!!! I have no complaints. I got the varsity blue and it's a great jean color! I'm 5'7 weighing 137 and I got a size 7...the fit is extremely comfortable and there is room to loose a little weight or gain a little...Im 17 weeks pregnant and think I'll def be able to wear these for a while through my pregnancy.
I am not a huge fan of jeans. These jeggings however, fit comfortably. I am 5'1, 115lbs and purchased a size 3. A bit long, but overall the fit is great. Definitely not your average mom jeans/pants.

Great for $20 Fit pretty well, I'm 5' so jeans never fit right. I weigh 113 and the size 3 fit well. They're petite but still about 2 inches too long, which is actually great for me!! I folded them under so they work pretty well for the price.

Best jeans ever! Best jeans I've ever bought and will be buying more! I am 5'3 185lbs and I ordered a 17 short. They fit perfect not right around the waste and they fitnoerfect on my legs and not a mile long.
My new favorite jeans!! LOVE!!!! Finally a comfortable skinny jean for long legs! I am 5'9" and all my height is in my legs. These fit perfectly.

Thin fabric, color lighter than in product images Ordered the aged blue and expected it to look like as shown in the product image(s). The color of the shirt received has a much faded look. The fabric is thin and light. A bit disappointed with the extra fade above and below the pockets. It came all wrinkled, which should be expected for 100% cotton material. Not a big deal as we always wash and iron clothing before wearing them. Ordered size small for a 5’4” tall person. The shirt has a relaxed fit, and will be a good shirt to wear during summer days. Looks good on dark shorts / pants. If not for the excess faded look, I would have given it 5 stars.

Great wardrobe basic! This denim shirt from luvamia is the type of wardrobe basic that you will reach for again and again. Layer it under a vest, wear it solo, layer it on top of a tank or tshirt, wear it open like a jacket, you'll never run out of options. This top is lightweight, yet sturdy 100% cotton. I picked this up for my daughter, she is 5'4" and 108lbs. She is wearing the size small in the helium blue. Made in China, this denim top appears to be well made. All button, seams and hems are nicely sewn and have held up well to repeated washings. We machine washed in cold water and line dried. It washed up nice and soft with minimal shrinkage and no color fading or pilling. Overall this is a great looking, easy wear top that will get a lot of milage in your wardrobe!

Perfect shirt This fits like it was made for me. I am 5'4" and nearly 190 pounds. The sleeves are the perfect length and the shirt accomodates all my curves. The fabric is light and soft. First, the color of this shirt is beautiful! It's such a vibrant blue. I haven't had toouch luck finding a denim jacket that I love, but I feel like this shirt does the trick. I'm 5'4, 135 lbs curvy and I find the fit to be very flattering. Oversized look without being boxy, still very feminine. Perfect!

I am kind of in between size, medium, and size large, and I ordered the classic blue. First of all, the color is darker than the picture shows. They still are not real dark blue, but they are darker than shown. As for the size, I ended up getting the large and they are just a tad loose. I am 5‘4“ and 145 pounds. Also, they are long and come all the way down to my feet. The sanding on the backside is a little strange. Both legs are exactly the same shading under the pockets, but the positioning makes my saddlebags look wider than they are. I may have to get some sandpaper or pumice and try to add some more to the front and the back. I guess when you pay less money, you’re not getting an individualized shading, but all of the pairs will be the same. The rips are at the knees and nicely done. It seems like the fringe on the bottom of the right leg is about a half inch longer than the left leg also. They are very comfortable, and I suggest if you are in between sizes, maybe go with the smaller size. I feel like these are going to be a bit baggy after wearing them for a while.

Perfect fit! Picture was taken right out of package, so a little wrinkled. These jeans are very trendy and cute! They fit me perfectly and are a great length for my 5’3” body. I wear a size 12, these stretch a bit so I think if you wear a 14 it shouldn’t be a problem.These washed up perfectly, didn’t have any more fraying either. Very comfortable and just enough stretch to move with your body. These are definitely made for shorter people like me, I would still recommend!

Great pair of stretchy wide legged jeans My wife can be somewhat complicated to buy for as she is only 4'10" and trying to understand measurements when buying clothes online can be hit or miss. Happy to say that these jeans only require a little shortening but otherwise they fit her perfectly. She likes that they have a stretchy fit so you don't feel like you are suffocating in them. The design is very nice and the materials and craftsmanship are very good. We would highly recommend these.

Soft and super comfy stretchy jeans. According to the size chart measurements I decided that a size 16 would be the right one for me. These actually have a lot of stretch in them, so I maybe could have gone for a 14 instead as I do occasionally have to hitch these pants up again, lol. Not the fault of the pants though! They are made of 71% cotton, as stated on the product page and on the tag inside the jeans, so they may shrink when I wash and dry them, and that will help with the slipping down. They have four functioning pockets, two front with flaps that snap closed and two back pockets without flaps. They are somewhat short, so that makes them great for my height (5' 4") and under, I think. Even on me, they just barely get to my ankles. The material is sort of thick, so I think they will be nicely warm in the winter. I have worn them a couple times with temperatures around 70 F and usually I am happy to get home and put something cooler on. I love the color of them and the comfy fit. With all the stretch they have, there is no binding or pinching, no matter what direction you can contort your legs into. They are well made and all around just a nice pair of jeans, especially for shorter people. I definitely can recommend this pair of jeans. Comfort and fit are great with them.

Stylish and comfortable high-waist jeans but beware of the sizing and length! I have a lot of good things to say about these jeans! First off, the high waist is just perfect, it cinches the waist beautifully, creating a flattering silhouette. I also love the wide-leg style; it gives a lengthening effect that makes my legs look longer and adds a touch of retro chic to my look. The square pockets are a unique and also have a bit of a vintage vibe, which I adore. The classic denim is another highlight. It’s thick and stretchy, very comfortable,great for cooler autumn and winter weather. Plus, they’re super easy to style. I find they pair especially well with crop tops, creating a trendy, well-put-together outfit. Despite the quality and style, though, I’m not entirely satisfied with the Fit. I always order a Medium, but these jeans run large. I need a belt to keep them up. Also, the length is a bit much for me, given that all sizes have the same 30" inseam. At 5’2” and 116 pounds, they’re too long for my frame. If you’re on the shorter side and don’t want to hem them, keep in mind that they might be a bit lengthy. Overall, for style and quality, these jeans deserve a solid 5/5. If I were taller, the small size would have fit perfectly. Just be mindful of the sizing if you’re petite!

wash first - but recommend! Unfortunately I didn't take a picture of the packaging, however, they come rolled in a plastic bag. I ordered a medium which according to the size chart, this should be for US sizes 8-10. I ordered a Medium as an incentive to push me to lose weight/fit into these pants by the end of the year. As you can see by the picture, they fit now - love it!! They are NOT tight, they are loose, comfortable and can bend with ease. They are VERY stretchy and while I don't necessarily like how they look on me yet, BUT you can see that they are very well made.The stitching is great, the pockets are very deep. I put my iPhone16 (for reference) in the front pocket and bent forward and it didn't come out. I place it in the back pocket and it moved up a bit but 90% of my phone was still in the pocket so it did not fall out which is always a plus! The pants are a bit long but I am short (again, I am 5'3) so short heels/boots will fix the issue. The pockets don't "bulge open" because of the material and they are really soft and comfortable!Highly recommend but wash these first - no, they didn't shrink.

We will see how they hold up!

It’s giving early jegging vibes. Pass. Maybe a different color would be worth it. Overall, I would recommend for anyone who’s curvy, with a hip to waist ratio of more than 9-10inches. The material isn’t see through and you might get a summer out of them before the material wears through in the thighs.

Boyfriend styled So I did not go down in size because I wanted them loose style boyfriend jeans looking/fitting. These pants do run big but that's because their boyfriend jeans, that's the style. I do recommend going down a size if you want them slightly more fitting but they will still be some what loose I'm sure even a size down. These are not skinny jeans styled pants but I suppose if you order down in size maybe you can get that kind of fit. But if you don't want to feel uncomfortable going down sizing know your measurements for a more comfortable fit around the waist. If you look under the sizing chart for these pants it will show you waist measurements with the sizes, buy according to your waist measurement not what size you normally buy. I'm going to order another pair down a size and see how they look less looser than what I ordered because looking at the size chart according to the waist measurement I should have ordered a size down but I'm happy with the jeans I ordered because I wanted them bigger/loose anyways, more like actual boyfriend jeans fit which is exactly what I got. Update edit 10/20/23, I brought the size 1 a size down and the fit is even better, still loose in the legs and comfortable around the waist not tight but also not overly loose as the size 3 jeans. I still like the bigger one's though because I like having all different fits and styles in jeans but going down a size is more flattering to my shape yet still loose and boyfriend jeans styled still not a skinny jeans look. Also fyi I ordered the medium color denim but received the light color denim instead, at first I debated on returning because I received the wrong color but ultimately I liked them so much that I decided to keep them. But I read other review complaints about receiving the wrong color as well so just beware that you may or may not receive the right color of blue ordered.

Thank you reviews that recommended sizing down First off, I LOVE these jeans! Secondly, the reviews I read on here were a huge help in sizing. It’s weird because this are definitely Women’s jeans, but the sizing is in Juniors. For reference I am 6’0, 170-175 lbs, and a solid Women’s size 10/30, which would translate to a Juniors size 11. Per the suggestion of previous reviews, I reluctantly sized down to a 9. The last thing I wanted was for the jeans to fit for an hour, only to stretch beyond repair. I ordered a size 9 with 34” inseam. I am SO happy I did! When I pulled it out the bag I thought to myself, there’s NO WAY this is gonna work. But I squeezed myself into the initially doubtful fit. Wisely I decided to wear them around the house for a couple hours, w/o removing tags (just in case.. again, because of reviews). And to my pleasant surprise this wonderful pair of jeans decided to form to my figure in the best ways possible. So if you’re in doubt and you got some junk in the trunk, size down and trust the process. Because I could totally see if I had bought these in an 11, I wouldn’t like them now. Also the 34” inseam does almost hit the floor for me, but the next pair I order will be a 36”, just so I have some extra length.

90's cowgirl dream jeans! (the "brand size" column on the sizing chart is key to finding your fit) The reviews on the sizing of these jeans helped me tremendously, so I figured I'd do my part and pass along some information. I've had my eyes on these jeans for about a year, and when I saw that the "antique wash" version was on sale for only $20, I knew it was time to pull the trigger and a new denim staple to my fall wardrobe. My only question was...what size? When I looked at the reviews, it was clear that I needed to study the sizing charts before making my decision. My measurements: height: 5' 7" waist: 26.5 in. hips: 38 in. thigh: a little less than 24 in. inseam: 32 in. On the Wrangler's website, the sizes on the sizing chart are all listed in even numbers while the sizes offered here on Amazon are in odd numbers, so that makes things confusing. On the Wrangler's website, my waist lines up with a size 0, my hips with a size 6 and my thighs with a size 14. According to Amazon, my waist lines up with a 0, my hips with a size 5 and my thighs with an 11. On the Amazon sizing chart, the very first column says "brand size". As far as I can tell, the first number represents the waist measurement and the second number represents the in-seam. These numbers are really and may be the most in determining what size to try. I knew the jeans were supposed to have kind of a baggy fit around the thighs, but I wanted them to be more fitted across the hips and backside area so they still gave off a feminine vibe. I decided to try size 5-32 first. The length was perfect. They fit well over my cowboy boots, rainboots and lace-up/hiking boots. However, the waist was gaping and the booty and thigh area were way too loose for my taste. I decided to send them back and order a 3-32 in their place. I am much, much happier with the 3-32's. The material is stiff and feels like traditional, high quality denim, but they are not too tight anywhere. They feel equally feminine and tom-boyish (which is my style, in a nutshell). I could wear these to do yard/barn work or I could dress them up a little with a girly top and different shoes and still feel cute. The $20 markdown was too good to pass up, and I'm really glad I took the time to find my size. On the Amazon size chart under "brand size", the 3-32's are listed as 27-32. Again, I have a 26.5in waist and a 32in inseam (basically 27-32), and that is the size that ended up working for me. The rest of the waist, hip and thigh measurements that correspond to the 27-32 row on the Amazon chart do not reflect my measurements at all, but had I based my purchase solely off the "brand size" column here on Amazon, I would have gotten the sizing right the first time. I hope this review helps somebody out there who's looking for a great pair of jeans. :) Sizes purchased: 3-32 and 5-32 (compared in the photos as "3" and "5", respectively) Size kept: 3-32

The best throwback dress of the season! I am mildly obsessed with this dress and absolutely cannot wait for it to be warm enough to wear! It is just about the cutest style ever, made even better by the lightweight but traditional, no stretch acid wash denim. This material means it holds its shape without clinging. The style is extremely flattering, it’s like it’s made for beer belly days;). The top and sleeves are cut in a way to still show a bit of shape so you aren’t totally shapeless.The material is not soft but I think if I put it in the dryer instead of hand dry, it’ll help that. I took off a star because there is a very big missed opportunity for pockets.The buttons are functional thankfully, because I do have to unbutton it to take off (since there is no stretch).I am 5’6, 160 lbs, 34D, and tend to carry a tad put off weight in mid section. I am a true medium and so is this dress, so very true to size. Nice length for my height and preference.

Easy to wear and flattering I like the fit and flattering style of this LookbookStore Women's Short Sleeve Button Down Flowy Tiered Babydoll Denim Dress. For size reference, I am 5'4", pear shaped, 36b, size 10, 150lbs. I picked up the medium in the reef blue and I am happy with the color and the fit. It is a good length on me. My calves aren't bad, but above the knee things take a turn for the worse. This dress hits me just right, short enough to be cool and comfortable in the summer, but long enough to fully hide my thighs. The empire waist is flattering and gives me shape without overwhelming me with ruffles. It is an interesting design, as the tiers are only in the back. The front is flat with just the one seam at the waist. The sleeve is nice as well, decent in length to hide my upper arms. Made in China from 85% cotton, 15% polyester, this dress appears to be well made, the buttons are well sewn and all the seams and hems are finished nicely. Despite the cotton/polyester blend, the fabric is not stretchy at all, but I went with my usual size and it fits well. I just wanted to point out that I am not at all busty, I carry my weight in the bottom, so this style dress works well for me. If you have a large chest or are very broad across your back, you may find this dress too restricting. Overall, I am very happy with this dress and think it will serve me well this summer, even into the fall. This can be worn very casually with a pair of Converse sneakers to a back yard picnic, or dressed up a bit with a sexy strappy sandal and a great chunky bracelet for dinner out. It would even work well as a bathing suit cover up. I could also see this with boots and a little cardigan for fall. It washed up well, I did a cold water wash and hung it to dry. There was minimal shrinkage and no color fading, it didn't wrinkle too badly either. Bottom line, a great wardrobe basic that you will reach for often!

Shockingly well-fitting and comfortable I was not expecting such a great fit -- perfect, really -- of these on me. I'm 5'5", about 142lbs, typically size 4-6 in most major brands, and the size 6 fits perfectly. No waist gap, no uncomfortable snugness anywhere, hits right at my waist; even the length works for me. The wide legs are pretty wide, bordering on baggy; I think I'm going to get them tailored slightly so they're less palazzo-like and drape more similarly to Levi's Ribcage Wide Leg (which I love the width of the legs, but Levi's just generally do not fit me well in the waist, butt, and thigh areas). These have a bit of stretch as well. I love them. I got them in another color, and have another on the way -- they're that great!

They fit like a dream! It took me returning and reordering but I received the size I needed and I just love these jeans! Here we go again with Amazon trying to tell me what size I am. Normally, I wear an 8-11 in jeans. I’m 5’7 and 160. The suggested size was a 13. I’ve never worn a 13, so I went down a size to 11 long. Size 9 short fits perfect 145lbs 5’4 I tried the 11 short (was recommended) but the waist was way too big so I exchanged for 9short, I can tell that after I wear them for a couple hours they’ll be loose. The black ones are super cute tho. i wish they’d stop making denim jeans that stretchy material, what happened to the old denim jeans from the 70s 80s 90s?

Love these pants For reference, I am 5’3” 180lbs and hold my weight in my upper body mostly. Menopause mom. I bought the Jenna in size 13 short and able to wear a heel with them. I have been wearing Wallflower jeans for ever. Love the fit for my body type and the fact that they come in different lengths! My biggest complaint would be sizing varies. I bought the Jenna a few months ago and my regular size was tight and I returned them. I’m not into tight clothes. I ordered the same style and size this time (by accident, I thought I was sizing up one) and they fit great. A 13 short in the black jeans is tighter also and feels shorter. Thankfully Amazon has a great and easy return policy. But other than that, these jeans are great.

Lighter than expected They fit very well, I'm 5'6 and 130 lbs and bought size 5. The color doesn't match the pictures. I expected them to have more drastic fading on the thighs and be overall a darker wash based on the pictures.

Inconsistent sizing Bought my 1st pair in April 2024. I liked them so much that I bought 3 more. All jeans purchased were size 13 reg. The 3 I just received were at least 3-4 inches longer. Why the change? As much as I wish I was 5'8" tall. I'm only 5'4". Disappointed in the sizing change.

Comfortable, Reg length is long I’m 61, 5’3”, 179 lbs - Age is important, because it changes body shape. I’m excited to find these jeans, so I bought 4 different pairs, hoping something would work. Three of 4 are keepers. I particularly like the stretch, because they’re as comfortable as yoga pants. I don’t mind the waist being a little loose, and it has a slight gap. I’d rather it didn’t cut in and make a roll. It’s not to the point I’m pulling them up. I have thick thighs and don’t want pull lines you get when you cross from fitted to too small jeans. I’m at that age that comfort is important, but so is style. 😁 Over 10 years ago a blogger, Grasping For Objectivity, did a big series on jeans, showing how important pocket placement is along with pictures of the same women in different jeans. It was the only article like that, and it’s still the best source as it showed comparisons of real women and told the brands. (Never buy ON) Why is this relevant? THESE JEANS have the RIGHT pocket placement and aren’t remotely close to $100. What. A. Score! These will be my go-to from now on. I found the inseam on regular is longer than many other brands. At 5’3” I should always need petite length but have a short waist and long legs, so I usually require regular length, especially in cheap brands like Lee. You can see that regular length is very long on me. I could’ve ordered petite, but I don’t mind. If you wear heals, this would be perfect. I will buy these again. I’ve found my go-to jeans!

Best jeans for women with curves I love this brand of jeans. I’ve been wearing them for years. I was so happy to see that Amazon has them now. The luscious fit is the absolute best for anyone who has curves or an hourglass shape. It has extra room in the hips and butt while still keeping the waistband snug. I notoriously have issues with pants when they fit my waist properly. They are so tight on my thighs and butt. But these jeans are great. I also like that they have a very slight boot cut. I ordered my true size which is a size one and they fit well. For reference, I am 5 foot five and 116lbs measurements 26 inch waist and 38 inch hips. No complaints.

Comfy but a little baggy These are very comfy jeans. The length was good. I'm 5'2" so sometimes even the short length is too long for me. These don't pool at my feet but give me enough that I can wear a boot or platform. I'm not crazy about the pocket placement. They are wide and low. The bottom of the pocket sits below the bottom of my cheek. Like under the booty ledge. It makes my butt look weird. I have to wear them with a shirt that covers it. For the price I think they are good enough.

Finally a good jean I have been trying to find a nice, comfortable pair of jeans that weren't skinny or above my belly button. I always have an issue with pants buttons pushing into my gut any time I sit down. This is why I avoid wearing anything but relaxed leggings, which is anything but classy. At 5'4" and 175lbs, I have an even hourglass shape, I'm usually a size 16. So I went with the 16 short and liked them for the most part. The issue was that they were a little baggy in the crotch and had a habit of sliding down. I sent them back and got a 15 short and they are perfect and the length is nice, I don't feel like I'm gonna trip over the hems. I can't even tell that I'm wearing jeans, they fit as comfortably as my leggings. This actually caused me to forget to undo them when I took them off. I included a squatting picture just to emphasize how stretchy and comfortable they are. The pictures with the green shirt are size 16 short, the purple shirts are the size 15 short. I would one hundred percent recommend these pants. I intend to get another pair later.

Not bad First I will say the color does not look like the picture. (I ordered the Jenna) but not a deal breaker for me. love how they fit and that they are long. Personally I would love about 1/2 inch to an inch longer but these will be just fine. For reference I’m 5’10 and wear a 18.

Fit like a glove right out of the package! As most people who shop for various products online , I was a bit hesitant to buy jeans sight unseen. Some things you just HAVE to try on. But for 30 bucks and a simple return policy, I figured I’d give it a shot. I had zero pairs of jeans that fit well and I was getting ready for a mini vacation. GOOD CALL! These babies for great right out of the box! The rise is perfect (about an inch below bellybutton) and they aren’t too snug in the hips or thigh area. (Gotta love stretch denim👍🏻) But most importantly, the length/inseam was spot-on for what I like. I am almost 6 feet tall and around 170lbs and can fit anywhere between a 9 and 13, depending on the cut and brand. They look great with boots, tennis shoes, AND flats! I have the hardest time finding a jean that looks good on my frame without trying to dress like a teenager and I try to avoid the horrid “mom jeans” at all costs. (I’m 48). After a washing they still fit great. They get just a bit loose around the waist at the end of the day (as most jeans do) but a decent belt solves that problem easily. I will definitely be snatching up the other cuts and colors from Wallflower! I have had a compliment from every person I’ve seen that knows me in the last few days, even one person that asked me if I had lost weight. Nope! Just a SUPER cute, awesome fitting pair of jeans! I feel like my wardrobe has expanded now that I can wear shirts that I haven’t been wearing because I feel uncomfortable showing off the ol’ muffin top. Problem solved! Thanks Wallflower for catering to the real woman, and not just the teenie bopper stick figure... definitely one extra satisfied customer here!

Ordered 29inseam, got 31, even though tag said 29? Grrrr Ordered short, received mismarked 31in inseam. Jeans are adorable and fit great except for length. I am 5'1, 120, about 36.5 hip, 27 waist, muscular legs. The outside tag says "5 short" but the tag inside is "5 REG". Clearly too long when I tried on and then confirmed with fabric measuring tape. So they sent wrong size and they must have issues with production line. But flippin cute so 2 stars and I might try again...

Best jeans I've ever purchased! You need these! I am 5'6" 178 lbs and curvy. My waist is small but my hips are large. I have a hard time finding jeans that fit my hips and legs but aren't too big in the waist. These fit me perfectly! They are stretchy, soft, and feel like they were made for my body. I'm shaped like a real woman. I usually wear a size 14 so I ordered a 15 and 13 in these jeans. The 15 fits great but after wearing them a few hours tend to stretch out and become a little loose around my waist. The 13s fit a tiny bit snug around the waist when first putting them on, but within a few hours stretch into the perfect fit. I was skeptical about buying jeans online but figured since I could return them for free, I'd take a chance. So glad I did!! I ordered 4 more pairs last night! I will try to add pics later. Also the colors and washes of the jeans are true as advertised. I will never buy another brand of jeans again. The material is great quality and I feel as though these jeans will last a long time. I am used to wearing scrubs a LOT so if I say an article of clothing is comfortable you can guarantee they are extraordinarily comfy!

My favorite jeans I love these jeans. I normally wear a size 4 or 27 women's. I think these are juniors sizes. The 3 fits perfectly. They're stretchy straight out of the dryer, and they don't loosen up throughout the day to the point that they're sagging at all. They stay put on my waist and the length is perfect. I have a physical job and they're comfortable to wear all day, and sturdy enough that they're not showing any damage. I just bought my second pair today. For reference, I'm barely 5'6" (167.64 cm) and about 133 lbs (60.3 kg). Built like an athletic rectangle, I guess. EDIT: I ended up buying a second pair. I did everything the same, just followed the "order again" thing. The second pair is much different than the first. Thinner material, lighter in color, not as comfortable, a little tighter. I definitely would have ordered size 5 instead of 3. They're still decent jeans, but the quality went down.

NOT ACTUALLY "CURVY" FIT I'm looking for a soft pair of jeans that don't constrict (I haven't yet been able to embrace the leggings-as-pants trend). My waist is smaller than my hips and WAY smaller than my thighs, so I have a tremendous amount of difficulty finding pants that fit. These are so highly rated that I was hopeful. Alas...these are not at all a "curvy" fit, but rather seem to be intended for women whose waist and hip measurements are nearly the same. I'm 5'7" and 138, measurements matching what the size chart listed as a size 9. Though my actual waist is a 28, and the waistline of these pants doesn't hit at my waist, nor is it a 28. I didn't measure it, but you can see how huge it is. The jeans do fit in the butt and thighs, but I could fit my cat in the gap in the waist (I didn't try that, don't worry). I'm giving three stars because they're cute and soft, but the description is inaccurate. They're going back. Also, ladies, if you're shaped like me and can make a recommendation for a brand that will fit, please reply!

I haven't worn them for a full day yet, so if they bag out during the day I will update my review!

Fantastic. PS, real pockets! I lost weight from 178 pounds (80.7 kg) to 149 pounds (67.5 kg). These jeans were my gift to myself as a reward. I'm 5'6" (167.6 cm) tall. I don't think of myself as tall, but I always have to order "long" pants. This was a huge thing that made me choose these. I purchased two colors of 7 long. More bling than shown Update: rhinestones on the back pockets all fell off with one wear. It’s like they weren’t even glued on! Just cheap. I didn’t like them on the back pockets anyways, but it’s the principle. I’m 5’4” 116lbs and pear shaped. I ordered size 1 short. I was looking for some cute mid rise boot cut jeans after seeing that the whole y2k trend is coming back into style. The ones I ordered came with more “bling” than shown in the picture. I don't personally like that look. The waist was gapping a bit on me. The short length was a bit short surprisingly, even though I have very short legs. I’m pretty sure a size down would be too small. I am giving the pants one more try I ordered the solid black color (that appears to have no bling at all) in regular length and may have to take in the waist. The light wash ones pictured I’m giving to a family member who likes them. Cute jeans through I hope the black ones I just ordered I like better.

Wow really Amazon! - updated Great pants, fit really nice. The reason for a 1 star review is because I can't wear them or order a new pair because they are out of stock on my size. No replacement options and it unfortunately took 2 weeks to get these in the first place. Next time look at the clothes you send to customers before you just bag them with a security tag still on them. This is why I don't like ordering clothes online. Too bad, the pants are really cute and fit great. 08/15/20 - I updated this review to 4 stars because it's not the pants fault that Amazon can't seem to pull the security tag off of clothes as stated om many other reviews as well. The pants are great, fit comfortably and in all the right places - my husband was able to get the tag off without putting a hole in the pants like other customers have gotten . So as far as the pants I give 4 stars. We shall see how durable and long they last.

If you got booty...! I am updating my review! I love the color and style of these. They are also very comfortable. I am 5'11", 175-180lbs and got 15longs in Jenna at first and they were too big, then I tried 13long and finally 11long. I have a 29 inch waist, 22 inches per thigh, and about 41 inch hips. I have a booty and struggle so very much to find pants and wear sizes ranging from 8 to 12 depending on the brand and cut. None ever fit though except Silver pants (which are expensive). I carry all my weight in the thighs and hips plus I lift weights, which doesnt help find pants. These fit perfect in the thighs and I loved the length. The waist to hip/thigh ratio was what I really needed to be dramatic and the 11longs came super close. I was nervous the thighs would be too tight but the stretch in them proved how adaptable they are. I have a tad bit of room left in the waistband but its as close as Ive come to perfectly fitting jeans! I definitely reccomend.

Best jeans ever. Amazon, please continue carrying the Juniors version :) Due to a medical issue I gained over 40 lbs and have lost almost all of it. I have been out of jeans for 19 months. My regular weight is 115 and I am now 125 lbs. I bought a 5 as I usually wear a 3. These are so comfortable! I am 48 but have a guys non curvy figure. And I am super out of shape. I think they will conform even when I start to tone up and lose more weight. And I actually think these could work from 115 to 130 and I am 5’4”. Length is good with tennis shoes or heels. Dress up or down. My only problem is I bought a “women’s juniors curvy luscious bootcut. The day AFTER I received my order Amazon stopped carrying the Junior bootcut. I found the same pants at JCPENNEY for 54.00 fee delivery, right now they are on sale for 34.00 plus an extra 20-40 percent off. Oh yes, and the top front is right below your bellybutton and the back top has a nice flattering rise. That means NO MORE plumbers crack, period. It gives your shapes that low rise bootcut look but maintains its placement perfectly! Plus this material launders beautifully, t use cold water and tumble on low. The very best part is they maintain their shape and support comfortably. That means you’ll look just as good walking in your house after work as in the morning. I really hope Amazon restocks the Juniors curvy bootcut. Amazon has separated its stock by size and color but it appears they are going to discontinue this item.

✨️ True to size ✨️ These jeans are so lightweight and comfortable, they accentuate your curves and hug in all the right places. I've had so many compliments on them and I've only had them for 3-4 days! I've never felt more confident in myself since having my son a year ago than I do wearing these jeans. I plan on buying more! I highly recommend these jeans! For reference I am 5'5, between 185-195lbs and Amazon suggested a size 13, I went with size 15 so if after the first wash they shrink I don't risk not being able to fit them. I have plenty of wiggle room, but not so much that they're baggy or unflattering. 15 is ✨️PERFECT✨️. I bought the Jenna and Camille jeans (the picture of jeans with the flame belt are the Camille).

Skinny legs The waist fit and length were perfect. I ordered a 3 long, which is similar to a 26W x34L. I'm 5'3 and 118lbs. The only complaint I have is that the legs are cut pretty skinny, and look weird over my boots. For reference I'm wearing ARIAT Breakouts. Just wish the legs weren't so tight

Great for curvy pear/hourglass shaped people! Let me start this by saying I wasn't expecting to fit in these as well as I do. I bought these knowing I could pass them down to my sister if I didn't fit in them as I wanted to. Jeans are hard for me to find that fit well, and I usually opt for trousers or leggings instead. Day 1 of their arrival I wore them to work (see picture) and I had no issues with them. They proved to withstand all the bending over, on my knees and fast movement through an 8 hour shift. They didn't make me uncomfortable, they didn't cause any chafing, or didn't unzip and expose my underwear like I've also experienced with jeans. I got a few compliments from coworkers and customers on them, as they usually dont see me in jeans. The flare on them is adorable, however I will say as im 5'3, I did have to roll them so they didn't drag while at work. So shorties, be aware of the flare drag! Color is beautiful I love the wash. I didn't notice any weird smell from them. And they're very thick, as I stated before I'm constantly on my knees at work and don't feel much stretch from them. I attribute that to their mostly cotton makeup, it's not cheap polyester that easily tears under pressure.

Awesome 1970’s Retro look Returned the bright blue day color, that cost 3 times as much but kept the elevated retro color that was a steal at $16.00 and took a chance on boiling them for 30 minutes on stove top and drying on sanitize cycle for maximum shrinkage. See photo laying on top of retuned pair, the length shrunk almost two inches. Still need to wear with heals, so not for everyday at almost 70 years old, but they make me feel young again. I am 5’3” tall with longer legs and shorter torso. I bought the size 12 short which did shrink all over so if you are big from the knees up these may not be for you. They were scary long looking out of the package but 99% cotton does shrink nicely. If I manage to shrink them a little more they could rate 6 stars!

The only thing is… make sure to check out the length in sizing. I usually wear a regular length but if you look at the length for regular in these jeans it says 33. Longs are 35 and shorts are 31. I should have looked at that more closely and ordered short. That’s my error. The regular were a bit long on me. Next time I will order the short. Lastly, I apologize for the smudgy mirror pics. Very long!! I am 5’7” 168lbs and carry most of my extra weight in my tummy. I ordered a size 12 regular. I’m not sure if I want to keep these or not. Good Flares I was drawn to these jeans because it said they were mid-rise (and on sale). I can’t do high waisted jeans and I have struggled to find mid-rise flares at a reasonable price. After reading the reviews I wasn’t sure if I would like them. Many sizes were sold out because they were on sale. I ordered an 8 regular in a blue wash and an 8 short in a grey wash. As you can see from the pictures, the regular comes up to my belly button. I’m only 5’ 3.5” and weigh about 135 but still. I was disappointed. They were definitely long, but I thought maybe with a heel I could get away with it. Then I tried on the short. They didn’t come up as high (although still a little higher than I wished). And the length was perfect. Some people said their size was a little loose. These are definitely not loose on me. I’m actually hoping they might loosen up a bit after I wear them. I’m returning the 8 regular and plan to keep the 8 short. I haven’t worn them other than to try on so I can’t yet speak for how they will hold their shape after all day. They do have some stretch to them; enough to make them conformable and not stiff.

Good material, odd fit for me. These jeans are quality denim and a great length for taller people. I'm 5'8", 175lbs, and I ordered the "Blue Veil Dx" color option in size 10L. The hem hits the floor and since I usually don't wear flat shoes, this isn't a problem. The price is unbeatable. I haven't owned a pair of Lee jeans in many years. Unfortunately, now I remember why: The fly area always seems to be crooked or slightly off center on Lee jeans. Every pair I've ever tried over the years has had this problem. After wearing for a while, walking around, etc, the fly pulls more to one side and looks crooked. This isn't the case on any other brand of jeans I've tried, so it must be some flaw with this brand. It's weird. But hey, they're nice jeans otherwise, and many people seem to like them. This brand just isn't for me.

Great jeans I was skeptical about buying such inexpensive pants. But I have worn Lee brand before and knew my size, so I took a chance. So glad I did! I ordered size 8 regular. I have a 34” inseam, being 5’8” and most of it legs! These jeans have a 33” inseam. Just long enough. The long size is a 35” inseam, which would be dragging on the floor for me. The fit is great for someone with slim hips and a small butt. Curvy gals might think the legs were too tight, but they work for me. Material is soft with just the right amount of stretch. Highly recommend!

SICK i am 5”8 and about 135 lbs and these jeans fit PERFECT for me. the inseam is a little long, but i only plan to wear these with boots. i got the 4 regular size. the denim is so nice as well!! these are perfect for going dancing or just wearing out!!! will prob be buying more colors

So, so comfortable!! These are probably my favorite pair of jeans I have ever bought. So, so comfortable and flattering. The prices vary wildly depending on the wash. The dark indigo I purchased were $35 and 100% worth it. Other washes go up to $75. I also got them in a grey color. They fit like a glove, and the waist sits perfectly on me, not too high, not too low. I am 5'3" and 135 lbs and I bought a size 4. If you only buy one pair of jeans in your life... THESE are them!!!! Most jeans give me muffin top, sag at the butt, slouch down with my active lifestyle. Eventually I have to wear a belt and my jeans look sad and worn after a few washes. Instantly after putting these on, I just knew. It was heaven! They somehow made my lower stomach/hip area feel snug and controlled, yet No muffin top. I practiced squatting around in them, did a few jumping jacks, etc. There was no shift or need for adjustment. I was amazed. Truly, I did not want to take them off. Felt like second skin but durable. I don't know what kind of magic is in these, but keep it coming! If I wash these a few times and they fall apart, I'll revise my review. But after raving to family about these and those who know about Democracy already, I have a hunch they'll stand the test of time. Okay, they're a little pricy, so start off with a pair that are on a good discount. So far, I can say I'd spend the retail price for the luxury of a good, flattering pant. Comfortable waistband but too large for me everywhere else. I was so excited to find Democracy Jeans because I typically have trouble finding jeans that fit my waist comfortably. (I’m 5’7 and 140 pounds. I’d describe my body type as slim but with excess weight in the belly area.) I usually wear a 6, but these jeans were so stretchy that the 6 was too large everywhere so I exchanged for a 4. The 4 fits so nicely at the waist and really hides my stomach! Sitting down is comfortable and I don’t have a bright red waistband line across my stomach when I take these off! But the fit in the legs and butt is too large (definitely doesn’t give me the promised lift) and there are little folds of excess material everywhere. Also, the fly won’t stay up to save my life! Every time I stand up, the barn door is WIDE open. Overall, these jeans deliver comfort… and I love that they exist to accommodate more body types! But I don’t think mine is one of them :(

These are NOT jeggings! These do not fit the same as what is pictured and they are definitely NOT jeggings. New to skinny jeans... (lost a star for color difference) I'm not big on skinny jeans, but I came across these and thought I'd give them a try. I'm 5'7 140 lbs (after reading reviews I got a size 4) and have a "bubble butt" that has ripped the back out of jeans before. These fit great and have enough give in all the right places. Such comfortable, but trendy jeans !! For us gals who likes a "little stretch" , a great stretchy lower waistband, tummy control, and cute straight legs. Don't get too big because they will stretch out. I own 8 pairs of these jeans in various colors. I am 57 and 5'2", 144 pounds and I wear a size 10. I at first bought size 12 but they do stretch out by second day in rear end and look too baggy. So stick to your true size. I have a 40" hips and larger thighs , so they do fit. and they have very straight ankle. very cute with ankle boots or you can roll them up a little with sandals. You will love them.

Baggy in the knees - not like pic - disappointed I just tried these on and I’m wondering what the raves are about in hiding the lower belly that is stretched from having kids. As I sit, muffin top is real. I’m 5’3 188 pounds so I ordered a 16 short. I received a size 16 but had to roll the pants up to fit my height so didn’t receive short. On the model the pants seem to hug the legs to show great legs but on me the knees are super baggy which is very disappointing. The thigh is baggy, the calf fit is good but these are not short and my knees look like they lost 100 pounds bc the fit is insulting. I utterly hate the process of returns and wish Amazon offered a doorstep return. These cost too much to be disappointed. To avoid the whole return process I’ll just chalk them up to yard pants and take this as this brand isn’t providing the look the model is wearing bc of her knee coverage. I’ve found better on Amazon with pants that are about $20. I’ll take my business there. Not as promised. Any pants I have are a size 16 and have never experienced such a muffin top nor - just picture this - you’re wearing tight leggings but the knee fit is 3xs your knee size.

Way better than I had expected Usually I’ll buy some fashion nova jeans in a 15 for $30 and they’ll show up a little tight but stretch out in a few hours. Few months in they’ll tear at the thigh anti-gap lol. A coworker convinced me to get jeans at Target in July. Got 3 pairs of “Levi’s” in a 14 (because they’re stretchy) for like $40/ea. Literally wearing these things to work every day because that’s the only thing I can do through this apocalypse. Some how a pair of these garbage pants ripped in a month. A MONTH. So. I’m on amazon and I see these here bad boys on sale for $9 and I figure I should honestly just buy six pairs and call it a night. Just in case though I upped it to a size 16. If they run small, they’ll fit. If they run regular, I’ll wear a belt. Well guess what. They run regular. The delivery lady Just dropped them off and I pulled one on and these bad boys are so comfortable, but I ordered them big lol. Anyway, I’ll wear a belt to work. I got two pairs but I came back to buy two more, but the sale is over, sad day, but not that sad. They seem way sturdier than the ones on fashion nova AND these pants not only have 4 1/2 Real Pockets but I can also fit my entire hand in the front ones. Heck, I can fit a couple TV remotes in the front pockets. I’m definitely coming back, and even though the $9 sale is over, full price is still a steal compared to Target’s “Levi’s” For reference I’m about 5’5 and 197lbs this week but I’m kindof dense in the bones. A lot of my weight is butt, a lot of it is thighs...these jeans don’t quite do the butt justice but that’s definitely on me because I purposely ordered them too big haha if I figure out how to update this review, I’ll edit to add pictures when I buy the right size

Dont Think This Style's Good for Petites/Shorts, Otherwise Nice Im 5'5" a little over 35.5" in waist, 45" hips, 24" diameter thighs and 16" diameter legs/calves, no thigh gap. What drew me to these pants were that they were high waisted and on a lightning deal for like 20 bucks. I didnt think much about the cut beyond the waist but figured tapered legs would be attractive. I think the deal breaker for me is the style. They look kind of like straight legs on me, and since I have large thighs it's just more fabric than I want in the leg area. I dont like how the fabric bunches behind my knee and though this isn't entirely on the jeans I don't think they're doing my bum any favors. I did go a size larger than i probably should have with the 16, I just hated the idea of the top of the jeans pinching my stomach. I also got the regular instead of the short but I dont think the tapers sharp enough for the extra length to make a difference here, meaning to give shape to my legs. I think the taper's only really attractive if your thighs arent super disproportionate to your legs. Maybe like 2 or 3 inches max wider diameter in the thighs in relation to your calves. At least for the fit I want. I was gonna send these back in exchange for some 14's but I think I'll have to go with one of the other cuts to get the fit I'm looking for. Besides the fit I think these were a good buy for the price. Nice stretch and color. not as thick as some denim blends, a bit thicker than like denim leggings. Comfortable to move around in, liked fit at the waist. Better than most things you'd find between 20 - 30. If I had to recommend based on fabric and comfort vs leg fit I'd recommend.

Fit is great, continues to look NEW Great jeans, I have purchased several times. After washing they still look NEW! At 72 it is difficult to find jeans that flatter but these definitely do! Try before you buy if not sure if size is a great feature. These jeans are not so heavy or light that they cannot be worn in ALL seasons. Sizing chart is accurate and true to size. They do not shrink when washed nor do they fade! At 5’5” the short still works for me ending just above my shoe heal. Average requires a 1 inch hem or simple turn up! (Yes, that fashion is coming back for jeans) Enjoy your Vanderbilt’s, I know I do!

Decreasing quality I love the Amanda jeans and have worn them for years. I bought the lighter Amanda jeans from this listing two years ago, and the darker Portland Wash jeans last week. The finishing on the new pair is so much more raggedy than the older pair after many washings! The fabric content is slightly different: the lighter pair is 73% Cotton, 25% Polyester, 2% Elastane and the darker is 72% Cotton, 26% Polyester, 2% Elastane. The first is made in Jordan, the second in Kenya. I did buy two other pairs in the two year interim, and I returned them as the “fits at natural waist” was uncomfortably well above my belly button. The pairs I kept both needed to be hemmed, but I’m used to that. I’m 5’4” with a 29” inseam. The Short length is too short at 28”, and the Regular length is too long at 30”. This pair of jeans fit comfortably and the darker color is more dressy, which is what I wanted. I’m glad I have a pair I can keep!

OMG...now my favorite jeans! In case you are wondering. I'm 5'9" and about 152 Lbs. I ordered an 8 Long and I could not be any more satisfied with my purchase. They fit! I’ve gain weight over the winter from yet another physical disability and even my “fat jeans” are not as comfortable. Didn’t notice that these were a form of stretch jeans - the flattering fit type . Normally, I shy away from anything ’stretch’ because they simply do not look well on me. As soon as I received them I tried them on to discover that as have to admit that not only do they fit well but the stretchability as not the cheap spandex fabric. It’s tummy smoothing but not to the point of suffocation or back pain or pinching. The fabric is a light to medium firmness around the tummy and derrière and actually comfortable (my problem areas). They’re mid-rise I think and the waist doesn’t dig into my stomach as there’s good stretch there too. Also…no “muffin top”. The legs are long and smoothing also. I would suggest to get your measurements correct to get a good fit. I really like the straight leg silhouette. My legs are more firm and muscular and these jeans don’t squeeze; no struggle getting them on. I have to cold wash them and wear them to see how well they hold up after time. So far I like them. If anything changes I’ll update my review. I bought size 18M, 34 waist, 32 length and I’m about 5’4” and 175 (+/-) lbs.

Big waist gap Size 22 Plus - regular length - Black 5'2" - 235lbs - short torso - large booty I've got what I call a " booty shelf". My bum sticks out a lot, and you could almost set stuff on it. So to be fair, a lot of women my height and weight may not have this issue... The waist gap is big with these jeans. No way can I wear them. When they slide down, which they will inevitably do, they'll still show way too much booty/undies, especially if I bend over and/or my shirt rides up. The regular length is long on me, but that's not unusual. Otherwise the fit seems fine in the hips/thighs. I'm returning them, so of course I don't know how much they'll shrink in the wash. These will probably work great for people without a booty shelf. Since I can't wear them I'm giving 4 stars, but for a different shaped body they could easily be 5 star jeans.
