Extremely comfortable but stretchy, stretchy These 22Plus, Gloria Vanderbilt jeans have a huge amount of elastic give which does not allow it be body conforming in the places you want them to be. For example, they do not help you look shapely in the tush, hips and tummy. I am 5'8, 260 pounds with a generous tummy. The jeans do not hide much tummy nor subdue hips and are very loose in the bottom side of the rear, & legs due to the stretch. Without a body shaper on they are a tad loose everywhere. With a shaper on, they are tighter but still comfortable. Yet to me, still not the most flattering look. They fit a tad bit like mom jeans in my opinion. I almost exchanged them for a 20 Plus just to get a more flattering silhouette. But I decided against it as one thing that they definitely are is the most comfortable jeans I ever wore. So, I decided to keep them as I am of the age (56) that comfort trumps style. My first designer jeans at age 14/15 in 1979 were Gloria Vanderbilts (see me in them in 3rd pic) . So buying these were like visiting an old friend that you greatly missed. If nothing else, I am decidely loyal to old friends and our fond memories.

I ordered the 6/28 short and they said 28x30” on the jeans when they arrived but they were short (picture with the white sneaker and leopard loafers) so I measured and it wasn’t even 29” inseam. I sent them back and got 6/28 regular length and that is supposed to be a 32” inseam (which would still be too long on me) but they were closer to 31” inseam (picture with the cat). Finally comfortable jeans! I have such a hard time finding jeans that fit right. I find them to be very flattering hugging my curves in all the right places without being tight and constrictive. I am 5’2” and a half (haha) and the shorts length hit just right for me. I did order the 18s as well to try and they were a looser fit but I felt the 16s helped wrangle in my trouble spots. I wish they had more variety of colors I would buy them all!

They actually fit! Perfect fit. I am 5'2" and 105 lb, and in my 60's. I usually wear a size 2. I ordered the 2-Medium. These have tummy control and are high enough to actually do the job. BTW I got Shadow Nebula and it is just a solid dark blue jeans color. I would say they are nice average weight, not stiff and not flimsy.

Super comfy, smooth waist jeans It took me a couple tries to get the fit that I prefer, but after returning my first attempt, these second attempt ones are PERFECT! The length and size are great. Good quality, comfortable, great price! These pull-on bootcut jeans are fantastic quality for the price. The fabric is substantial like regular jeans, but soft and stretchy. I love that these don't have a zipper and button to cut into my belly = very comfortable! I don't have much in the way of curves, and I do think that they would look better on you than on me if you have some. I'm 5'2 and 110lb, and the size 2 is just a little big on me (the 29" length is perfect), so I wish they came in my usual size zero, but they are so comfy and soft I'm keeping them. I guessing that these in size 2/short would work well for ~120lb, 130 if you like your jeans tight.

The BEST. Where have these been all my life?! Looked for years for a great pair of jeans that really are basically jeans .. not leggings.. but with the stretch & comfort waistband of leggings. I finally found them & have bought both washes (colors)! I am 5’4” 150 lbs and size 8 is perfection.

Comfortable and nice looking. Awesome! Size 18 plus with 29 inch inseam. My height 5 feet 2 inches wearing low heal boats. My weight 195 lbs. I was comfortable riding a horse for over one hour. No seam irritation and no waist slippage.

High quality, great fit, very flattering I love these jeans! They fit so nicely and are so comfortable. The stretch is wonderful. I’m 5’3” & wear the size 4 long. They have a tummy-slimming panel, nice width waistband that doesn’t roll, 33” length is long enough to wear a wedge or good for a boot.

Not for tall girls Bummed these didn't work. I am 5'10", 170lbs and ordered the 10 Long. They are way too short for my taste. I'm fine with ankle length with a skinny pair of jeans but expect my boot/flair to at least touch the ground barefoot or with flats. I couldn't find the inseam length in the description when ordering but it turns out they are 32". I'm accustomed to "Regular" length being 32", "Long" at 34". Quality is really nice. I was worried that since they're pull-ons, that they would be be too stretchy or thin. They are 68% cotton, so that helps deliver the weight I like. As I am returning them, I will never know if they get majorly loose or saggy in the crotch after wear/ washing. LOVE that all the pockets are functional. Love the dark wash. They are form fitting in the bottom, thighs and waist. No gaping at the back. Waist lays right at the bellybutton. Wish there were belt loops in case you wear a shirt that reveals the top of the pants. I love wearing pull on jeans but I don't want anyone to know they are! Again, I am sad they aren't a true "Long" otherwise I'd give them 5 stars.

No slip. These fit! Kind of mid rise These are Keepers. Need more for squirreling away to avoid future pain. I'm 5'3" 115-120 lbs, I usually wear size Small/4. I ordered these jeans in my usual size 4, and they fit true to size. The jeans have spandex in them, so they're very stretchy - I can high-kick, squat, and do the splits in these jeans. They pull up like leggings, there's no zipper or buttons, they just pull right on. The pockets are functional. I usually wear a 28" inseam snd I ordered these in a 30" accidentally so I'll have to hem them, but these jeans are otherwise perfect, they look like normal jeans, but they're so stretchy and comfy that they feel like wearing leggings. The waistband pulls up over my love handles, eliminating muffintop. My new favorite jeans!

Runs a little small, but great jeggings 2023 UPDATE - when I say that I made these nearly an everyday staple for the past 2 years, I'm not joking. I wore these almost everyday, washed and dried them in the dryer endlessly. I finally purchased another pair (identical size and color as previously) and I am AMAZED at how well these held up! The wash slightly faded and there is one small spot where a thread loop (decorative only) came out of one back pocket.. that's IT. The fit hasn't shrunk in the slightest. So DO NOT HESITATE to buy these extremely high quality for the price and comfy jeggings!!! I'm always looking for inexpensive comfy jeggings for around the house when I want to feel like I'm still in pajamas but not necessarily *look* like I'm wearing pajamas. Grabbed these in a size Small. FIT I would say these run small. Not insanely small, but in particular the elastic waistband is too small to be comfortable. I'm a 28" waist and generally order everything in XS but this waistband is really tight on my waist. Other than the waistband I like the fit of these leggings. I am 5'3 and they hit just below my ankle which I prefer. QUALITY These are super stretchy but still look like authentic Jean fabric and stitching so it looks good and feels easy to move around in. LOOK/STYLE I like the wash of these jeggings, they're exactly as I hoped for. I don't think that the overly small waistband makes these flattering on me so I will only be wearing these with longer shirts that cover most of that area. The stitching and look of the fabric is an authentic Jean look and not cheap looking at all. FINAL THOUGHTS I do think these run small and if you dry clothes in the dryer like I do, I expect these will eventually shrink and become even smaller. I recommend sizing up at least one size, maybe 2 if you really want to be comfy. I would likely purchase more of these in more colors and medium size next time

This is the first pair of jeggings I've purchased, and I LOVE them!!!!!! They look and feel like real denim jeans! I'm not a fan of 'skinny' jeans, but I decided to give them a go (because I can't find anything similar in boot cut or flare without a hefty price tag). I've seen pairs of jeggings at Walmart that looked.. well... not good lol. I've also seen other people wearing jeggings and you can totally tell that they are, in fact, jeggings. They look kinda cheap? But not these, they look amazing. I've never felt more confident in any other pair of pants (is it technically 'bottoms'?). I'm very happy with my purchase. I will give you a break down of my body shape and weight. Something that I absolutely hate!!! I have fluctuated up in down weight wise my whole life. I have some extra skin as a reminder. But, here goes... I am roughly 5'8" and weigh 240. I am sort of awkwardly proportioned. According to me lol. I carry most of my fat in my belly. I have a hard time finding real jeans that actually fit me properly, because my legs aren't that big. My natural waist is 44, my hips are 54, and my lower hip measurement is 34. So when I try on jeans in a size I can get up high enough with my tum tum, the legs always look baggy and weird. Anyway, I think it's important to remember that I could be standing next to someone that is the same height and weight, and we will not necessarily be the same size. My weight is in my belly, but some are fortunate enough to have there's evenly distributed. I ordered an XL, because I fit in to a XL in yoga pants, and leggings. They fit me perfectly. The length, feel, and fit was amazing. They aren't as thin as leggings, but they aren't as thick as some denim jeans. They structure to them, so they don't highlight every bulge. I hope this helps someone.

Those reviewers whose pants split right away were likely wearing them waaay too small. If you’re on the fence, go for it!

I’ll most likely need to go up to, at least a 34. Very unfortunate. Although, returns are super easy, it’s still inconvenient!

Freaking Jackpot! Normally when I buy jeans, I spend 20-30 minutes crying in front of the mirror in a self loathing kind of way. I’ve suffered from BDD since I can remember and pants, jeans especially, have always been my biggest nemesis. All that being said… sweet baby Jesus these jeans are the freaking epitome of comfort. For reference, I’m 5’4”, 130lbs and haven’t seen the inside of a gym in probably 10 years and my ass is clearly nonexistent — I purchased the Maui Views (Waterless) in a size (4) 27 Short. Unpopular opinion but I detest high waisted jeans. I’m “short torso - long leg” so hard pass on anything that even gets near my belly button. I was nervous about the rise of these but am so glad I took the chance because they truly sit just perfectly under my belly button and at my waist. I’ve never felt so comfortable and honestly, like myself, in a pair of jeans. I could not recommend a pair more than these. *only hiccup in my purchase was that the original pair claimed to have been delivered but was in fact not. Amazon customer service quickly rectified that and sent a replacement pair overnight.

(no information on the length in the description) The material was a bit thinner than I was expecting for Levi's but had a nice slight stretch, If it wasn't for the length I probably would of kept these.

I like these jeans but the vendor is not clear about the length These are great quality, just as you would expect from Levi. They are more mid-rise, not low rise and they fir nicely on the waist/hips. I have knocked a point off the star rating because of the poor sizing communication. I ordered a '27 Regular' to fit my 5'4" and 125lb frame. The sizing chart indicated that the inseam of a 27 - which is considered an Alpha 'Small' - would be 30 inches. Unfortunately, what was delivered was a 32 inseam so they are 2 inches too long for me. I'm so frustrated! They need to change the description to exactly match the jeans you're getting. So, instead of labeling them '27 Regular' why don't they label them '27 x 32' just like the Levi's label does on the back of the jeans! I would love these jeans if it wasn't for the 'too-long' issue. Ugh!

Poor fit for women, and very short. I ordered these jeans in a size 34 (pictured) and also a size 32. For reference, I am 5'5", and I have a 31" waist and 45" hips, and am muscular. Neither pair fit me at all, which sucks since I love the colour and distressing. The larger pair fit me comfortably throughout the hips and thighs, but was massive in the waist. Almost comically so. I ordered a pair two sizes down, which is tight through the thighs and hips but is STILL far top large through the waist. To make matters worse, the smaller size (32, not pictured) are too short for me to wear, even with flip flops or another super-flat shoe... and I'm only 5'5"! Overall, I would not recommend these for women. They have a fit for a child: very straight up and down body with hips and waist that are the exact same size, and they are incredibly short as well. Based on my experience with these and other pairs of Levi's jeans, I think they should stick to making products for men. They clearly do not understand the shape of a woman's body.

Great jeans I was on the fence about getting a 24 or 25. I went with 24 based on measurements and it’s perfect! Proper low rise, hugs the right places, and comfy. It feels tight at first but has a little bit of stretch room. For reference I’m 5’3”, 24in waist, 35 inch hip

Quality is great I'm not sure how I got such nice jeans on sale at such a great price, but I'm thankful so thought a review is the least I can do. First off, thank you for various inseam options! I'm typically too tall for most jeans, & need the 34-35" inseam. Second, these are flattering! I showed the back pockets so you can see how cute these are. The value for price is worth it! Thank you!

A MUST BUY The feel the exact same as Buckles jeans. Only thing I wish was different, would be if the jeans were tapered in more. Otherwise worth every penny for the price. I’m 5’4, 155 lbs and bought a size 5X36 (36in long)

LOVE LOVE LOVE these jeans Was looking for a pair of boot cut jeans for my new cowboy boots. I’m 5’6” and weigh 134 and got size 3/34 and they fit PERFECTLY and are very comfortable!!

Definitely long enough!!! I love these jeans and bought 2 pairs. At 5'11, 195lbs the 15/37 fit damn near perfect! I'm slim built all leg and these jeans reach the floor. They are a heavier denim, stretchy, form flattering in me, and built to last. I will be buying more! If you are a tall girlie, don't think.. just buy! ❤️

Longer than expected I love these style of pants. This is my third pair of Willow’s. Unfortunately the previous two have ripped in about 6 months or less. While I wore these almost daily, it was not hard use. I used them mainly for office work so was disappointed when they ripped so fast. This new pair tag says they are 32 in length but are more like 34’s. It’s not a big deal but just slightly annoying. They are slightly darker than my previous pairs also. Otherwise they are really comfy pants with a slight stretch. I am 5”5’ and 175 wearing a 11-32(most likely miss marked 34’s).

No hiking my jeans! I've worn Q Babies for years, they fit OK, better than anything else I'd come across, maybe a 7 out of 10. They definitely needed hiking every time I stood up. I decided to try Willows in the same size ( I'm 5'9" 140 pounds and wear 7/8 34) to see if there was a difference. Oh yah! I carry my weight in my thighs versus tush. This picture is day 2 after sitting at a desk half the day. Never hitched once. Amazing. Fabric is soft but heavy enough to wear to the barn; they seem to have a little more room in the crotch, not visible really but you can feel the difference. Now if only they had a straight leg version in white...

I'm 5'11 and 125 pounds and I've been searching for months I ordered a size 0 inseam 38 which is a rare find. These fit amazing. They were pretty tight when I first, but after wearing them for a couple hours they fit me perfectly. I cant tell you how long it's taken me to find a pair of jeans that don't come above my ankles when I sit down and im so happy I found them before winter, because I'm tired of cold ankles! The front pockets are really hard to get my hand into but I could care less because they first good! I have no butt and they still fit snug on my butt as well. Highly recommend these jeans and the price is epic!!!! I'll be buying more for sure

BUY THESE! Okay ladies....these are a must have! After putting these on I’m done with my Silver Jeans and my Rock Revivals. These are truly amazing. I couldn’t get great pics but these fit so well! I’m 5’10 and 180-185lbs and I bought the 13x36. These stretch in all the right places and are so comfortable. I love the mid rise, they’re perfect for working outside or date night. I’m buying these in a darker color as soon as they’re back in stock!!! I’ve not washed them so if they shrink in the wash I will edit my review to add that info.

Good jeans For reference: 5'5" 110lbsAt first I ordered the Maggie in size 1 (32) and it was too tight. Then I ordered sizes 3 and 5 which, honestly, I like both of. The 3 is snug without squeezing me to death. The 5 is a bit looser which is more my style as I tend to like that roomy feel. Granted, both of these sizes leave a gap at the small of my back where they don't hug. I don't particularly care. I'm not looking for a "flattering" pair of pants. What I do need are pants that fit comfortably and hold up to wear and tear. These jeans are pretty weighty meaning they're good quality. They also have real pockets- not those waste-of-material tiny finger tip dips you get with other women's pants. Length is perfect, reaching the floor but not tucking completely under my feet. Overall, solid jeans and a brand I plan on sticking with. Can't wait to go riding in them. Good luck jean hunting, y'all!

I'm 5'4 140 and tried the 9-32 couldn't button these but they're super cute so I'm going to try a bigger size Edit- first 2 pics are the size 9 second 2 are size 11. I didn’t end up buying these jeans. They’re not the best fit for me. 9 was too small and 11 gapped around the waist too much.

I love the waistband Good: Comfortable. Flexible. Good color, not super dark or too light. The jeans washed well, no shrinkage. Bad: The jeans were slightly long on me but it was expected since it has a 32 inch inseam and I am petite. The denim material seems thinner than my other Wranglers. I am 5 feet tall 128 lbs (give or take) and ordered 5W 32L. The waistband on these jeans are reminiscent of my wrangler Q-baby jeans. I like how they don’t have the gap on the back of the jeans. The Wrangler willow jeans are higher in the back which is great for me because I require more coverage in the back. I have always been a fan of Wrangler jeans and so far these are great.

Perfect for taller, curvy, athletic gals! I'm 5'8" and range in weight from 170-190 and the 13" x 36" fit great with enough length, so the jeans are not too short while riding but not dragging on the floor. (My normal pant size is 12-14 and other jeans are 31-32" waist.) The length at the bottom hem is even with the back of my 2" boot heel (Ariat VentTek western boots) so I am closer to 5'10" with the boots. I have trouble finding the longer inseam with my waist measurement so I'm glad these fit! It's great I can order online and get fast prime shipping too. I haven't tested them for riding yet, but I will get a LOT of time in the saddle/barn this Summer and will post an update after I try them out for durability.
reviewer_name_raw: Not all who wander are lost

user_comment: Love the fit Very high quality with the kind of dense stretch that provides a great fit without getting too loose or baggy after wearing awhile. Here are the measurements of the size 9 Long: Waist 30” (stretches to about 33”) Hip 37” Rise 9 1/2” Inseam 34”

I share because I care... So, it takes a lot of courage to share this photo but it was necessary, because I care about YOU as an Amazon shopper. I need to save my fellow 5’5” or shorter ladies from a huge jean mistake. Because I don’t know who decided these were considered “mid-rise”, but my Lord, y’all. The zipper is literally longer than my hand. If you are much shorter than me, you have a strapless jumpsuit. I’ve had a few pairs of Wranglers in the past for riding. And usually they are my go to because they do last. But these will be sent back and the hunt continues for a decent pair of jeans.Side Note: I got Davis, 9x32. The denim is actually really nice, I liked the back pocket detail and the length was great. So pretty bummed about the one foot Mom jean zipper.

These look great on my daughter. She is aIK size 8, 5 foot 7 and long-legged and the size 3 in 34L is a great length and size for her. They are the nicest jeans I’ve seen on her, flattering and great quality.

Nice jeans, sturdy material but bulky These are well-constructed and a gorgeous color. I like flares but it seemed the whole leg was flared, not just the lower portion. So lots of fabric around my glutes/thighs. It’s hard to see this and the amount of extra fabric in my pictures. but they are very bulky and heavy. These do run long as I’m 5’7 and had on 2”Heels so they didn’t drag the ground.I wish these didn’t have so much bulk in legs and I would have loved them. But ended up returning
I am 5’7” and I thought a long would not be too long for me. It was way too long so I tried a regular still 4 inches too long . The material is nice though and the jeans are very comi.

Fits perfectly! Ordered a size 16 short. I’m 5’0” and usually wear a size 16. This are just long enough for me to wear heels with. I love the slightly oversize fit without it being too baggy. Very comfortable and great quality

Stretchy and tummy control! I am curvy. And picky with jeans. I am about 190# and 5’10”. My hips are 45 and waist around the belly button is 36. I bought 12 long which is the size I buy in American Eagle. There is no gap in the back. Tight in upper thigh but they are stretchy I can do squats without thinking they will rip!! These jeans are great!!!! I will buy again and all the colors.

Cute I gave four stars because I don’t *always* like the way these pants look on me. I’m obviously shorter and heavier than the model, but my expectation was slightly higher. Styled right I do really like them, and they’re really comfortable! I really like the third pic with the white shirt (also from Amazon and it’s amazing!), but I didn’t like them at first with the leopard top or another top I paired with them that’s black and white, so maybe they don’t go well with prints, who knows? Lol. They run big, I’m usually a 16/33waist, but I got a 14 based on other reviews and they fit great.

Nice pants I ordered a size smaller based on other reviews and I probably shouldn't have. They button but are tight. Hoping they stretch a bit as I wear them. The material is nice abd I love the color. I ordered them in a long as well and they hit my ankles (I'm 5'9" )

Nice fit, but color not variated like images. I got the Cape Town colored jeans and was disappointed that they definitely don’t match the photos, which show variation on the wash. The ones I received are just a solid medium blue and not as cute/trendy. However, I do like the stretch and fit. I am 5’9” and 160 and the 8L fit me nicely overall. Because they are a stretchy material, they do shift at the waist the longer you wear them so I did have to pull them up a little throughout the day (especially after bending) but I don’t think a smaller size would fit. The length is good for my height but not as long as some “long” jeans I’ve had; I think if you were taller than 5’9” they wouldn’t be long enough (even the long size). They are comfy though and I like them!

Tighter in the hips. I have such a problem finding jeans that fit. I'm a straight for through the hips with hardly a butt. These are tighter through the hips and relaxed in the legs for me. I actually find them really comfortable, but they don't fit me as the picture shows. Otherwise I'd give 5 stars. But for the price and for jeans that are actually long enough, these are great. I'm 5'10" with a thin frame. So the length is what is important for me. I do like that these are thicker jeans. They do have stretch. But they're not "fitted" through the legs like I expected. Still comfy enough to keep!

Comfortable, Flattering, Great Value - GO FOR IT!! Haven’t worn jeans in years, instead ankle-length, moderately supportive athletic leggings had become my mainstay. I am 5’2”, approx 115 lbs with tummy and let’s just say not massively firm from tush through to thighs — despite being a casual 3x/week runner... After trial and error, I have determined I am a 2S... Any bigget or longer and they would not hit my ankles in the perfect spot, and the waist and butt would be too loose... These things are A-MAZING... they provide support, smooth out my abundant lumpiness, keep my muffin top in check and feel great! I have yet to see how they stand up to linger wearing, whether they stretch out and wash well, but will back with an update once I do... While I certainly have obvious issues and a body befitting a 50-something, somewhat active woman battling the UNIVERSAL challenges of a changing physique (ahem) I am pleasantly surprised EVEN PLEASED with the magic of these jeans... I kinda feel like I am misrepresenting myself to the world (but in a good way, lol) .... Love these so much that I have been wanting to order them in GALA, but they are not available... So as a back up I ordered another in Cape Town— the wash highlight on my first pair was perfect and happily, the over all tone is darker than it appears in the product pic. But the second pair ordered was visibly larger in the thighs and lighter in color overall and so I am returning them and hoping to re-purchase one that fits like the first and will wait patiently and hopefully until they offer the GALA in 2S. If you’re on the fence I highly recommend you take a chance and order these… I would also recommend Levi’s gold label modern skinny Jean — very similar feel and fit and currently available in a very dark wash called immaculate… I have already purchased two of those and I’m very pleased… I never thought I’d have a jean wardrobe: I am thrilled! Thank you Amazon! Than you Levi’s 👍🏼🙏💕😎😍

My new favorite jeans! For reference, I'm 5'2" and ~130ish lbs, a little bit of a curvier body type! I recently picked up these jeans, and honestly, I was pleasantly surprised. The fit is great – comfortable and stretchy, and they don't lose their shape in between washings! The fabric is durable but still breathable, and they are SO comfortable that I actually don't need to rush home to take them off at the end of the day. Plus I didn't have to spend 1/3 of my paycheck on them, definitely a great price for the quality! Delivery did take a few days longer than expected, but all in all, if you're a little bit curvy and need a new pair of reliable jeans that are actually flattering without suffocating you, I definitely recommend giving these a try! 👖💙

Loved them more than I thought I would I’m 5’6, 176.6 lbs & bout size 10 with 30” length (this always helps me so much so I thought I’d add it in) I gain weight evenly with a little extra around my middle. I thought-because I hadn’t read the description, because I forget to do important things like that- that these we jegging style with no button or zipper. I’m glad I didn’t see that it had them because I wouldn’t have bought them. Since 2020 I believe strongly in “the closer to lounge wear the closer to God”. You can quote me. Alas these have a button and zipper but it doesn’t make my fupa look like a butt! (Insert every happy emoji here) That is always my fear with traditional jeans. - I have one butt I don’t need 2. Also these aren’t high rise jeans like our moms (I’m 38) or teenagers wear. They’re mid rise. Can I get a hell yea?!?! I put up some review pictures and in them I I sucking in my gut in one and I’m not in the other so that you can get an accurate idea of muffin top if you’re my size. I feel like these are the perfect height to not be too muffin top producing. I know you may not be able to tell because I don’t have a fully suck-inable gut but whatever I feel like it would help me if people did that for me.

Great jeans I’m really happy with these jeans. They don’t feel cheap or super thin and are going to last me a good time. I’m 5’5” at 146 pounds and got a size 8 short. They fit perfectly and the length of the leg goes right above my ankle which is what I was looking for. Can’t go wrong with a good pair of Levi’s

Sized small, but almost perfect I swore I ordered a 28inch waist, but received a 27. When I got it - just in a bag, I instantly thought I would have to return it. We all know the drill and pain of trying on pants… but the 27’s slid over my hips and then buttoned up. I could have even gone with a 25, I think. 🤗 I am 5’3” 150lbs - 28inch waist, 41 and 43 inches on the high and low hip, and a 30inch inseam. These jeans hug my thighs but are not a struggle to get on. I also have really big calves, so this is surprising. My hips are easily accommodated with the zipper and button. Once on I have a tiny <2inch back pant gap which is easy to hide or covered by wearing a belt. So that’s the almost perfect part. Had I gotten a size 25 or a high waisted pant I do not believe this would be a problem. On the other hand I have a short torso and my rib-hip length is <4-5inches so I’m always hesitant to get a high waistline pant for this reason- as it shortens my appearance by a foot. My butt looks good, but it does not lift it. My husband thought they wear nice on me… I haven’t washed them yet - I’m just happy to have a pair of pants that fit well enough and flatter my appearance. Should they shrink or unravel in the wash I’ll amend / update this review.

Cute jeans Update: ordered another pair in size 4 and they fit better than any other skinny jeans I've ever bought..... but the color still isn't right.... it's like they are sending a completely different color and I really liked the lighter one in the options. They look lighter in my photos than in real life because I had to lighten the picture, since the room was kind of dim. The color is not as light (I ordered Capetown waterless) as the photos on here, but it's pretty much the same as the blue Laguna waterless. I think they are getting them mixed up or something. This is two pairs in the wrong color so far 🙄. The fit is good, but maybe runs a little big? I am 5'5" and 130lbs and the size 6 regular length was pretty long... Def doesn't seem like a 29" inseam... Seemed closer to 32". Ordered a pair in the 27" and the length is great, however I think I need to get a size 4 as this pair looks great but starts falling down a little when I'm walking around. LOVE that there is NO HUGE GAP IN THE BACK! Yay! Once I get the size perfect these will be the perfect pants.

Good quality, no gapping I order a size 2 in the Capetown waterless color. I usually wear a size 4 in jeans but lately I've noticed a lot of people have changed their sizing and I'm ordering more brands in a size 2. I'm 5'3" and 113 lbs, pear shaped. These weren't the best jeans I've ever bought but they are somewhat flattering compared to most brands I've bought as of late. They are slightly loose in the waist still but they don't gap nearly as bad as other jeans seem to do. I'm short but I don't buy " short" jeans because they end up looking like high waters. These hit at a decent length without bunching up too much and also looked good rolled at the bottom. I have a very short torso and these hit just below my belly button so if you have a longer torso it may not be high waisted on you. I'm not sure I'd order more but I'll be keeping these.

Flattering & Fit Well But Color is Not as Depicted I purchased the Size: 6 | Inseam Length: 28 Inches | Color: Cape Town-waterless. They fit true-to-size, I am 5' 3" ~133 lbs and curvy. The fit was snug but flattering with a generous amount of stretch. The 28" length was perfect - right at my ankle. For anyone wondering, these fit very very similar to the pull-on version (which I also purchased) and the sizing is consistent between the two. In my opinion, they are equally as comfortable and you get real functioning front pockets with this version. I absolutely would have kept them if the color was as depicted in the photos. However, the color of the whiskering and fading is much more yellow and dingy in person and not at all white like the pictures. Overall, these are a really great value for a twenty-ish dollar price point especially if you find a color you like. I'm trying to lose the quarantine 15 and not looking to invest in expensive jeans until I do.

Fit comfortably I recently had barriactric weight loss surgery. I wasn't really sure of my size so I got a size 14. I am 5'7" and weigh 185. I still have a bit of a tummy. These fit so good. They move with you without feeling like you have to keep pulling them up. I am buying more because of the stretching I can lose more weight and still wear them.

Color much darker than shown Shopping in this pandemic without being able to try on is a nightmare. Love Amazon wardrobe, but the husband and I have both bought and returned so many jeans because of the color discrepancies in the stock photos vs what arrives. Il prodotto è arrivato il giorno dopo l'acquisto, all'interno di una busta di plastica ben sigillata, recante il marchio Amazon e all'interno della busta di carta di Amazon. Indosso una taglia 48 e ho acquistato una taglia 48: veste perfettamente. Il jeans è a vita alta, copre l'ombelico, non risulta aderente, né ingolfante, ma accompagna dolcemente le forme. Sono alta 1.73cm, posso, quindi, affermare che il jeans resta aderente lungo le caviglie e le lascia scoperte di due centimetri, senza creare l'effetto "casa allagata". Il colore corrisponde a quanto indicato dalle foto. Unico neo: l'odore di plastica che proprio non se ne va. L'ho notato con tutti i jeans marca Amazon: servono numerosi lavaggi per fare attutire questo odore, che comunque resterà per sempre nel jeans. Ho la necessità di coprirlo con l'igienizzante spray. Questo a causa di materiali non proprio di grande qualità, infatti sono jeans che tendono a deteriorarsi facilmente.

As happy as I can be Ordered size 14, 5'7" / 202lbs and I don't love the way anything fits me at this weight. That being said, these jeans are not bad. They fit well, not super tight and not baggy. There is lots of stretch in the denim, so they are very comfortable even when sitting down. The denim is not thin so that it does not show every lump or bump. The waist goes to just at my navel, which means there is no muffin top. I posted a picture of the inside of the jeans front panel. I don't know that I look "thinner" but they don't make me look worse and thats a good thing. The jeans are long enough for 3 inch heels (I only had 2.5 inch heels on in the picture.) The denim is thick enough and has enough stretch to cuff them at the bottom to make them ankle length to wear with flats and still look cute. If someone wants the jeans to be tight and form fitting (for the lifting and tucking) I would suggest sizing down one size from usually size.

Pretty nice jeans I felt compelled to write a review because there were none in my size range. I usually wear a 6R at 5'7" 140ish lbs, which is what I ordered. Something I noticed before ordering was that the measurements on the size chart for sizes 6 and 8 were the same. What? So how do I know what size to get? I usually get a 6, but knowing this brand is usually for younger people it felt like a risk. As you can see from the photos, it's a really snug 6. I did try these on at the end of the day when (for me) pants are tightest because of gravity 😂 I think that after some wear and washes, they will get softer. The material is a bit rougher than I usually like, with less give, but you get what you pay for. They will be good for cool weather. I also like the wash, I don't like huge lines on my hips and these aren't too in-your-face. (I would have opted for a darker wash but it wasn't available in my size.) So overall pretty good, I'll throw them in the rotation and pair them with a thrift store flannel to keep the dream of the 90s alive 😎

The best of the barrels For reference, I carry all my weight in my midsection so it's very difficult to find jeans that fit comfortably and don't show my apron belly. I typically wear a size 12 or 14 and am 5'1" tall, so length is also always an issue. I first ordered the $98 Free People barrel jeans in all the colors, hoping they would look cute. I tried one on and didn't even bother trying on the rest, I immediately returned them. Unless you're a size 2, the FP jeans were clownishly huge in the legs, but the denim was buttery soft. I am not typically satisfied with the quality of clothing on Amazon, but decided to try several barrel jeans because I wasn't having any luck from name brands. To my surprise, these fit GREAT! The fabric is a denim blend so not the same quality of designer fabric, but they are beyond flattering - comfortable stretch in the waist, slim through the hips, then a reasonable barrel flare with a tapered bottom. They skim the tops of my shoes so I have to cuff them up a little and they're perfect. Might hem them, but I hate losing the original stitching. I ordered 3 colors and love all of them. The Black is perfectly faded, the Deep Blue is a really nice medium blue, and I'm so happy they make the Retro Blue, which is just like the FP jeans with the slightest green tinge. I initially ordered a pair of barrel jeans from another seller, Grapent, and I kept them but the wash is not as nice, the pockets kind of stretch out, and these jeans are a better shape and quality of colors. If you're an apple shape and hard to fit, I really recommend these. I'm so happy to be able to buy into a trend for less than half the cost of the designer brands. What a find!!!

roomy, stretchy, so comfortable these are SO cute and the perfect price for trying this trend. my favorite silhouette for jeans is barrel and i feel it flatters pear shapes beautifully. it's also the most comfortable imo as your thighs/hips have room and the curved shape balances out my lower legs. i love the wash and the generous pockets. i ordered two sizes up but would've been fine with one size up because i'm bottom heavy & tall, but i'll just wash/dry to hopefully shrink them a bit because i love them too much to return! a belt would also work well but i'm actually a fan of how low they sit on my hips (we love body positivity around here) i'm 5'10, fluctuate between 190 and 200 lbs, very full on the bottom (34"ish waist, 47" butt/hips) and these hit just below my calf (perfect cropped length for me)

YES! THEY'RE THAT GOOD!! Just received the pants today, and I'm OBSESSED. These jeans are very close perfection in my opinion! For starters, I love the distressed look without having holes or looking TOO distressed. They look like something that would say Ralph Lauren on the label tbh. The fabric is soft (not a stiff denim), not too thin or too thick, and they have a nice amount of stretch to them. I'm usually a size 10 (but sometimes 12 depending on the brand). For reference, I'm 5'6" and 170 lbs. I ordered a size 12 because I don't like anything tight around my waist and I was concerned with them running small. The 12 fits nicely and I DO think that if I ordered a size 10, they may have pulled around the waist just enough to be able to see some of the white interior of the pockets (I hope that makes sense). The legs have just the right amount of "barrel" to them (not as barrel shaped as FP jeans). These jeans are PERFECT for anyone looking to try out the barrel jeans trend without committing to a full on barrel jean. I'd rate these jeans 10/10 and ended up ordering 2 more in other colors!!! WORTH EVERY PENNY!!!

Cute and comfortable, runs small I had low expectations for the price, but these are surprisingly flattering and comfortable. One caveat is that you need to size up. I usually wear a size 4 jeans so I ordered a size 6. They fit comfortably but are tighter in the waist and hips than my barrel jeans from Anthro. So if you want more of a baggy look at the top, you'll want to go up two sizes. I am 5'2" and the length is just right for me, so for a taller person they might be too short. The fabric is super stretchy and comfortable, color is good. They are cheaper looking than the Antro/Free People versions, but also $125 less. :-) Updated: after a couple of washings and frequent wear, the jeans are pilling. I have never seen denim pill before, so it must be from the quantity of viscose that makes them soft? I'm glad I have a pill shaver but just thought I'd mention it.

Fit well and are very affordable I'm 5'3", 134 lbs, with a 29.5" waist and 40.5" hip measurement. The size 6, 32 inch inseam, feels just right. These fit almost the exact same as the 315 shaping bootcut and they were half the price. One thing I will note is that these came without any tags on them so I suspect that they were returned previously. They don't have signs of use besides that though so I'm comfortable keeping them, especially since they are well priced and fit very well.

As a petite woman standing at 5'3" and weighing 125 lbs, finding jeans that fit well has always been a struggle. But these jeans? They're great, especially for the price point. First and foremost, the fit is good. They hug my curves in all the right places without feeling too tight or restrictive. The stretchy fabric is incredibly comfortable, allowing for easy movement throughout the day. One of my biggest pet peeves with jeans is the dreaded "gap" at the back when I squat down or bend over. But not with these jeans! They stay perfectly in place, leaving no room for any unwanted exposure. If you're a fellow petite woman struggling to find jeans that fit just right, look no further. These jeans are a good choice.

Run big and stretched out a lot I was ready to love these jeans because of the shape and style, but unfortunately I’m stuck with a pair of jeans that stretches out too much and looks too big after just one wear. I’m 5’7, 180lb, 33” waist, 45” hip - based on the size chart first I ordered size 14, they fit somewhat big and didn’t “suck me in” at all, so I exchanged them for a size 12. At first 12 fit pretty well, comfortable, just loose enough but not sloppy or baggy. However after just one wear, they stretched out too much and are not as flattering as I would like them to be. I should have sized down at least to a 10. As far as the quality - these jeans are well made, no complaints. I feel like the color is somewhat different in person, definitely has a “vintage” look to it. Not a deal breaker just a matter of personal preference. Bottom line - if it wasn’t for the sizing, these jeans could have been a good find.

Excellent jeans for pear-shaped figure I love these jeans. Finally a pair of jeans for my pear shaped figure. Some people call it reverse fit, some people call it pear. It is when your waist is smaller than your butt and thighs, mine is 2 inches smaller than most pants that fit on my butt and thighs. I’ve included pictures so you can see how wonderfully they fit all around. They fit so great that I bought another pair. The only thing I would say is I’m 5 feet five and I had to go with the long size. The regular, which I also kept hits me just a little shorter than I like. But it looks cute with a pair of canvas, tennis shoes. I’m 160 pounds and size 12.

Nice fit and quality jeans, follow size chart These straight leg jeans by Levi's are high-quality and flattering. Great fit, and comfortable too. I am 5'7", size 8-10, 152 lb (37"/31"/40"), and ordered these jeans in a size 8 per the size chart. The size 8 fits me perfectly so, if you are between sizes, you may want to size a little bit down. These jeans are definitely high rise and come up all the way to my belly button. They fit me exactly perfectly all the way down: not too tight anywhere, but not baggy and loose either. The fit around the butt area isn't the most flattering, but it does me OK. The length is also perfect on me, perhaps on the long side. I also appreciate that the fabric is substantial and the pockets are real. The front pockets are not very deep, though the back pockets are sufficiently large. The fabric is quite substantial compared to other jeans, but still soft enough not to be irritating. Overall a really nice pair of jeans from Levi's if you're looking for straight leg.

I’m a dude, and these are the perfect 90’s jeans. I just wanna preface this written review and say that yes, I’m a dude, and believe that this is a great 90’s look jeans purchase regardless of your gender. The men’s ones out there are just horrendous and I’m tired of buying and then returning (Amazon and in-store like Levi’s or GAP), and the ones from Temu never fits me because of the horrible sizing. I’m 5’9 with a size 30 waist and an athletic build (I wear size L or XL depending on brand for men’s shirt). After numerous research, I find that there some dudes out there still that still swears by women’s jeans only. Since why not? I used to wear tight skinny women’s jeans back in my emo-phase days in the early 2000’s anyways, so I decided to check out this baggy version that is in-style nowadays as I really like the style. I ordered a large and they fit okay, I could’ve sized down to a medium though, but the large is still good with a belt on. These are stretchy, lightweight, and comfortable. However, just like the women’s jeans back in the day when I used to wear the skinny ones, I’m still disappointed that women’s jeans doesn’t have a big pocket like the men’s jeans do. Why is that? Oof. I wish that would change as some of the ladies out there have these mega tablet-like size phone, and with my iPhone Pro Max, it was sticking out and when I sit down, or shift to a weird angle, they slide off. Same thing for my wallet. Overall though, these are the exact jeans I wanted and highly recommend.

Perfect! I was surprised that these fit as perfectly as they did. Perfect length for sneakers — I’m 5’4” but my legs are kind of long in proportion. Really happy with them! Undecided I'm 5'2" and weigh 138 lbs. My waist is 34" and my hips and thighs are very wide (45" and the widest point). I purchased a size large. I like the way they fit nicely on my waist (not too tight without the weird gap in the back), but they are very tight on my hips, which makes me worry that they will ware or tear in no time. The length is a tad long on my short stature, but manageable. Another reviewer mentioned that these are not meant for pear-shaped ladies, I would agree. I purchased these to try something other than skinny jeans for once. After testing about a half a dozen pairs of jeans, these were the most flattering. I plan to keep these and see how things go.

No stretch but cute fit I'm pear shaped. Hips and butt. Large was snug on my hips. 5'2 144 lbs. Length is perfect if I wear heels. Debating if I should of gone large. I'm afraid if I wash them will shrink.65% cotton. Hmmm. What shall I do?

Versatile jeans, loose legs without being frumpy Great jeans! I’m 5’1” 145lbs so a little stocky, and bought a medium. I thought the waist fit was a tad small but once I wore them a while, it relaxed a little and now fits well - though I probably won’t run them through the drier in case they shrink. I love the high waisted style - the loops are large enough to fit a nice belt and ALL pockets are actually pockets ☺️ I have to hem them a little since jeans are usually long on me which I don’t mind. I like how you can get a more loose flaired end at the bottom or cuff them for a more tapered look (see images). I appreciate the looseness of the legs while still making my behind look good and not frumpy. Just know that these don’t have any stretch to them, and if you have lower belly fat like me, this won’t do a great job of flattening/hiding it.

sizing might be dependent on which color you choose i am 5'11", 145lbs. I wear size 29 pants ~9 in US sizing. if you're tall you know shopping for pants online is always a gamble where more than half might end up as capris or high-waters. I am pleasantly surprised with these. these are the first pair jeans not labeled as TALL that actually make it past my ankles. I think the sizing discrepancies might be dependent on the colour you get. I got large and they are a bit loose instead of being tight like I was expecting from the reviews. material is okay. it's really thin denim so it's very comfortable but I have my doubts if it'll hold up over time. button feels secure, large pockets w really smooth fabric on the inside.

Good Fit! I really enjoyed the fit of these jeans! Although they were oversized, the fit was intentional and true to size. They were a bit long on me as I am 5”2, and had a good amount of stretch to it. Very flattering and ultra comfy!

Baggiest jeans I've worn in a long time, but FUN, received compliments, fairly comfy! I've been wearing skinny jeans and straight leg jeans for a long time, so I decided to 'step out' with these Ripped Baggy Wide Leg Jeans! They are regular denim, non-stretchy. I ordered my usual size 4 and they fit pretty well. For reference, I am 5'7" tall, 130#. The length is perfect, the waist is pretty good (very high-waisted) although a little snug, and the butt fits okay (a little loose). They are baggy and wide from the upper thighs all the way down to the wide bottoms. I'd say the legs are super loose and comfy, but the waist is what is snug since it's so high-waisted. The amount of rips is perfect, and I think necessary to off-set the bagginess and amount (lots) of material that these jeans have. I was able to stay in them all day, although would have been more comfortable if the waist was not so snug. I love the way these jeans look in the front, but do not love the way my butt looks. I guess since there is no design on the back pockets and since the butt is loose, it makes my big butt look big :(. I'm giving these jeans 4 stars as I wish the waist was slightly looser to be more comfortable, but I wish the butt was tighter to look better. Glad to own these fun new jeans though! My teenage daughter saw me wearing these jeans and said, "What are those? You are turning into me!" (And she approved!) Thank you!

Teen daughter likes these My teen daughter is 5’4”, 118 lbs, and has 27.5” waist. She wears size small or 4 pants. She got these jeans in size medium and she’s happy with the fit. The waist is snug but it is not too tight. The hips and legs have a wide relaxed cut. The pants are a bit too long for her to wear with flat shoes so she usually folds them up unless she’s wearing heels, platforms, or bulkier sneakers. There is no fabric content tag. The fabric feels like a quality heavier type of denim with a very small spandex addition. These jeans have a little give but they are not stretchy. The stitching is nice on the exterior and has some small issues on the interior but none are bothersome and don’t compromise the durability of stitching. There was also a piece of plastic foil stitched under the exterior waistband, perhaps a leftover of some kind of a tag but I was able to rip it out.

Great fit, comfortable and teen-approved! I picked these up for my 13yo, who loves things baggy and "cool". They did not disappoint. The fabric is thicker than I expected (yay!! No threadbare jeans!), and the cut seemed to fit her well. They are long on her but as she's only 5'2" tall, I expected that. She usually wears Doc Martens anyway and likes a platform, so I expect the back cuff getting scuffed up well be an asset not a deterrent from wearing them. Well worth the price and they definitely impressed me.

BEST JEANS EVER!!! I absolutely love these jeans. I’d wear them every darn day, if I could!! So comfy and so cute on. I’m super picky about how my jeans fit and I put these Babies on and I was instantly in love!! I’m 5’8 and about 130ish. I got the Med

True to size I’m happy with these. I am 5’3, 115 pounds, 43 years old and the 26 fit perfectly. They were actually surprisingly snug around the abdomen and waist at first, but loosen up just enough as you wear them. The thighs and legs are a roomy cut, so if you like a sleeker look in the thigh, these aren’t it. In some popular mall brands like Madewell, Express etc… a 26 runs large, but these are not sized that way. If you are between, use your larger size . They are flattering and lay flat in the front, despite being button fly. They are a tad long for a short person if wearing with flats. Perfect with heels.

10/10 these are amazing !!! These fit like a glove ! I’m wearing a size 32. LOVE these straight leg / high wasted jeans!! I’m around 125 and 5’6” and I got a size 26, these jeans look great with sneakers and also a small heel/bootie. Since I got these they have become a staple. One of my regulars on rotation.

PERFECT Pull Up Jeans!!! ABSOLUTELY LOVE THESE JEANS! Fits me like a glove and the length is just right. I’m 108lbs 5’3 and I ordered the size 2S. I even bought 3 other colors! However, the Harmony color’s cutting is different and about 2 inches tighter, so I had to exchange the Harmony color with a 4S size. Overall, the cutting is really great, very comfy to wear, and it comes with the front pockets which are hard to find from other jeggings. I no longer need to deal with zips and buttons. Totally satisfied!!!

The waist size was not accurate! Great jeggings. Cute style, cute color and would be a great fit if they were an actual 28in waist, instead of the 30in waist it clearly is. We may try to rebuy them. But unfortunately returns are not hassle free!

Tall girl problems Disappointed but not surprised I am 6'2 and curvy. Finding jeans has been a lifelong journey, I was hoping that these would be long enough because it says long however the length on these jeans are a 32 in and the chart says that long should be 34 in.

Gaping in the waist I had such high hope for these pants. After reading reviews and measuring and comparing to size chart, I ordered the 12 (I’m 5’-2” and 158lbs). They fit perfect in the thigh and calf, I have bigger thighs) but the waist was gaping. I’m afraid that a 10 would be to tight in the thigh area and based on how much these gaped at the waist I think 10’s would gap too.

Great for thick-waisted, perimenopausal/menopause body changes 49yo, 5'3", 185lbs, Style: Sun Worshiper, size 14. I'm presently in the throws of perimenopause and at my heaviest. These are the only jeans that flatten my tummy area, hiding a small apron depending on underwear with minimal muffin top. I'm self conscious about the weight gain and feel most comfortable in these jeans. The inseam sizing is off as I initially tried on 14S but they had an inseam is 27 so I went with 14. I took photos without a shirt for transparency of how they fit on someone thick waisted like myself. With a shirt you wouldn't be able to detect the waistline. The slimming tummy panel hides any fat rippling. These are jean material and have the same weight and durability as a regular pair of jeans versus jeggings. Once I lose weight I will size down as the tighter my midsection gets then the slimming tummy is more effective.

I got mine just delivered and tried it on and so far, I like it. I was looking for some really comfortable pull up jeans that I could slap on the go. My regular jeans were suffocating me so I was really going for comfort. I was scared to get it because there were some splitting reviews. I got the size 4 and inseam 30. I'm 5'4" (163cm) and 130lbs (~60kgs). They are about the perfect fit and size for me, a tad bit loose on the calves so giving a loose skinny look. (I prefer the slightl loose compared to the skin tight jeggings look) They do give me a bit of a muffin top but I don't really mind as long as they don't either slip down as I walk around or squish me breathless. No slipping noted as I walked around. I had on regular skinny jeans right before (wax jeans size 3 - 26" 27" waist) and they are really similar at the waist. Band feels nice and secure and it didn't seem to give me the weird crotch. I will see how it wears after a wash but so far so good.

OMG THESE ARE THE BEST These just arrived today and OMG these pants fit me so perfectly I could cry!!! I have a hard time finding pants as it is because I am 5'5" with short legs so everything is always too long. Add in that my legs are thick and I've got a butt and we have problems. Well, I just had my first baby 4 months ago and have been in this awkward stage of nothing fitting. I've been wearing my maternity pants and compression leggings still because I couldn't find any jeans that fit. UNTIL NOW. These fit me perfectly... Seriously. They are just at my ankle, and having had a c-section I feel supported by the panel insert in the belly area. These are so comfy. I can't tell you how relieved I am that I found these and that they fit me... These may just be my jeans for the rest of my life, I'm definitely buying more pairs!!! I ordered these in black and the weathered denim looking ones and I love them!! I ordered size 10 with 28 inseam. Buy these!!!! Oh if you are reading this and are also post partum, I'm wearing a Maidenform Shaping thong. These are amazing and offer support for the belly postpartum.

Best jeans I have EVER owned! The fit: there is a meshy band (like heavy duty pantyhose) across the belly that completely eradicates my "Mommy" tummy. I have a little pooch, loose jiggle, and sometimes it buldges if the waist line pinches. The waist band on these secures and FIRMS my entire waist - no jiggles or buldges. Then there's my rear, which isn't so perky anymore. These grab my cheeks and pull them up - my butt isnt flat in these. My inner thighs are smoothed out, and cellulite is hidden everywhere.The fabric: they feel high quality, well made. Soft, but durable like a denim/canvas - and they stretch and give when I bend/move without loosing shape or stretching out of shape when I srand back up. I wore these to 3 childrens birthdays in one day and still felt so confident in them to rock to a friends party that night.Size: I am 5"11, 160, bought the black long in a size 10 and they fit great. ps - the long still barely hits my ankle bone, but at least thats better than capri length.These pants are magical and I am SSOO bummed the other colors are out of stock. I did order their recommended similar jeans (skinny, signature, totally shaping) but they zip - dont have waist band and dont lift my rear the same.

Worth the price if you have gut issues Very comfy and worth the price just for the comfort alone. I’ve had multiple abdominal surgeries and it’s not easy to find pants that don’t torture my mid section. I was skeptical of pull on pants but they don’t look “old lady” like I imagined pull on pants would look. Length is right on at ankle and I got a 4 regular. I’m 5’3” and short length is too short. Rise is truly right on as well. I wished there was a little more support in the ab area but these will do! Another thing the front pockets are fake except the coin pocket. Another review had stated all four pockets were functional so I wanted to point that out. These are a much better looking alternative to sweatpants which is what I usually wear around the house. I might just get another pair. Hope this review is .

Unsure I’m still trying to decide how I feel about these pants. The 12s were too big, so I purchased the 10s this time. For the most part, they fit well, though a bit snug in the waist (I do carry most weight in my stomach). They have enough stretch to be comfortable though. The back pockets are big, which would be fine (since I keep my phone in my butt pocket), except they’re big enough that it goes below the bottom of my butt and creates an odd look. I’m certainly not bootylicious, but these pants definitely make my booty look flatter than it is. If you have any chub in your stomach, these pants don’t look great with a tight/fitted shirt - you’ll want to pair with something looser. I have examples of two different shirts of reference. I love doing a French tuck with my tops (tucking the front into my pants) - it looks fine from the front, but from the side, the pants definitely accentuate my stomach pooch. Since measurements are when deciding to buy clothes online, mine are 40-33.5-39, 168 lbs. As you can see from the pictures, they’re definitely a longer pant - I’m just shy of 5’6” and they go just below my ankle bone. Super comfy, but I’m not sure yet if I’ll keep them, since I want to feel confident in whatever I’m wearing for work and these definitely accentuate some flaws and are a bit hard to match tops with.

These Levi’s are great! I’m 5’4”, 165 lbs. I have them in stormy sky color. The fabric feels like great quality jeans material. I wasn’t sure what size/length to purchase, so I ordered 12S, 12M, 14S, and 14 M. When I received them, I only opened and tried on the 12M and 14M. The 12M fit great, but fake front pockets, it’s tag was off and placed into the back pocket. Most likely returned by someone. The 14M fit nicely too, although not as tight/form fitting, and they had functional front pockets and all its tags were attached. Love the color and quality. It’s a dark wash, which I like for the versatility. I will wash in cold and hang dry before wearing them.

Love the Fit: Snug with Stretch I am 5'10" tall and weigh 168 lbs. I purchased a size 12 Tall. It hugs your lower body, yet has stretch and the waist is snug. It was slightly tight and I could have purchased a Size 14. The denim is strong, good quality. I purchased these same pants in twill in 4 other colors in Size 12 and they have more flexibility. I Love Them!

Size chart confusing. A nice fitting pair of jeans, that are comfy are an investment for me. I'm all about quality, fit and comfort with jeans. I'll pay a higher price if need be. I'm somewhat hard to fit and picky when it comes to jeans. These were cute and the reviews were convincing. I'm a true size 4 in jeans and pants. 27 inch waist. I have narrow hips and pants usually creep down on me and I have to crank them as the day goes on, if I don't have a skinny belt on. These have an elastic band, and they feel like the'll stay "put." LOL Pay close attention when you read their sizing chart. Make sure your'e reading the correct chart for sizing. Either skinnys or boot cut, because both charts are different sizing. The first pair I ordered was a size 2 28' waist. Too small! Returned those and ordered a size 4 29" waist and those fit. I received the second pair promptly. I ordered 32" inseam. So, the manufacturer tag is marked 4L/waist 27 L32. Again, this varies from the size chart listed on the add. Other than the confusing sizing, they are cute,fit nice and comfy. The waist is elastic but isn't "overly" stretchy though. Somewhat light weight compared to other jeans, only moderate amount of stretch. Hopefully they won't get too baggy and stretched out the longer the wear time.

EVERYTHING THE REVIEWS SAID AND MORE! LOOK, NO MUFFIN TOP :) I bought these Levi's hoping they were half as good as the reviews said and they Really, Really are! Long story short, I went off my diet and I'm feeling blahhh! I had to go to a school function last night and "be seen"... so I pulled these out of the drawer, took them out of the bag and hoped for the best. I was alittle worried because they have a "pull on" waistband, no zipper or buttons and I thought they might look alittle "old". But as long as you wear a shirt, tunic, sweater that comes down long enough, your good. The inside tummy panel really works great. I went with the 12 Long (5'10 185lbs was 180lbs) and the fit & length are perfect. They're were alittle tight going on and it was a questionable fight for a minute, but I won. I'm wearing the Harmony style.. These hide any bit of muffin top you might have. If your a bigger gal like myself, I highly recommend trying these. They are flattering, the style looks good on and the price is right.The Measurements For 12L:17" Across Waistband,10.5" Front Rise,32" Inseam. They are exactly what I needed. I paired these with a layered tunic and ballet flats and I felt good and was comfortable all night. Definitely Buying More.

Every short girls dream So I hesitated on purchasing jeans online bc im short and feel like my body is an odd shape, but I am so happy with these pants. This is my first size 10 pant which made me frown, but we can thank Covid to that. The pants feel and fit so amazing. Im 5ft2in and these fall at my ankle. I got the shortest inseam which I think was 28 on the options. I weigh 149lbs and intend on losing some wait but I feel like these pants would still be good if I dropped a size or 2 bc of the stretching of the material. Also, I love the dark denim, it doesn't have a weird scent after washing them like some pants do from dye. I am absolutely going to be purchasing a couple others. I do have muscular legs and tend to have issues with pants fitting my quads and these pants just have am overall comfort to them and I'm not constantly tugging my pants up.great quality.

Comfy/Versatile/Attracts Hair & Dust I love these stretchy, pull-on skinny jeans! They're very comfortable, and the short size is a pretty good length for my petite 5'1" height. If I were 1" or 2" taller, they would fit perfectly, but they work totally fine as is. The supportive wide waistband is a wonderful design for tummy flattening. Dealing with a chronic pain illness makes me want to wear comfortable clothes whenever possible. I'm often at home wearing pajamas, but for being out, these jeans are an awesome choice that mix comfort, fashion and efficiency. These jeans are perfect for just about any occasion, whether it be work, play, a date, or anything in between. They're rugged enough for hiking and soft enough for lounging. The fabric is very smooth and stretchy. The only problem is that the material easily attracts lots of fur and dust if you have pets that shed. These jeans aren't the most butt-lifting like most regular tight denim jeans are. They're also not as tight as jeggings although they're still skinny. But these jeans are still very flattering and form-fitting, and you can't beat the comfort level. I like these a lot! I think you will too.

Comfy and soft I like these jeans. They are soft and have the elastic waist band. The waist is higher, so they don't fall down when I bend over or sit. I also like the shorter length. I am 5'1", so it's hard to find jeans that aren't too long! I do feel they are a little on the thin side, but I do not see any wear so far. Buy them!! I can't say enough good things about these jeans! They look and feel like real jeans, not like jeggings. They have a tummy control panel that comes down about 3 inches from the waistband. It completely flattens my fupa and lower stomach pooch!! And I can still comfortably eat in them. I will say, the more booty you have the more difficult it is to pull them up bc of the stomach compression part. I have 42 inch hips and thick, relatively muscular thighs. I got a size 8 in these which is average for me. I'm an 11 in juniors sizes, if that's useful. It takes some effort to get them over my butt, but once I get the compression fabric up over my thighs and butt I only have to do the *jumps while pulling pants up* once to get them the rest of the way up. 😂 The waistband is smooth and invisible under a thin t-shirt, whereas most jeans cause weird lines where the button and zipper are. They're stretchy enough that there's no gap at my lower back. They do have to be pulled up pretty regularly for me when moving around. I think I might also get a pair in size 10 to wear on more active days.

Nice Pants I usually don't leave reviews, but I'm starting to notice that some reviews don't completely help me. So hopefully I can help someone. I am 5'3", 180lbs. I ordered a 10s (short) and it is right below my ankles which is good. The pants itself is soft and comfortable. It does get lint but not too bad. I ordered the 10 because I did gain weight and size 8's have been a little snug, but these are looser than I like but that's not a big deal. I will definitely update the review once I wash these jeans to see if they will shrink. The band rolls down when I sit or bend down but that always happen to me with elastic waisted jeans. The pants sits below my navel, so if your looking for tummy control these are not for you. I personally do like the jeans, I will order more but in a smaller size.

They're ok I'm 5'4 and 120-125 lbs and got the size 2S W26 L29. As far as quality these are great, they seem to be made with good material. I decided to keep them although the length is a little short and the crotch feels weird.

Fit better than the regular Levi pull on. I tried the regular pull on Levi’s in size 10 and 12 and found the 10 too tight and the 12 too baggy. But the way these skinny pull ons are made the 12 are just fine. I get the skinny jeans look I’m wanting without feeling squeezed into the jeans. 5’4 140

Fit better than the regular Levi pull on. I tried the regular pull on Levi’s in size 10 and 12 and found the 10 too tight and the 12 too baggy. But the way these skinny pull ons are made the 12 are just fine. I get the skinny jeans look I’m wanting without feeling squeezed into the jeans. 5’4 140

Great Jeans for the Price I'm 5'5" and 150lb. Short torso and prefer high waist jeans. Usually wear around 10 to 12. Sizing chart for these was so confusing that I ordered an 8, 10 and 12. Went with the 10. Here's why: 8 - I knew by looking that they would not fit 10 - tight around belly, too tight at first. But butt and thighs fit good. 12 - Waist was much more comfortable, but butt and thighs had too much extra fabric. Wore the 10 all day and even went skating in them. Never sagged. Never got the "MC Hammer" crotch. The butt is still looser than I'd like, but it's not saggy. The waist was fine, even with sitting most of the day for work. Did not feel like I I had to pull them up after squatting down and standing up, although I still did this. Probably all jeans will do this unless they're jeggings. Have washed and dried (on low) twice and so far, size has not changed. Gave 4 stars instead of 5 because they just do not hug my little to no butt.

Fit Like a Glove These attract sooooo much dog hair and lint FYI but I like them. Tight in a good way. It’s very hard for me to find pants that fit so I’m shocked I got the size right the first time. It was hard to put on because there’s no button and I have a huge booty but once I squeezed them on they’re perfect. The waste band is a little too tight and uncomfortable to sit in all day at work, but I wouldn’t want to go up a size. Im 5’5” and 145lbs but have very large thighs and butt and legs in general, very small waist comparatively, I ordered a size 6

Sizing is inconsistent These feel great: comfortable, stretchy denim with real front and back pockets. I love that they come in long sizes that are long enough for me (5’10” and 170lbs)! I ordered 2 colors of the same size (10L), and the waist band was vastly different causing a completely different fit between the two. Unfortunately, I need something between them so they’re both going back. Otherwise, they’d be exactly what I’m looking for.

Comfortable but don’t get black if you have animals These pants are comfortable and stretchy. I was surprised by the fabric initially but I ended up liking it. I am 5’ 4”, 125lbs, normally wear size 6/8. I bought the size 8, length 30 and they were loose fitting in the legs but just how I like it around my waist. I could size down but thankfully looser jeans are in! In terms of length they pass my ankles but aren’t too long. I’ll probably get a 28” inseam for a pair to wear with Vans-like shoes. Also you can’t tell in the picture, but they make my butt look great! I bought the black pair and was going to keep them until I spent two minutes in them and realized how much they attract dog/cat hair. I have hardwood floors and a leather couch and yet I was covered in lint and hair. Just not practical for my life.
