Extremely flattering, especially the color/wash I'm 5'8 with longer legs and I have an impossible time finding jeans in a 10 Long which fit me, since my waist is a full 13" smaller than my hips (30"/43"). And I cannot stand it when skinny jeans fall inches above my ankle, it drives me nuts. These gave me that relieving feeling of when a skinny jean just perfectly hits the top of my foot (without being super tight around the ankle) - perfection. Normally I avoid mid-rise jeans but the fact there was a 10L in stock, as well as the price, convinced me to take a chance. I'm so glad I did - these are incredibly flattering! I've never owned pull-on jeans before but that's changing fast now. Also the wash "Immaculate-Waterless" is the absolute perfect color and wash - not too dark, not too light, with just enough whiskering around the areas you need to create a sort of contoured shapely look. I do wish the rise was just one inch higher, but that's probably because my lower waist is quite drastically bigger than my natural waist so they're a tad tight. But I'm sure they'll relax a bit after a few wears.

Excellent for Tall ladies For starters, I’m 5’11, 125lbs, usually wear a 28 in jeans or a 4-8 depending on the brand. I was skeptical about these, I didn’t love the idea of pulling on jeans, however I have been pleasantly surprised. I ordered a size 6, with 32” inseam and they fit PERFECTLY. They are not soft like athleisure leggings, definitely a stretchy denim. They pull on easily, and provide a small amount of stomach support and flattening (but not uncomfortably tight). I have hips, a decent booty and large thighs & calves for my build. It makes finding skinny jeans difficult because they fit in the waist but are too tight on my thighs and claves, but these were comfortable and fit both thighs and calves well, without being too constricting. The other issue I run into as a fairly tall woman, is jeans length. The 32” inseam hit me EXACTLY where they need to, right at my ankle bone. Overall, I love these, and if they sold these in white, I would buy them immediately and they would likely replace my white Paige jeans.

No es alagador. Acabo de recibirlos. Decidí comprar este color porque se ve blanco en las fotos, aunque dice que es lila, así que me esperaba que no fuera del mismo color de la imagen ; además nadie había hecho una reseña para este color. Es cómodo y definitivamente elástico pero no esperes una elasticidad de leggins, estos son JEANS! Pero definitivamente se estiran más que los típicos Jeans que tienen botonadura, pero que también tienen cierta elasticidad (ver foto) No estaba posando en las fotos y me veo más gorda de lo que estoy dispuesta a admitir, pero, no se ven alagadores. Los voy a conservar porque puedo arreglar lo holgado de las piernas. Lo único bueno que puedo decir, es que de la cintura me queda perfecto, pero la cintura no estira casi nada, así que me costó mucho que pasaran por mis caderas y eso que no tengo caderas anchas ni trasero tan grande. Mis medidas (inch): Cintura: 35.5" Caderas: 41.5 Mi altura es 4ft 9ich (Sí, soy casi un pigmeo lol) Así que me quedan largos como se puede ver en las fotos, pero tiene arreglo.

I'm throwing away all other jeans! These are the BEST jeans I have ever owned in my life. I can never find a pair of jeans that fit right -- too big, too small, too long, too low, always falling down etc... These are perfect. I am 5'2" and 135-140lbs. I carry a bit of bulk in my bottom and thighs. I've had 4 kids so have a mama pouch that I needs tamed. I don't tuck shirts in so the elastic waist doesn't bother me at all. It will occasionally roll down in half if Ive been sitting a while but for the most part is high enough to be slimming but not so high that it acts like the bottom part of your bra. Length? I purchased the 4-Short and for someone with short legs, it's a perfect length. This is unheard of for me! The jeans are soft with a perfect amount of stretch. I have gone from hating to wear jeans to now owning 3 of these pairs. If you are a short, curvy lady..... don't hesitate to purchase!!

Shapes You, Yet Still Reasonably Comfortable These jeans fit pretty well and shape you in a flattering way. I tried both the short and medium length and didn't see a big difference, but I'm 5'3." I weigh 128# and ordered the 6 -- I was wondering if I might need an 8. Other reviewers complained that if they went big, the butt was saggy. In my case, the waist is tight, but reasonably comfortable -- same can be said for hips and thighs and basically all the way down to my ankles. The part that bothers me after a long day is actually the crotch! I wonder if this is because I have a larger behind and thighs (you can see in the photo that they pull in the thighs) and would get more crotch room in the 8 without the sag others complained about. I may try an 8 and see. I also have not washed these yet, so if that changes the fit, I'll update.

Love for postpartum Love these jeans! I was looking for an elastic waist jean for postpartum as my pre pregnancy jeans were still a little tight, but going up a size was too big. These are perfect. They are snug in the hips and waist to stay on, have a thick band, but fit perfectly everywhere else. Now that I’m back down to my pre-pregnancy size, these still fit snug and are my favorite jeans. I am normally a size 4 in American eagle jeans, I am 122 lbs and 5’4”. I bought the Size 4, 28 inseam, standard pull on in Harmony-waterless. I want these in every color!

Cute but too short 1 star off for length but otherwise would have been 5 stars. These are so comfortable and cute I just wish they were longer. I am 5'10" and weigh about 155/160 so I ordered the 8 long and while they fit my hips and legs great they are still showing my ankle bone 😭 I am so tired of the short pants look and was hoping these would be longer! I still like everything else about them so I'll keep these and keep looking for something similar but longer. Sorry for the bad lighting in the pictures!

These pants are INCREDIBLE!!!!!!! I never write reviews, but these are easily the best jeans I have ever owned in my life and I had to share!!! They fit perfectly in every dimension to the point where I can't believe these aren't custom made for me. I'm 5'11" medium build so I ordered the 8 long which is my normal jean size. I have them cuffed in the photos so you can't tell, but the length is ideal for my legs which is so rare for me. They are incredibly comfortable without sacrificing the look of real jeans. The tummy panel is FANTASTIC. I'm bloated right now from travel in these photos, but these jeans would never spill that secret. Plus the lack of buttons/zippers makes for a very sleek silhouette. They also fit beautifully around the butt due to the stretchiness of the pants, but aren't so elastic they flatten it! On top of all of that these are more comfortable than my leggings. I'm in love with these pants and will be purchasing the rest of the colors to replace every other pair of jeans I own.

Very comfy! But, will give you that mommy tummy look So these are AWESOME... but mostly when standing. Sitting down, the band really crushes into your abdomen and causes some serious FUPA action. I usually wear the Express perfect lift jeans and don't have this kind of issue. The Express jeans have the thick waistband feel on the entire front to hold your mommy tummy together, but on these only the waistband is thick and holds you together, and the rest of the fabric is thinner, thus the mommy tummy getting pushed out under the waistband for some unattractive bulging. But, very cute on the butt, legs and an overall slim look, just wear with a forgiving shirt if you'll be sitting a lot. I’m a size 8, 5’3” and 145lbs.

Most comfortable stretch jean! Beware these do seem to run big. I really love these Levi stretch jeans. I have other Signature by Levi Straus jeans that I have purchased on Amazon. They all fit amazing. I am always a 10 short so that’s the size I ordered. These are definitely comfortable but are a bit longer in length and not tight around the ankle as shown in picture. I think maybe I could wear an 8 short in this style. I am considering buying another pair in the smaller size because I like this style and I am really curious to know if they run slightly big. Overall, It is a great stretch jean.Update on 11/25/19...I ordered the 8 short and am wearing them today. Not truly happy. I thought sizing down from a 10 to an 8 would reduce the bagginess and that the ankles would have the skinny fit as shown on the model. And no there is no “skinny” to these jeans. I attached a picture of the 8 short to let you see where the are baggy in the thighs and ankle. Don’t get me wrong, these are very comfortable but I wanted the look portrayed on the model. ☹️ And I do love the Levi Strauss& Co. brand.

Great if you get the right size I ordered the black 6M. I’m 5’ 5” and 145-150lbs and 6 months post-second-baby. I’m currently wearing a size 8 or 10 jean. But for some crazy reason I ordered a 6 in these. *face palm* These were way too small for me. Had I ordered my real size I probably would be wearing them right now instead of returning them. So they are made to fit tight and suck you in - almost like compression leggings around the waist. So if you order the right size, these are probably a dream. I didn’t get photos of the ankles, but they are slim, but not TIGHT around the ankles. The material is stretchy but thick. They don’t have the feel or look of leggings. I thought they felt and looked more like black khakis instead of denim. I would say more about the rest of the fit, but it was all pretty bad because of my poor ordering. If I reorder in my size, I’ll update the review.

Buy them! Great pant! Comfortable without being sloppy. I am 5'9 and have butt and thighs for DAYS and a little belly. These pants fit perfectly. I am always nervous to order jeans or anything comparable, especially online but I took a chance and I'm glad I did, they look and feel great! I ordered the longer length and they hit right above my ankle. The waist comes up just past my belly button, it could stand to be a little higher but I could also stand to lose a couple pounds and then it wouldn't matter. I've only been wearing them for about an hour but I haven't had to yank them up yet, which is great news if you're a big booty girl like me. I usually have to yank up jeggings constantly. Nice to find pants that fit nice and aren't leggings or classic jeans. Buy them, you won't regret it!

Beware....Size chart in the description on website does not match the true size. The sizing chart is wildly off from the actual sizing you receive. 18 Long according to the size chart should be Waist: 39 1/4" - 40 1/4" Length: 34" However, you can see that the 18 Long that I received is W 34" and L 32" which is more in line with a size 12 Medium Length on the size chart. That's a big discrepancy. If it was only one size down, I would just exchange them but I don't have the energy to keep ordering until I find the correct size. I'll buy jeans somewhere where I can try them on. What a disappointment!

Love the material & style but sizing is way off To begin with my measurements: hips 47 inches and waist is 37.5 inches. 5'5 feet tall at a whopping 190 lbs. I am boldly sharing my photos of my muffin top with you all. I hope the visual helps xD I ordered size 16 with inseam 30 (no option for short, regular, or long, but would have bought short if prompted). So these clearly ended up far too small. Getting them on was a battle. I had to do a combination of jumping and wiggling to pull them up. Even harder getting them off. I've got size 12 short Maurices jeans that fit better than these (although those are too small as well). Last year I bought size 14 short pull on jeans from Belks that are now too small. I thought maybe I am a size 16 now since I gained weight. I was so hopeful for these. My fiancée doesn't think they're worth returning, since I'm on a weight loss journey, but I wanted jeans that fit now rather than later. I wish I knew what size I need to order, sizing chart appears to be no help. I will say though, the material is very soft and stretchy (I did still get them on haha). I think these could be great with the proper size. I'm not sure in my case if one size higher is best or even two sizes higher.

Nice actually!!! The material is little bit thin, but overall they’re really nice! Very comfortable and actually long enough!! I am 5‘10, 149lbs - and never find any jeggings, leggings or pants that are long enough. I will definitely be buying another pair of these. Very flattering!!

Good fitting jeggings. Look like real jeans. My jeans are all a bit tight after I gained 10 pounds so I have been on the look out for jean-like leggings that didn't look like too much like fake jeans. Not an easy thing to find. These No Nonsense Women's Denim Legging actually look like jeans. They are not thick and stiff like jeans but they have good texture and coloring. I like that, if I roll them up, they have jean-like coloring on the inside so they look very much like a real pair of jeans. I am 5"5", 139 pounds and got size medium. I also tried the size small and they were just too tight around my gut to be comfortable. The mediums fit really well around my stomach, hips, butt, and legs. I do have thin legs so these don't cling super tightly like a pair of high Spandex leggings but that's fine. That makes them look even more like real skinny jeans. The length is good and they seem to be made well. I will probably buy a pair or two more. They're not warm but definitely not see through or flimsy. If they were just a bit thicker for cooler weather, they would be just about perfect. They are the most realistic looking jean leggings I have found so far.

Better than expected! So first off, I weigh 180, 5’6” and usually wear a10/12 in jeans. I ordered a large and they fit perfectly. I was concerned when I read the waist is below the belly button- that was not the case. Waist hit right where it was supposed to. Super comfortable! Color is close to jean material, my hubby thought they were jeans. Only complaint would be I wish they were longer, but I do have long legs so it could just be me. Haven’t washed them repeatedly yet so can’t remark on durability. Bottom line I will be ordering more!

The small is too loose for xsmall wearers I'm 5'2" & at the time of the photos, I was 26 waist 36 hips & around 107 lb. I bought this pair in 2019 & wore it for about 3 years before it became very faded. It is very stretchy, it's flattering except the pockets are kind of narrow & longish? makes your butt look like it is slimmer but longer lool. It's not durable, don't expect to last more than a couple of years, depending on how much you wear & wash it. Back then when I wore this pair the most, I was around 115 - 125 lb & it became my go to pair for everything. I would say, I only recommend this pair for people over 115 pounds, otherwise it would be too lose so if you wear xs, the small will probably be too loose. I hope it helps.

Sturdy, nice dark jegging denim I liked the dark denim. True to size. I’m 5’4, 200+ lbs. This waist band is sturdy but comfy & goes up enough. I liked I could fold at bottom for that casual look. Thick enough not to be flimsy, but thin enough to be comfy. I was cautious & bought 1 for now. I’d buy another👍🏼

Perfect affordable pregnancy pants! These are seriously the best "pants" ever. I usually only wear dresses and leggings (blue jeans are SO uncomfortable), but I wanted something to wear tshirts and sweaters with. I have these in a medium prepregnancy and they fit perfectly (for reference I was between 135-140 pounds, 5'5", and a size 6). I wanted comfortable pants to wear during my pregnancy and even with maternity pants the crotch area would be too tight and cut into me, and with how much my hips spread my thighs felt too restricted (not to mention that maternity clothes cost a small fortune). So I took a gamble and ordered these 2 sizes larger and the XL are PERFECT! (Hoping I can get a little more wear out of them postpartum until I can get my body back) For reference, I am 36 weeks pregnant, 5'5", size Medium in Motherhood Maternity/Old Navy Maternity, and 190 pounds. Most of my pregnancy weight is in my belly and hips. P.S. The length is good too, fits about the same as the mediums. There is a little bunching around my ankles but nothing bothersome, I've worn both tennis shoes and boots comfortably with these.

Get the sizing right! I ordered a large and should have ordered a medium. I am returning these but for nothing that is wrong with the legging itself, just a misjudgment in sizing on my part. I am 5'5 154lbs and usually wear 30/31 or size 10 pants. I have belly and hips but not really any booty or leg meat SO! that being said, I can't speak for other woman in the same height/weight class as I, but for me, these fit loosely with a bit of sag that just didn't look appealing. I think getting the size right makes all the difference in this particular brand of legging, so hopefully this short description and pics will help. No other issues - nice material, very comfortable, the waist didn't pinch the skin, seemed well constructed and I may just order these again except in the right size. ;-)

Happy! I'm happy with this purchase. These are medium weight and don't feel thin or flimsy. Color is classy dark denim. Comfortable! I'm very happy with the value for the price. I am buying these because I need more stretchy clothing for my post partum body for the summer. I think the only thing I might not like is that the waist is rolling down, and then feels a little tight, but I think that's mostly because of my baby belly. I"m 5'-10", 190 lbs, and measure 44" around my butt, and the Large fits perfectly. They are also nice and long! I rolled them up for more of a capri look, but they roll down very long. With no time to shop, I'm turned to amazon for my summer wardrobe, and I'm excited to wear these with some of the cute tops I've purchased on amazon!

Really nice but not a great fit for postpartum These are surprisingly thick for a legging. I wouldn’t be concerned at all about them being transparent. Also I feel like they would be pretty sturdy and warm (once again considering that they are leggings). However I used the size chart and other reviews to determine that I needed a medium. Clearly they are too small for me. I’m 9 weeks postpartum. So a lot of my weight is in my belly. This isn’t high enough or low enough to accommodate my belly. It hits at a terrible spot, I don’t really feel like these are a high rise. Maybe a midrise. They are also extremely tight in the belly and crotch area. I was going to return them but I decided to keep them in hopes they fit when I’m thinner. Currently I am 145 pounds. I’m 5 foot 4 inches. I would say a majority of my excess weight is in my belly. So maybe a large would have been better. I’m really disappointed that they don’t fit now.

It hits at a terrible spot, I don’t really feel like these are a high rise. Maybe a midrise. They are also extremely tight in the belly and crotch area. I was going to return them but I decided to keep them in hopes they fit when I’m thinner. Currently I am 145 pounds. I’m 5 foot 4 inches. I would say a majority of my excess weight is in my belly. So maybe a large would have been better. I’m really disappointed that they don’t fit now.

Worth a try I’m 5’3” and 150 pounds. I got Medium. They are thicker than expected. Definitely leaning on the jean versus legging side. Still have decent stretch. They are a little long on me. Great look and fit I'm 5'8" around 165lbs Was looking for a good comfy pair of jeggings as I've gained around 15lbs. I didn't want to spend too much because I can't wear these to work, so only once a week would I want to wear these casually on my days off. Love the color. The rest of the reviews are correct, they run long, even for me, so I cuffed them at the ends which is fine. They have the appearance of a real jean, strechy, high-waisted and functioning back pockets. Definitely going to buy these in another color. The waist band doesn't roll down like some jeggings and leggings. Fits as expected. I'm a 12 in pants depending on the waist height, so I chose large and they fit great. Hope this helps!

Incorrect sizing chart Sizing is difficult to fully comment on because ... I ordered these based on the sizing chart, XXL indicated as 18 and 20, displayed on Amazon’s product page (image attached). I received an XXL, tried them on, and wondered why the fit was off. The sizing chart that the jeans/leggings come packaged with shows an XXL as 20 and 22. Leggings seem well made, soft jean material, but gathers at the knees. Length is great, I’m 5’ 7”. Waist band falls naturally about an inch below my belly button. These feel like they will slide down if I bend over, maybe my butt’s too big. LOL! If I ordered an XXXL (not available), would fit too large. As a woman, I know that just because these were too snug fitting for me, they may not be for you. But then again, I’m not used to wearing leggings where the material gathers at the knees—not attractive looking. I ordered the dark denim blue. Three images attached.

Comfortable- great for tall people These do have a pretty good jean look and real back pockets are . I find they fit true to the size chart provided. They are very long though. Will be getting more I like the look and feel of these jeggings. Only worn once so I'll see how they hold up after washing but material feels sturdy. I will be hanging them to dry. I'm 5ft 3in and length is good. My weight should be 125 but I have 35 to 40 pounds extra sitting in my gut, butt, and thighs. The jeggings still fit good, about an inch below belly button. Overall I am very happy with purchase and glad I took the chance to try them.

The look of jeans with the comfort of leggings Reading reviews is the best thing you can do when ordering anything online, especially clothing! I'm 5 ft 7 in, 145 pounds, and typically I would purchase Large legggings. However, other users suggested sizing down, saying these run big, and they were right! I ordered a Medium and they fit perfect. They're comfortable and soft, and a bit long, but for me that's fine because I'm pretty tall and will enjoy rolling these up on warmer days to wear as capris. They look very much like denim and for $14, they are a bargain. Much more comfortable than your average skinny jean. All in all, I would recommend these!

False: 100% cotton made in Bangladesh // ONLY 57% cotton, 41% polyester & 2% spandex made in Egypt Pants looked nice online with description 100% cotton, which I was looking for. And made in Bangladesh. The jeans that I got delivered, was ONLY 57% cotton, 41% polyester & 2% spandex made in Egypt. I gave it another try and ordered a replacement and received again ONLY 57% cotton, 41% polyester & 2% spandex made in Egypt. As I called customer service 04/09/24, they said I will get the same if I ask for another replacement. And they will make note of my comment, that it is only 57% cotton. But still - That is clearly FALSE ADVERTISEMENT. See pictures. Be aware.

Comfy! Slightly baggy. I'm 5'3 and 115-117lbs. Very comfortable jeans! I was worried that it would be too long, but apparently the 29" inseam (4-short) is perfect for me. I like that the material isn't suffocatingly thick denim; it's actually pretty thin. I also like that it's cheap!! On their website, Levi jeans are like $50-$110!! I want to buy more pairs and rotate out my 720s lol. They are a bit baggy on me, but I have particularly thin legs haha.

SIZE & MATERIAL ARE PERFECT! Love these! Love the fit. Love these, I hope they don’t ever stop carrying them. I could only find this one pair. Walmart usually carries, but they have been out of mid rise. I have had to buy them one at a time from 3 different sites. What I buy…. “Signature by Levi Strauss” -Size12/MID Rise/Straight Leg/Reg Length- -Also Fabric is awesome-Fit is awesome- **True to size** **Mid Rise/waist line, is the perfect Height for me. No muffin top, and no nipple huggers. **Straight leg is an awesome width. Love that I could find a straight leg that doesn’t flair too much, or drag. **waist =31” **Regular length = 32” ** GREAT FIT** I’m a size 12 weight 167 lbs. 5’6” I am a med bone size, so that is where some of my weight is. I have narrow hips, and a big butt. (which I hate, but that’s genetic. Why in the WORLD would anyone want a large Ass is crazy! But whatever.) **GREAT FABRIC/MATERIAL** Soft but still jeans, and not too tight, loose enough to still wear while hangin’ at home. Usually when I get home, the first thing I take off are my jeans. All my jeans are too, stretchy. Stretchy is great, until they fall down a few inches, after a few hours, like tights do, and I end up having to wear a tight belt. And my regular cotton jeans have no stretch, are a hard & stiff fabric. They fit great until I sit down once, or twice, because reg, non stretch “hard/stuff fabric” , actually just stretch out, and never stretch back. **Stretch But with these Signature jeans the fabric has the perfect amount of stretch, a bit stretchy, and a bit reg jean. Just enough to wear 2x before a wash. Hope you try them. Happy jean hunting.

These do not fit like "skinny jeans". They have a little extra fabric at the knee and ankles. I got a size 7 and maybe could have even sized down, because they have a little stretch. Length runs a big long I ordered these for my daughter who looked them over (before ordering) and she approved. Which was a shocker to me. Teens and pre-teens are so hard to buy for. It's like a game, that I seldom win. We ordered them, and they came pretty quick. These are cotton jeans, which means they are going to shrink unless you hang them to dry. Which turns out is a good thing for us. I ordered a size 2 and should have gone with a 0. She is 5' and weighs about 94#. So heres to shrinking! The material is soft and feels as though they have been worn and washed several times. The color is good too. There won't be any of the breaking in, as the designer worked that into these jeans. She is happy with them, (gasp). She did say if the size would have been correct, she would have worn them straight out of the package, which is big for her. Without her throwing a fit, I am taking these jeans as a win, or as a recommendation.

Great Jeans, Wrong Size. Beware “Amazon Size Recommendation!” Update: Got the right size (25), attached a new image of the fit above. Original review: I couldn’t decide between getting a size 27 or 28, and Amazon’s recommended size was 28. I got the 27 because it would arrive in time for my outing, and it still ended up being way too big (I prefer skinny/bootcut jeans to fit tightly), so the Amazon recc would’ve been way off. The Amazon size recc is based on just your height and weight, not all your measurements, and it messed up another order of Levi’s jeans I got, so I would trust the size guide instead! Other than that, the jeans are amazing. They’re comfortable levels of stretch while still being thick and giving quality, the deep blue color in combination with the contrasting thread in the “Casting Shadows” color is just 🧑‍🍳💋 chefs kiss perfect. Also, in the last image you can see it has the Levis stamp and tag so it’s definitely official, at least my variant! It also comes from the official Levis store and Amazon is strict against impersonators so I don’t think this is fake like that one reviewer claimed. Some Levi’s jeans have a tag that’s just red, without the logo, I own some of those. So yes! Buy it, but make sure to order the right size! My measurements: Height: 5’7 Weight: 120lbs Natural waist: 27 in Belly button: 29 in Hip: 33 in Butt: 37 in

Other jeans I own and fit into are 12-14.

Nice pair of distressed boot cuts for a good price. Not perfect though The first thing I noticed about these jeans (Bb002-blue) were how comfortable they are and how good the rips at the bottom hem will look with heels or boots. Also, how darn baggy they are on me, especially in the thighs. My thighs are only 18” around so I’m used to this. Drying in the machine helped to shrink them a bit and make them wearable for me, although now they’re kinda short. I should have gotten a size 2, but there’s none. For reference, I’m 5’7”, 27”Waist, 34”Hips, usually a 1 or 3 in AE jeans. A comfy fit, the waistband isn’t too tight. But the back pockets are a bit on the large size, making my butt look even smaller. That being said, I’ll mention some other flaws I noticed. Nothing major. The “creases” where the thigh bends are clearly very fake looking. Not like they’re genuinely used and distressed. I was also disappointed to find the style isn’t quite flared. It’s more boot-cut, the flare being overly exaggerated in their pictures. Still, they look okay. They don’t tend to keep their shape either and loosen while wearing (as did all the ae denim). I may need to keep dry-shrinking these, as they do loosen with wear, but they’re coming to fit nicely and I think I’ll add them to my regular jean cycle. Chances are, you’ve got more curves to fill these jeans out, so they’ll fit and look much better than they do on me. That being the case, I’d recommend these for sure, especially at this value.

What's up with the cost difference? I purchased 3 *different* pairs of these jeans. One pair of "Immaculate, One pair of "Janice" and one pair of "Mascara." All in the same size, 16L. All described to be the same cut, "Gold Label" etc. etc.. All you have to do is chose the 'color.' But the price varies wildly. So I assumed something other than the color of the jeans was the basis for this reasoning. Wrong. Apparently the price is soulely based on popularity. Seriously? Please read on, because I do love these jeans & I did get a more than satisfactory response from Amazon. I'm 5'9" and thick in the middle and prefer a little extra length to my jeans. I probably could have gone a size smaller, but I wanted to allow for at least ten percent possible shrinkage due to washing. And I don't want to look ridiculous. The jeans I ordered have slightly different shades of dark blue with gently faded patches. But there really is no other way to tell which pair is which. Remove *all* the tags, and you have 3 pairs of the same jeans on in different shades. When I showed my husband, he didn't notice much of a difference between the pairs either. Just that the 3rd was slightly darker. However all three pairs cost different prices. The most expensive pair being titled "Immaculate." Once the jeans came in the mail and I had tried them all on, surprisingly *they all fit exactly the same.* I have no problem admitting that I'm very happy with all three pairs. But laid out side by side, all 3 pairs of the jeans look *very* similar. Was I sent the same model *twice*, or what? Why is one pair so expensive? No where on the jeans' tags do I see any difference between them other than their scan numbers. They have no 'names' or clear numbers, like my beloved "501's." If I had gone to the mall and picked out the same jeans myself, they would have all been from the same shelf at the same price. They are all the same length, cut, rise, style, and fit exactly the same. The inside and outside labels, text/print and logos are nearly the same as well. And only one pair states "Gold Label" on the outer back leather tag. It's like the one pair of jeans came out a year earlier under a different name - but is otherwise the same in every other way to the other pairs. So I don't get why one pair cost me twenty one dollars, the next pair costing me twenty four dollars, and the last cost a whopping thirty eight dollars? I'm seriously considering returning the most expensive pair/s (which ones though?) and reordering the cheapest pair/s again. Like I said, I *really* like these jeans. They all *fit* the same and fit well and are flattering. But I feel like someone is trying to pull the wool over my eyes here, and I'm really annoyed by the difference in cost because that is the only thing that really differentiates one pair from another, aside from the color. Because if it's color/dye that costs more, that's absurd, and one or more pairs has to go back. In the mean time this whole thing has kept me from actually wearing any of the jeans. So. Which pair or pairs of jeans do I return?!

Reliable Levi's, though the Amazon size listing is confusing I’m 5’3”, 118 lbs and ordered 28 reg based on another pair of Skinny Levi’s I have. Based on the confusing chart for ordering, I wasn’t sure what I would end up with. I got a 28/32 that also says 6 Medium ( guess that translates to the “reg”). I would say the fit in this Vintage pair is comfortable, if not a bit roomy, but at least not tight or restrictive. Length is perfect. I ordered color StayPut, and the actual version is not as faded looking as the pic online, but I like what I got just as much if not better. I really like the flare (ready for a change from the skinny). I also ended up with the actual “vintage” version, which has really cute 3 row stitching on back and front pocket. I noticed at least one review pic had the traditional 2 row Levi stitching, so odd that you don’t know what you’re getting from this particular listing. Anyway, I am very satisfied with my purchase, with a very fair price for new Levi’s.

Garment Shrank 4 inches after washing! These are Misses size 16 Short. I am 5'6" 170lbs. I ordered two pair. I tried one of them on and they fit, except were too big in the back waist (as expected). I usually order short, since that is exactly the right length because I never wear heels with jeans. These were the right length and fit in the leg and all around. I planned to have the waist taken in, but decided to wash one pair, to see what the shrinkage was and whether or not I should wash the other pair before having the alteration done. I thought they might shrink some, but was really floored to see how much they shrank! Now, they are way too short and too tight in the legs and too small all around. I have never had this problem before. I am returning them.

Do everything jean I'll write a review because I know it can be for other ladies on a quest for good jeans. I've probably ordered 10+ pairs on Amazon and these are the winners. My measurements: 28" waist. 37" hip. 30" inseam. About 5'3". 133lbs. Athletic build. 30 yr old mom of a toddler. These jeans stay up without a belt. They are mid rise just below my belly button. I gave up on low rise cut jeans awhile ago because I hate having to yank them up every hour. The Levis website says these are relaxed through the hip and thigh. But if you are an athletic build and occasionally do outdoor chores in these pants, I found that they fit great and still flatter. I dislike skinny jeans because I prefer to wear my pants over my winter/fall boots without looking awkward. These bootcut jeans are a rare find in the skinny jean saturated market. Material is medium weight cotton blend, not paper thin. Decent cold weather jeans. I may wear leggings underneath if the temps drop lower in NH. Hope this helps another lady who needs "do everything" jeans.

SHORT, BIGGER GIRLS—buy these jeans! My body type: 5’ 5” and 242# with short, thick legs and a belly. I’m not familiar with this type of sizing, so I went with a 38 Short (I usually wear a 20 short in “regular” sizes). These jeans feel like they were made just for me! The material is very soft and stretchy, and the bootcut isn’t too flared at the bottom. The front of the pants can be pulled up over my belly without riding too far up in the “front” (if you know what I mean). Also, the back of the jeans doesn’t gap out if you pull them up in the front which I LOVE! I have washed and dried these jeans which made them shrink a bit, but the stretchy fabric allows them to still fit and loosen up after a bit of wearing. Highly recommended for my shorter, bigger girls out there!!!

I can't believe it!!! A pair of jeans that fit with no alterations needed!!! When I find a pair of jeans (or pants in general) that fit, they are usually baggy in the tummy area and fit my butt (or vice-versa) and 9 times out of 10 they are way too long so I end up taking them in to be hemmed. I love wide leg pants and usually I guess they aren't the most flattering on shorter legged people - I am - 36-33-42 and 5'4" - and maybe I'm just kidding myself but I think these jeans look pretty good. And even better, they feel GREAT on. They have a perfect stretch. They fit my butt but aren't too tight - I always have trouble there too. I washed them with darks and tumble dried them and they came out great. The unfinished hem still looks good - no extra fraying or anything like that while being laundered. I think they are fabulous jeans.

Great fitting jeans! The reason I didn’t give these jeans 5 stars is because 1) they decreased the front pocket size and, 2) they understate their inseam by 1.5 inches. I am 5’ 8”, 138 lbs and I have long legs. I need about a 34” inseam if I’m not wearing flats. I originally ordered a 28x34, but the inseam was actually only 32.5”. Today I received the 28x36s and they fit great. I hedged my bets that they, too, would be understated by a full 1.5 inches and I was right. They are actually a 34.5” inseam (not 36”) and perfect with a 1.5” heel. The classic straight leg Wranglers give women a full hand front pocket, which I find respectful. These are only as deep as your fingers.

Decent pair of jeans I am 5’3”, 155lbs. This pair is a size 8 (I am always able to wear a size 8 in Levi Signature - as they all run big IMO). The waist measure 15.5 inches flat, the inseam is 27 inches (ankle length, if you are taller than 5’4 these are gonna be short, almost a perfect length for me at 5’3”), 11.5 inch rise, the leg opening is 14 inches. They are a mid-weight cotton blend with plenty of stretch. The rise is long, maybe a little too long, so the crotch hangs a little low if you don’t keep them pulled up. They are a nice slim straight style, that are a pretty classic cut that will likely always be “in-style” They are comfy. I have washed themed and they really didn’t seam to shrink and they have done a decent job of holding their shape throughout the day. Overall a solid pair of not expensive jeans, great for wearing around the house, but definitely don’t do anything to enhance your figure; like you don’t put them on and think “wow, these look great” it is more like “wow, these are a perfectly serviceable pair of jeans”. I mean for $23 you can’t go wrong really.

Durable. Pretty long. Versatile. Very Skinny Legs. Affordable. Favorite Jeans & affordable! First time ordering jeans online & after I’ve lost 40+ lbs, at that! Loves: I am 5’8” and these go below my ankle, so I can cuff them at my ankle, if I wanted & wear cute bootie shoes. I have SKINNY legs and they are very fitting & flattering! Material, zipper, & button all feel very durable. All 4 pockets are REAL! Also, the price is not bad at all compared to other pairs of jeans on here with much thinner material. Possible inconveniences: Not a lot of stretch in the waist area, but it works. I’m 168-170 lbs and a size 31 was suggested . I got a size 30 because all other stretch jeans I own, usually loosen up throughout the day; idk if these will, honestly. The material is on the thicker side. If my thighs and calves weren’t skinny, these would not fit, so they are SKINNY. I could have felt very comfortable in a 31, but I am expecting to lose additional weight, so these will do, but whatever size is suggested to you, these are true to size in the waist. BUY THEM!

Strange Fit, Baggy & Long My wife is disappointed in the Lee Women’s Sculpting Slim Fit Slim Leg Pull On Jean. They simply don't live up to the "sculpting slim fit” description. If anything, they do the opposite. My wife thought the stretchy waist would be comfy and slimming, but they feel more like maternity pants. They are loose around the abdomen and crotch and the size 8 is way too long on her 5'5 ½" frame. She has to wear heels to keep the hem from dragging. The back fits okay but it's not overly flattering or special in any way. The sizing seems way off. 8 medium is more like a 12 large. No thank you to vanity sizing in this instance. The material is smooth but not breathable. The stitching is decent in some areas but seems messy in other spots -– definitely not refined like lux jeans, so you're getting what you pay for. The overall style is unflattering and makes my wife feel weighed down and uncomfortable. These are jeans she probably won't wear again — she flat out dislikes them on many levels. I would agree that they do not bring out the best in her form and style and I noticed she had to keep pulling them up while we were shopping. ~ Kort

Very comfortable...but they stink! I think I’m giving these a 3.5, I went back and forth with the star rating. These are very comfortable pants. I wasn’t sure whether to go with the 0 or the 2, and the short or the regular. I’m 5’3.5” and my go-to Jean is a size 1 in Celebrity Pink. I ordered the Size 0 Regular. They seemed a tad long for me (I think I like the shorter length on me in tighter jeans) but they were perfect with rolled cuffs. They are definitely more of a relaxed look than I am used to. The legs were looser. I liked the rise and it was high enough to contain everything but I don’t think it gave me a flat butt the way some high-waist jeans do. Great lighter color. I think I would have preferred the shorter length, but with the looseness of the pants I’m not sure that would’ve looked better. So I’m ultimately glad I went with the 0R. If you are on the smaller side of a size 0, you may find these too big, but that’s what free returns are for. The thing is these pants STINK. They smell so bad. It’s like formaldehyde meets moth balls. I thought after a wash the smell would be gone but nope! These still wreaked after a wash. I’m not going to return them and hope that a few more washes will get the smell out, but if you’re sensitive to that, I’d skip these pants.

Super inconsistent sizing! AWFUL quality fabric. UPDATE: ugh. just look at the new photos. Changing to 1-star. Do NOT recommend. After a few months, I’ve found that these jeans are total garbage. They are droopy and unflattering, and the material quality is extremely poor. Even freshly washed, they never un-bag. The elastic in the fabric buckles out or pulls in weird looking strips in multiple places, and it doesn’t even smooth out after putting them on. TERRIBLE! Wish I never bought them, even at my insanely cheap price. Overall: Meh. I would have returned these if they were at what appears to be the very reasonable sounding full price. - Material is very stretchy, but a bit thin. Nice for summer, but you can catch some VPL even through the dark wash. - Inseam length runs short. I wear flats and almost always have to get short lengths for a 30" inseam, but the short of these hit in an awkward spot above my ankle bone. - The listing didn't specify, but these are pretty high cut. They hit right at my belly button. - Butt pockets are higher than I expected, but not too 90s looking. I bought a pair of these in 2 short. Initial fit seemed just okay, but for the very low price I decided to grab a second identical pair in case I ended up loving them after a few wears. Turns out... They are clearance priced for a reason! In the photos, you can see two pairs of the same jean- same exact size, same exact color, but cut at least a size differently. The waists have about 1-2" difference in diameter! Luckily, the material is stretchy enough that the cut too small jeans are just good enough. And the cut is high enough that cut large ones are also just good enough. Neither are great, but for the price I found them at, I'll keep them while I wait for my normal jeans to go on sale (AE jeggings).

Misleading Color I ordered the “Blue” color version of these jeans in size 12 as recommended. I’m 5’2 & as you can see in my photos, the crotch area is baggy/big, as well as it’s a bit too tall for me. The photo in the advertisement is a much softer blue toned than what I received. The blue denim I received is not only darker but the acid wash effect is also harsh. I think the seller may have sent me the “N Blue” pants because the blue denim advertised, doesn’t have the acid wash design in the back. The placement & harshness of the acid design looks terrible as if someone diy’d it. The only positive thing I can say is that the denim material itself is soft which is comfortable for wear. Overall I’m not sure if this was false advertising or a clerical error of being sent the wrong item but I’ll be returning these pants. I’ve lost faith in opting for an exchange because who’s to say any of the jeans are the correct style/color of what’s advertised here. Not worth $40 gamble.

Baggy fit perfection (edited review) Edited review: Scratch the sizing comment in my original review. It took me a few try-ons and changes to love that I got them so baggy. Coming from skinny jeans it took me some time to figure out why I wasn’t crazy about them. Decided it was the color so I soaked them in a bleach solution to lighten. Then I added more rips and washed and dried these a couple of times and I flipping love them. Definitely going to order the lightest color offered next. They’re so comfy and I get a lot of compliments when I wear them. As a 40-something this style takes me back to being a teenager and I forgot how great it feels to be comfortable in jeans and not dying to peel them off at the end of a day. Original review: Ordered 2 sizes up so they’d be baggier on me and I should have probably just got one size up. Wear a 10 in American eagle and got the 14. The material is soft and on the thinner side which I like. The rips are perfection too. The vertical seam that goes down the backside/butt is crooked and sort of drives me crazy when I look back in the mirror but overall these are nice jeans. Super soft and stretchy; VERY high-waisted I'm a straight-leg girl, but I liked the look of these online so I thought I'd give them a try. I actually like the way they look and feel overall. The flares balance my broad shoulders nicely...they definitely do away with that top-heavy look that skinny jeans and a blousy top can cause (I'm an inverted triangle shape, so leggings and skinnies can make me look like a chicken if I'm not careful lol). They're incredibly stretchy and soft, and I was shocked to find them long enough for my 33.5" inseam (I NEVER find jeans long enough unless I order tall, and even then they're sometimes only a 32"). The length alone made me celebrate, and I can actually wear them with heels! My only issue with them is that I am short-waisted, even though I'm tall, and these are not made for a short torso. They button at my ribs, which I kind of hate. It feels suffocating. I typically buy mid-rise jeans (I like them under my belly button an inch or so), so getting used to these literally poking the bottom of my breasts when I sit down will be a whole thing. They're ok when standing (no muffin top!), but sitting feels super...awkward. I don't think these will replace my usual Joe's jeans, but I enjoyed wearing them for a night out when we were in Vegas, and they were quite comfy. They'll be nice for occasional wear, and are a great compromise between slacks and jeans when it comes to dressing for those occasions in which you're unsure of the dress code. I ordered a 14 (I usually wear a 31 or 32 jeans) because I have a bigger booty, but these have so much stretch that sizing up was unnecessary. They're quite roomy in the waist...I think a 10 or 12 would probably have been better (if I reordered, I would go with the 10). They work, though, because they're so soft and stretchy. I would recommend these as long as the uber high waist doesn't bother you. If you're used to skinnies, these still have that soft feel, so they're a good transitional option before you dive into a pair of stiff, high waisted Levi's. I washed in cold and dried on medium. They did shrink a bit (which is what I was going for when I put them in the dryer). Now I would probably just hang them to dry.

Never had pants be so thick & high quality material & still this stretchy!!! I have trouble with pants because I have wide set hips, a tummy, and Itty bitty chicken legs with no butt. These are a great solution because they're so stretchy!!! They fit comfortably around waist but aren't super baggy in the butt and legs. Still a bit bigger in those areas than I'd like but in a not quite tight enough way not a baggy way. They also cover my ankles even though they're high wasted and pulled up past my belly button! I'm almost 5'10 so pants always look like high waters on me. The most impressive thing about these was the fabric quality. Normally if I find jeans with amazing stretch like this then they're super super thin or like jegings. It doesn't bother me I like thin pants but then they wear and tear very fast. These are very thick sturdy durable denimaterial but SO stretchy and comfortable!!! An awesome pair of flattering jeans! Definitely a good order!
Perfect fit and length! Perfect length for me (5’3) they fit in the waist hood and thighs despite my height which I loved!! Washed well!

SO FLATTERING These fit me perfectly. I’m obsessed. Size 10 or 12 pants, usually. About 170lbs and 5’6” and got the 30 Regular. These Levi’s have such a nice height on the waist. Perfectly covers and shapes the pelvis and tummy and lands right at juuuust above the navel on me. That area of denim is shaping as well as the rear. Kinda cinches at the natural waist and a bit more under the rump than other styles. At the waist, there’s almost a V-shaped waistline and I think that also aids in concealing the love handles area. The denim is very sturdy and while they’re not stretchy like some other cheap brands, they will conform to your body and have just enough give as to not be constricting. I got some kind of light indigo color - it’s pretty and would probably go with any top or shoe style. I love how the ankle isn’t suffocating like traditional skinny jeans! I wanted a tighter leg but tiny bit of give at the ankle (unlike the “straight” styles with is way too loose) and these fit the bill. The regular in a 30 is 30 for length as well and is AMAZING on me. Not too short at all. Front pockets are pretty short and useless but I don’t care the rest is a dream! These can be worn dressed up or down.

Love the flattering cut. But sizing information listing is misleading Got a 24 regular. I checked the size guide for inseam before making the ordering; listed as small, medium, long. Wished I have the legs for 34 inseam.

LOVE these jeggings! I am 5'9" and have longer legs so finding jeans that fit AND are long enough has always been a challenge. I have already ordered another pair and will stalk...I mean patiently wait for the black version in my size to restock! And you cannot beat this price!

I like to read reviews before buying an item because I am 6' tall which makes it difficult to shop for clothes. To be honest, I forgot what size I got but I think it was 10-long. They are not long to my standards - on the photo you can see that "high waist" is not up to the waist and leg length is barely enough to look ok when walking. It looks too short when sitting (also on the photo). I like the material, so that's a plus. Pants are super hard to put on because they are tight in the waist area and I barely squeeze it through my hips. Also, as a side note - while I am tall, I think I have a very proportionate body, so my legs aren't long compared to the rest of my body. I am keeping them and might even get another pare when this one is done, but there are things that I wish were better :)

Long-Legged Luxury: Perfect White Jeggings for Tall Travelers!" Finding the right fit for jeggings can be a tall order when you are 5'9" with long legs, but these white jeggings in size 6 Long were a fabulous find! I took them on a trip to Italy in March, and they were a dream come true. Not only did they fit my long legs perfectly, but they were also incredibly comfortable and flattering, making them the ideal travel companion. Worn on the stunning Amalfi Coast, they matched the beauty of the surroundings and handled everything from long walks to leisurely dinners. The jeggings maintained their shape and comfort throughout the trip, proving to be both durable and stylish. For anyone looking for great-fitting, chic, and comfortable jeggings that cater to taller frames, I can’t recommend these enough!

First thing I absolutely loved is the color, exactly as pictured. They material is soft yet sturdy and the stretch is perfect and a nice snug..(I feel cute) fit. The waistband is super confortable.. not like a regular jean and it doesn't cut Into your waist. Important if you have a a belly and pouch like me. The length is perfect all the way down to my ankle. There was no weird smells. When I sit or bend down my backside business stays my business. They feel almost like a second skin like I could wear them all day and not even think about it. I would definitely recommend. Love the price too as I recently had gastric sleeve and am having to buy others more often I definitely will buy these at the next size down. Overall great buy for the money. Very satisfied!!

LOVE THESE I am middle-aged and all my 9/10 Old Navy jeans are too tight. I have extra weight around hips/tummy that I want to loose (hopefully), and I am not ready to give up. So meanwhile, I thought I would try a size 10 Long in these no-zip Amazon jeans. I am 5'7", and as you can see from the full leg picture, maybe I did not need the "Long". But I just now pulled them on and have not washed them yet. I would rather have a little bit of bagginess by the knees then too short at ankles. I am very satisfied with the feel and fit. It is a decent thickness of fabric --not super thin, but not thick and heavy. These are very comfortable everywhere on my crotch and legs, including the waist. The waist band seems midwaist and sits about 1.5 in below navel. Not highrise or lowrise or hipsters, etc. Some of my belly is inside the jeans, some above, but nothing feels constricted. Snug but not too much. Super comfortble when sitting and bending/squatting and my crack does not show. Hope this review helps you. I would try them out at least, since Amazon Prime gives you time to try them within the return window. I only bought one pair for now, but wow! I am excited about these. Have a blessed day.🙏💖

Color Unexpected. Otherwise Nice The color is described initially as Blush Pink. For reference I have photographed the pants on a Blush Pink quilt. Not even close to a Blush Pink. In ordering, the color is described as Coral Pink. Closer, but I would say Salmon Pink is a correct descriptor. It’s okay, Salmon Pink will work into the autumn, so maybe it’s even better. But I did order three tops to go with Blush Pink pants, and thus is disappointing. I took a star off for that reason. I ordered size 12 Long and the fit is perfect (5’7” 163 lb). It’s a thicker denim than in other leggings from Amazon Essentials, more like regular denim. Still has stretch. I think I will get a lot of wear from them.

Perfect for postpartum jeans I really like these right out of the bag. I had two babies back-to-Back and I have DR and lots of skin. The waist band on these are gentle and feel forgiving when I sit and stand. The butt fits great! Now I’m 5’7 and ordered a tall so that I can fold the bottom for cuffs and these actually have a normal length, but it also gave me a longer hip and thigh which I LOVE. It’s been why I order long pants. The demon is a great color. Need to wash before wearing because it smells like manufacturing and it’s mildly irritating to the skin. The denim is thin and has the perfect amount of stretch. I would buy these in every color! 10/10 would recommend

Not bad but I can't seem to get them in black. I purchased these pants in camel and white. I am 5'10" 222lbs and I bought them in xxl long. I love the length. I think the colors are true to pictured. The quality seems good. As you can see, I carry a lot of my weight around my middle. These pants have a wide waistband that helps suck me in a little. My only complaint is that, even with my big middle, the waist could be a little with belt loops added. They stretch out a little after wearing. I've tried to order them in black but twice I received a different pant, one being a Levi's brand, neither did I like. I'd really like to have the same pant in black. They're comfortable pants. I like them.

Stretchy yet firm I'm 5'3" 172 lbs and wear a L in most things. I have short legs so I purchased these in a 12 short. They are slightly snug at the waist, but too much looser and I'd worry about them sliding down. So far they work for me. I haven't noticed any sliding yet and the stretchiness is forgiving yet firm, so I'm not too concerned about the knees getting baggy. The back pockets are real, but not the front. I would definitely recommend these. Hopefully I still like these at the end of the day. There was a weird spot on the waist band where the stitching was done over several times. It's pretty itchy right now, maybe I'll just get used to it. Or maybe it rubs my stomach raw and I curse Amazon as I'm putting bandaids on the spot. only time will tell.

comfortable & attractive pull-on jeans, good value for the price The fabric these jeggings are made of is thick enough to look like real jeans, but just a soft and stretchy as leggings. Way better quality than most jeggings. I got the black and the dark wash and I really like both of them. I love having no button to cut into my tummy when I sit, or bulky zipper under my shirts and sweaters. I'm 5'2" and 110lb and the 0 fits snugly but isn't too tight to sit cross legged. The 0 is likely long enough to cover your ankles if you are up to 5'6" or so, but for shorter legs it's easy to just tuck the extra fabric under for a hemmed look. These are likely to become my go-to jeans to wear with sneakers or boots. I wish they made them in boot cut, too!

Good fit and comfortable, but created a muffin top I ordered the short size 12 and they are good but not the ideal jegging for me but still worth keeping. I have taken two photos with two different shirts and you can see that with the loose shirt, it camouflages my waist area. With the tighter shirt, it created an unflattering fit to see my fat bulging from the tight waistband. Ideally a looser band would be preferred, but I have a difficult body to fit jeans and pants since I am 5’3” and I have more of a “gut” than butt. So to get pants to fit in the butt, I have to order a size down but then it creates a tight waist and sometimes a muffin top and this is exactly what these jeggings did for me. But other than that, the length is great, the material nice and stretchy while still looking like jeans, they are comfy, and the waist band is still comfortable, despite the muffin top effect. I think I’m going to keep and just wear with looser shirts.

I see other reviews where the buttons fell off, etc. So far my buttons are on just fine. I love the high waist, it prevents love handles and these will look good with just about any top-T-shirt, blouse, turtlenecks, or any kind of shoe or boot. In photos I am wearing low kitten heels with black tights. They arrived nicely packaged and quickly after ordering. Looking forward to loving them a long time.

Beyond Flattering Let me lay out my body negatives so you know just how awesome these wide leg jeans are. I'm 5'3 weighing 180lbs, short legs & short torso. I have a solid athletic build with a firm muffin-top and small fupa. I carry most of my weight in my stomach and breast. I'm never without shapewear on; most times two of them on. I purchased a medium in these, the perfect boot cut denim stretch jeans, and boy are they comfortable. At first when I pulled them from the package, the waist looked really small, esp in comparison to the nice wide leg and ample bottom part. So of course I tried them on without shapewear just to see how much stretch was in the waist part. I was able to button them, but not comfortably. I knew they would be perfect with my shapewear on. I ordered the 'wased black'; I love the faded look. The stretch on these jeans is a nice firm stretch. And because they are not really denim material, they are not heavy like denim, but sturdy enough to still feel like a great pair of jeans. The best part, they got my booty looking great. I absolutely love these jeans, so I plan on getting two more pairs.

I liked these and was happy I had ordered the XS assuming they would be the winner or so I thought….(XS) Always Black, when I tried these, the fit ok except that the fabric felt thinner and the color had a slightly washed look to them and then the length was shorter than all of them! UGH!!! So frustrating!All sizes were shorter than the stated 32”. They varied between 30 and 31. So bummed because I liked them for the most part. I could accept the lesser quality if I could find one that had the qualities of three put together fabric of the ice blue, the smaller size and color of the Always black and then the right length of 32"! LOLMy favorite fabric and fit were the ice blue. Always black my favorite color, so I will try one more time and order XS 32” inseam Ice Blue and Always black and pray that’s what I get. I’ll try to update.****UPDATE:Ended up order the “Always Black” only in XS/32”So disappointing and frustrating. What I got was extreme faded black looked like they had been washed and again the inseam was not 32”! The last 3 photos are the newest pair! I mean come on!😞

These jeans are amazing! These jeans are very comfortable AND so cute and stylish! They have the perfect amount of stretch, and a nice high waist that isn’t too high. The wider hem makes them look high end and a bit more like a trouser jean. I have these in 2 colors now! I’m 59-years-old, 5’5” and 165 lbs -normally a size 10/12 the L with 31” inseam fits perfectly.

Good fit lightweight denim When my husband and I sold everything we owned & moved to Mexico, I packed on almost 30 lbs. We overindulged in all the delicious food here, and stayed in Airbnbs for months with no access to a gym. As a result, none of my clothes fit anymore, so I needed something I could wear that was at least as flattering as possible given the current state of my body. Since my size 10 jeans were too tight, I decided to get a 12 in these. In the photos, I weighed about 180 lbs, 37 waist , 42.5 hips and they were the tiniest bit snug, but the spandex in the denim allowed me to confirm to my body instead of giving me muffin top. Since I'm 5'7" with long legs, I ordered the 32 inch inseam. The length is perfect for a low wedge heel on me. I love that these jeans are lightweight with some stretch. So much better than the stiff denim from the old days (I was born in the 70s, raised in the 80s). The denim back then was thick and so stiff if you gained an ounce jeans weren't wearable anymore, and even if you could squeeze into them, they dug into your skin and left indentions and red marks. This more modern, stretch denim is so much more comfortable to wear, and the lighter weight fabric is more bearable in hot climates like Mexico.

Keeping but there are things you should know I read all the reviews and went into this knowing that these are not high quality. What gave me hope was the curvy girls I saw that loved them. For reference I'm 5'5", 36/27/40 measurements, 138lb. The good: I don't have a gap in the back. (this never happens!) The length is perfect. (also never happens) They are a little stiff but mostly comfortable. My thighs and butt have room to breathe! The bad: The distressed look on these is so tacky. I'll try to mute it by using dye. The seams are awful and could need reinforcement in stress areas. Look at the belt loop placement. Couldn't be bothered to make them symmetrical. The hardware is cheap looking and you'll need to rub a wax candle on the zipper to prevent sticking. Overall if you're athletic build/curvy these are so worth it for the cut alone. I very rarely find pants that fit me. I want to cry. When these inevitably fall apart, I'm going to take them apart and sew a higher quality copy of them with fabric that has stretch. This is such a relief after years of skinny jeans and manufacturers that pretended we didn't have bones, muscle, and fat on our bodies. With those very human things, you can also have a small waist! I'm mostly happy and glad I bought them. Took two stars off because they won't last long and I shouldn't have to dye them and resew the belt loops right out of the box.

Shorter inseam than advertised Unfortunately, I really do need every bit of the 32 inch inseam that was advertised. I will be sending these back as they do it just a little bit too short for flats. Would be ok with tennis shoes but that’s not why I needed them. I also feel like the hemming at the bottom was shorter than shown but that’s just my impression. They are very stretchy and would be comfortable to wear, just not long enough for me.

3 1/2 stars These jeans fall between 3 and 4 stars and I don't think I would buy again. The good: The fit is great. I am 5' 7" 135lbs and normally wear either a 4 or 6 in other brands. The small in these fit nicely. They are a little tighter around my waist than I'd like, but I do have a pretty big waist-to-hip ratio (menopause belly!) However, they are stretchy so are wearable. The length is perfect on me and they are wide in the leg without being super-wide and baggy like some brands I've tried. The bad: The denim is low quality and the fabric is stiff. I have washed and steam-ironed them to soften them up a bit but they are still pretty stiff. Also, on the pair I received, the distressing is uneven. I ordered the deep blue color and there were three parallel white lines that looked like scratch marks on one leg. Luckily, I have a deep blue felt pen so I lightly touched up the white lines and now they aren't visible. I am sure I will wear these but they won't be my go-to jeans.

Fitted and stretchy I really like the cut of this jacket. I am 5’8/235 and got a size XL. It hits just below the waistline and can button up if needed. It has a snug fit and wouldn’t work with anything thicker and T-shirt material - very tight in the upper arm area. I like the stretchiness of it, and the pockets are deep enough for a phone and keys. The fabric is super soft, but does show every little detail if you spill or get anything on it (including streaking from laundry detergent, which you can see in these photos). I agree that it has a strong smell out of the package, but washing it took care of that. It works well for fall weather, not thick enough to replace a jacket though.

I needed a new denim jacket to fit my current size. I liked this darker wash color and classic look. It is important to measure your bust, waist, and hips to determine your size; don't go off the standard sizes you buy in the stores. I saw some reviews from women who also bought an XL and claimed it was too small; and although their weight was slightly less than mine, they were also much shorter than me. I am an athletic body type with broad shoulders, but still have womanly thickness. In the photos I have on a wireless bra, but I dont think a push up would fill in the chest area too much more (38/40 C). Your true measurements are the key. If you get your correct size, your abdomen will have a straight line silhouette (like in my photo), but if you want to show your curves, a size down may do the trick. If you are hoping to wear thicker shirts or sweaters with the jacket, I suggest sizing up one or two sizes, because the arms are a form fit. The denim is light weight (perfect for TX), but not thin (great for "fall" and "winter" we have in our state). It has enough stretch in the fabric that you can unbutton the sleeve cuffs and roll them up as I did in my photos. My hips are wide, so I can only button the bottom button and the jacket still look decent. But I only want to wear it open anyway. Overall, this is just what I wanted and I am very pleased! I'm excited for the weather to cool off some more so I can wear it. If I find issues with the stitching or quality, or have issues after a wash, I will update my review.

A Solid Buy! I was looking for a more fitted jacket to wear with my knit dress. I am very pleased with this. It’s soft, stretchy, and buttery smooth. Perfect jacket 🧥❤️ I think as debating between a large and a medium just because i have gained a lot of weight in the last couple of months. I decided to go with the medium and it fit perfect! I bought the darker color in Medium and lighter color in Small, and ultimately decided to return them both just because I thought the way they flared right at the lower band was unflattering on my already bottom-heavy figure. My measurements are 35-29-39, and you can see from the pictures that both S and M fit me, although M would be better for layering over anything long sleeved or thick, while S would be a good fit for layering over a sundress or tank top.

The Levi's jacket is cut narrow in the shoulders, and has a more boxy shape. This one is broader in the shoulders and has a more tapered shape. The fabric is decently stretchy, and the jacket just molds to your body like a good-fitting pair of jeans. Looks super cute with jeans or a skirt and boots. The only negative I can think of is that the front pockets are seriously tiny, so small that my keys barely fit in there. (There are 2 inner phone pockets that are great. Shorter than I expected I have a lot of pairs of leggings and joggers from this brand and I've always been impressed that they come all the way down to my ankles, because I'm 5'10" and have trouble finding pants that are long enough. So I assumed these would fit similar to the same brand and size of leggings and joggers, but they're a full 2 inches shorter, as you can see in the photo. If you're average height or shorter they'll probably be fine, but I will not be purchasing this style again. Other than the length I like them. I'm keeping them because I got them for a Halloween costume and don't have time to replace them.

the olive ones are reversible! (pics) I got the olive ones mainly because in the ad photo they looked like they had a subtle vertical stripe texture. In person, they do have that texture and it's very pretty, enhancing a long silhouette. But guess what? As I removed them I saw that they have more pronounced striping of varying widths inside. So I turned them inside out and put them on, AND THEY ARE SO CUTE! Yes, I'm totally going to wear them this way. The details from the outside of the pants (stitching around real back and faux front pockets, waistband, fly, rivets) all show on the inside as well, I honestly don't think anyone could tell they aren't supposed to be worn inside out. OMG, are other colors of this legging striped inside? I have been looking everywhere for striped leggings. Most designers seem to think that legging stripes should be horizontal--you know, so that you look as much like a chubby toddler as possible. OK, so, standard features that other people who aren't as stripe-obsessed as I am might care about: 75% cotton very long-- I got the One Size; I'm 5'8", curvy muscular 140lbs, but there is plenty of length, scrunching at my ankles beautiful shading so that it's not just a bland olive smooth- and sleek-looking super-soft and comfortable stylish design great value for the price did I mention that they're striped on the inside and reversible?!

Cute and Great Fit For sizing reference: I'm 5'5, 135lbs, 28in. waist, 40in. hips, approx size 6 pants and I ordered the OneSize. I didn't know what to expect for this cheap price, but so far I'd say I'm pleasantly surprised! I have a booty and I often have the "waist gap" problem with pants, but since these have the elastic waistband and are stretchy overall, they fit perfectly everywhere, including the waist. The material is neither too thin or thick and the pants seem flattering on me. I can definitely see myself getting more colors in the future, in both capri and legging lengths. I do agree with another reviewer that there can be a tendency to create a "camel toe", however, so that is an area for improvement.

Sizing inconsistent! I purchased these in Ellis, Black, and Seattle, all size 6 Medium. First off, they're not very high rise- more like a mid-rise. My Levi's all have a much higher rise, so if you're looking for a true high rise, look elsewhere. Secondly, you're going to want to order a few different sizes bc they're pretty inconsistent. Cute jeans I ordered these as a loose-fitting pair of jeans so I could wear to work comfortably. They fit the bill. They’re nice and loose in the thighs. I finally found some black levis that fit. These are the blackest black levis I have ever seen. They are soft and stretchy and they fit great (My butt is lifted up). As I mentioned in another review (Levis 311 skinny plus, kabekona camo), I currently wear an 18 in Womens Levis 505 which run larger than most levis so I sized up to a size 22 to be safe. However, I did not have to hold my breath to button them or wiggle into them, they just slid on, so I'm happy. I will also have to wear a belt with these which is always the case for me. For reference: I am 5"5 with thick hips, butt, and thighs and currently weigh around 260 and my stomach is not flat but it is smaller in proportion to the rest of my body.

True to size, stretchy, size down if you are between sizes. long leg friendly I have been looking for some cropped wide leg jeans for a while and haven’t liked any of them. This is the first pair that I think works on someone with long legs, I typically wear a 34 inch inseam so some of the other cropped jeans look like an odd length on me… These hit in a better place. The fit is true to size, obviously I was hesitant to buy jeans that are not a numbered size, but size small S works for me. The denim does have a good amount of stretch though so if you are in between sizes, I would recommend sizing down because these have a good amount of give. The material of these jeans is a medium weight denim not too heavy, but not too thin, with stretch. This is good for summertime when you are on the go, comfy enough to squat down in. I would definitely recommend these, See photos for an idea of the fit. hope this helps you!

Not 100% cotton I'm 5'3", 152 lbs, 32" waist, 39" hips, and wearing a size 31. The waistband is about an inch below my belly button; to me, this is on the lower end of “mid rise”. The widest part of my calf is 15", and I found it a bit snug in that area for a bootleg. These were my least favorite of the six I ordered under Prime Try Before You Buy. All jeans I selected were (supposed to be) 98%-100% cotton, and these were described as 100%. However, they are actually 93% cotton, 6% polyester, and 1% elastane, which makes them an automatic fail on that front alone. After losing 125 lbs, I’ve found stretchy jeans highlight, rather than conceal, the loose skin on my thighs. While they aren’t as bad as those with a higher poly content, they can’t compare to the 98%-100% cotton jeans, and thus, are going back. The jeans I tried were: Levi’s Women’s Premium 501 Original Fit Levi’s Women’s 501 Original Fit Gap Women’s High Rise Straight Fit Wrangler Women’s Cowboy Cut High Rise Slim Fit Tapered Leg Wrangler Women’s Real Wrangler Classic Fit Mid Rise Boot Cut Silver Jeans Co. Women’s Suki Mid Rise Curvy Fit Slim Bootcut (Amazon won't allow me to post with hyperlinks to each item)

Love These I was really nervous about buying such a baggy style jean with my body type. I’m 5’0” and about 155lbs, with a curvy, athletic build. Difference between pure and non-pure versions I ordered these jeans in a size 9 in both Amal and Amal Pure colors. I already ordered the jeans in a size 9 in the color Lake and loved how soft the material is. I wanted a lighter color and chose the two Amal colors to compare which one I like better. Both of them have too much fading for my liking so I returned both. Also, the stitching is a bit off and is more noticeable on these jeans than on the Lake pair. The Amal color has the wallflower logo on the back and stitched within while the Amal Pure does not. The Amal Pure has a plain black tag within and less decorative back pockets. Also, the Amal Pure has tiny front pockets while the Amal has more normal sized front pockets that you can actually fit your hands deeper inside. The Amal color jeans are slightly lighter than the Amal Pure. In my pictures with them side by side, Amal is on the left and Amal Pure is on the right. In terms of fit, I am 5'4", 140ish lbs, 38" hips, 29" waist. I usually wear a size 7 in juniors and a 4 or 6 in women's and the size 7 in these jeans was a tad too tight so I kept the 9 in Lake. I love how soft these are but they do slide down when walking around extensively or when getting up from sitting. They are more like leggings than jeans. UPDATE: I wrote my original review in Fall 2018. It is now July 2020 and Wallflower instasoft jeans are the only jeans I wear since they are soft and stretchy and don't bother my sensitive skin. I still have my original Lake and they are a little less dark but still in great shape. I also have and wear regularly Shannon and Hayden all in a size 9. The Lake seems a little tighter than the other two but all are softer than normal jeans. I always wear these with a belt since they are super stretchy. But overall, they have held up to constant wear and look great which is surprising since the material is thin and my thighs rub.
