You may consider sizing up due to waist Update: I originally wrote my review saying I didn't like how these look but I have changed my mind! I have them on with a cute summery top and some sandals and I have to say I like the way they look! Cute, casual, something I'd wear to coffee or for errands--which, I am about to run errands now and feel pulled together but effortless, because it was effortless. So glad I sized up because the waist is nice and loose in case I eat something makes me feel really full :D ha. In the photo I took to show you how it looks, it doesn't really show it as flattering as these are when you pair them with the right top and shoes--and again, that's when I want a comfy and casual look for errands or coffee, maybe lunch. ------- I usually wear a size 4 but I sized up to a 6 since I wanted a more looser comfortable fit for around the house and maybe errands or possibly coffee out with a friend. I hope the pics will help you to have an idea of fit and style of these jeans. I will note here that I don't find them to be 'stretchy' jeans but likely they will loosen up even more after wearing a while. Fit: For reference, I am 5'2" about 116-117 - my body type is 'curvy/hour-glass' but fit - I'm often called 'a tiny woman' though I see myself as curvy and powerful. Take in to consideration your body type. I have a small waist but if you have a different body type where your waist isn't small in relation to your hips, these may feel constricting at your waist. They come to just right under my belly button, where I like high-waisted pants as I don't like super high waisted. I am glad I did size up since while the legs are wide leg, the waist is 'nipped in' quite a bit so that it fits tighter--the size 6 allows it to be looser and comfortable around my waist area, while the size 4 would have felt too constricting to be loose and comfortable. Would the size 4 have fit me? Yes, but, again, not in a nice loose comfortable way that I wanted. Actually, I laughed when I unrolled them as they looked a bit comical with the wide legs and tiny looking waist (they come rolled up in plastic, which I hate that - it's why I am not always keen on ordering clothes from Amazon - no tags, in stinky plastic, ugh). Length: These come to just below my ankles. My legs are relatively longer than my upper body but I am still short so if you are short like I am, then these aren't going to look cropped. I didn't mind the length. Material: The material is soft and pliable. It's thick but not in a stiff way. It does have 55% cotton but I washed and dried mine on normal cycle so the water was warm and the dryer was very warm, before trying on, as I do everything because I'm just that way. I don't know if they shrank, but if so I believe it was minimal. These did have a chemical odor to them. Not overpowering but it was there, even somewhat after washing so I'll need to wash them twice. Another reason I hesitate ordering clothes from Amazon. Details: The front pockets are 'slit' pockets and I was surprised at how deep they are. The back pockets are the small 'patch' pockets. Other than that they are minimal in design, in a good way. Button and zipper fly that seem sturdy and well made. Stitching looks good. These are sort of style of 'mom jeans' but not really ...? I absolutely do not like the mom jean style and wish that had not come back but things will come and go - I am in my 60s so I've seen a lot of styles come and go! I do miss lower rise jeans as they fit with my shorter torso! But these in the size 6 don't constrict and I achieved that loose comfortable fit I wanted and they aren't too mom jean looking. Color: I almost ordered the darker pair but these light blue are nice too. A little retro. All in all they will be a good pair of jeans to wear when I want to feel comfortable. I wouldn't order another pair though.

So glad I sized up because the waist is nice and loose in case I eat something makes me feel really full :D ha. In the photo I took to show you how it looks, it doesn't really show it as flattering as these are when you pair them with the right top and shoes--and again, that's when I want a comfy and casual look for errands or coffee, maybe lunch. ------- I usually wear a size 4 but I sized up to a 6 since I wanted a more looser comfortable fit for around the house and maybe errands or possibly coffee out with a friend. I hope the pics will help you to have an idea of fit and style of these jeans. I will note here that I don't find them to be 'stretchy' jeans but likely they will loosen up even more after wearing a while. Fit: For reference, I am 5'2" about 116-117 - my body type is 'curvy/hour-glass' but fit - I'm often called 'a tiny woman' though I see myself as curvy and powerful. Take in to consideration your body type. I have a small waist but if you have a different body type where your waist isn't small in relation to your hips, these may feel constricting at your waist. They come to just right under my belly button, where I like high-waisted pants as I don't like super high waisted. I am glad I did size up since while the legs are wide leg, the waist is 'nipped in' quite a bit so that it fits tighter--the size 6 allows it to be looser and comfortable around my waist area, while the size 4 would have felt too constricting to be loose and comfortable. Would the size 4 have fit me? Yes, but, again, not in a nice loose comfortable way that I wanted. Actually, I laughed when I unrolled them as they looked a bit comical with the wide legs and tiny looking waist (they come rolled up in plastic, which I hate that - it's why I am not always keen on ordering clothes from Amazon - no tags, in stinky plastic, ugh). Length: These come to just below my ankles. My legs are relatively longer than my upper body but I am still short so if you are short like I am, then these aren't going to look cropped. I didn't mind the length. Material: The material is soft and pliable. It's thick but not in a stiff way. It does have 55% cotton but I washed and dried mine on normal cycle so the water was warm and the dryer was very warm, before trying on, as I do everything because I'm just that way. I don't know if they shrank, but if so I believe it was minimal. These did have a chemical odor to them. Not overpowering but it was there, even somewhat after washing so I'll need to wash them twice. Another reason I hesitate ordering clothes from Amazon. Details: The front pockets are 'slit' pockets and I was surprised at how deep they are. The back pockets are the small 'patch' pockets. Other than that they are minimal in design, in a good way. Button and zipper fly that seem sturdy and well made. Stitching looks good. These are sort of style of 'mom jeans' but not really ...? I absolutely do not like the mom jean style and wish that had not come back but things will come and go - I am in my 60s so I've seen a lot of styles come and go! I do miss lower rise jeans as they fit with my shorter torso! But these in the size 6 don't constrict and I achieved that loose comfortable fit I wanted and they aren't too mom jean looking. Color: I almost ordered the darker pair but these light blue are nice too. A little retro. All in all they will be a good pair of jeans to wear when I want to feel comfortable. I wouldn't order another pair though.

You guys!!??!! Come on!! You shouldn't have...now everyone is gonna hate me. Listen, I didn't ask them to, I didn't know about it in advance, heck, I didn't even know they were aware of my existence. But obviously, Gap did & does. They went ahead and designed a pair of custom jean leggings made to my exact measurements. Now, even though I plan to buy every single pair of size 6/28, I decided it would only be fair to share my stats with you as well as the stats of MY custom made, comfortable, flattering & 100% perfect fitting jean leggings. Me: 140-145# 5'3" 33.5" waist (measured at the bottom of my bellybutton because that's where MY CUSTOM LEGGINGS sit) 40" hips (measured at the widest point, mid-bum) MY CUSTOM LEGGINGS (Measured flat) 9" rise 14" waist 17" hips They have a REAL zipper fly, not one of those "just for show" kind commonly found on jean leggings. Every pair I've tried in that past with a "fake fly" always had extra fabric that bunched up at the crotch & made me look like I was smuggling oranges whenever I sat down (& they didn't look any better when standing up, either). The traditional 5 pockets are fully functional (real) as well. The stitching/color are absolute perfection & the plain, proportionately sized back pockets are what my denim dreams are made of. If you have time pls check out the pic I attached to see another one of the reasons why these fit so well. I hope my humble opinion helps you in some way. Thank you for reading.

Good quality, good fit, good purchase for busy mom of 6 I like these jeans quite a bit. I broke my foot and wanted something that would fit over my orthopedic boot, and these did great. But I also found them to be very flattering. I'm 5'7" and about 200 lbs, usually a size 14-16, with a slight pear-shaped body and I carry most of my weight in my belly (Mother of six, any surprise?) The 16 fit me nicely, and I don't think I would have been comfortable in a 14. The jeans are fitted through my thigh and knee and open up below that. They just skim the floor when I wear low sneakers or flats, so they can easily be worn with some heels and boots as well. The waist is the definition of mid-rise, coming just above my hips and about to my belly button. After a couple days of wear, they loosened just the tiniest bit, which added to the comfort but didn't have to be hitched up constantly. (That's important to me as a mom!) I got a darker wash, which I like but they kind of turned my hands blue before I washed them. I definitely recommend washing these separately the first time. These aren't "Oh my gosh! Best jeans ever!" But they are very good jeans, and I don't regret the purchase at all, especially at this price, and with the option of Amazon wardrobe. I also tried the Amazon Essentialls mid-rise authentic bootcut jean and preferred these because of the fitted upper leg, which the "authentic" style lacked.

Close but not quite right I liked the color of the faded black and the length was good for me at almost 5'8". What I didn't like: The fabric was not comfortable at all. Maybe it would soften over time? The fabric sort of creased or puckered right under the patch pockets as you can see in the photo. Flattering pants but sizing chart is incorrect I have other colors from this Lee style & wanted the tan color. Despite the material being the same these are a heavier feel. The other colors are more lightweight. Due to weight gain I opted for a size 12 regular rather than a 10. The sizing chart doesn’t fit my body shape at all. Based on their sizing chart with my hips being 45” & my waist being 39” I should’ve ordered a 14 or 16 so take that into account. I’m also 5’6” & weigh 175 pounds. The size 12 regular fits me perfectly as I attached pictures to reflect. The material is 98% cotton & 2% spandex. There is very little stretch to the pants. The material is soft. I launder in cold water on the delicate cycle. I don’t dry these out of fear these will shrink due to the cotton. These do come out of the washer extremely wrinkled despite taking them out immediately. In hanging them to dry the wrinkles don’t come out. These definitely need to be ironed each time. I feel these pants are of quality & have longevity as my other pants truly have. The price is well worth what I paid. I do recommend just be aware of the sizing.

Very disappointed that this didn't fit me how i'd like, they're so comfortable! Okay, so I bought these pants because I needed them for a work/volunteer gig. I had high expectations because I have had other Lee pants and I really want them to fit properly. I'm 5'3 and 110-113lbs, not exactly the curviest person around. I ordered a 4-Short. So...they do fit at the waist. Perfectly, I might add. Not tight, but definitely not loose. Very comfortably, exactly like how I think a pants should fit. However, they are both VERY baggy on my relatively skinny legs and a little bit long even though I bought the short version. When I put it on, I loved how comfortable they were, but disappointed how baggy and unkept they looked on me. I would get a 2, but I doubt the waist would fit. For my short queens (those sub 5'3), you might want to look elsewhere. I have a feeling they'd look rather long on y'all. They are otherwise brilliant pants and great Lee quality. Such a bummer.

Fantastic pants. Let me start with stats: I'm 5'6" 150lbs (athletic build, or pretty straight up n down build but spreading with age 😭so maybe I'm an apple now, lol. Anyway...), a 36DD Bust (usually M top,large in button ups typically) , a 29"waist (usually size 6 in jeans/pants or 4, brand makes a big difference there), and a 31" inseam. I got these in a 6 and if say they for well. There's plenty of stretch to the very soft fabric making these comfortable and flexible. They definitely just fit me comfortably in the waist line (no belt needed) any tighter they'd be uncomfortable. these also are definitely midrise pants which at my age I prefer. The length is perfect for flat or low heel shoe. I washed and dried these and did not notice any shrinkage. They came out softer and ready to wear. My use for these is for camping. Great value. These seem like great everyday pants as well. Breen color is pretty. Brownish green. True to product picture. I would also say these are a they're straight leg pant. These for over my ugh boots snugly, over a hiking boot nicely and with athletic shoes without dragging. I highly recommend these at this price.

Wrinkly, lint-attracting, long-crotch capris These are horrible. Some of the wrinkliest pants I've owned. They attract lint easily. Texture is weird and velvety. Size depends on color I tried the Charcoal, Plaid, Black, and Flax. Nice Leopard Print The leopard jeans are a nice variation to the common plain regular jeans. The leopard print is what I like the most about the jeans. The shape of the jeans is different from my usual skinny jeans and especially the legs and the hip feel a little boxy. I am 5'2" petite, 140lbs, 31" waist and 43" hips, and size 12 fits just right. If I gain a little bit more weight, I won't be able to fit. The jeans feel fine from my waist but there are some creases that form in the front, also the pants are too long but for somebody taller this won't be an issue. The fabric feels a little bit stiff and rough compared to regular jeans but after wearing them for a while they get softer and adapt better to the shape of the body. I wash the jeans in a cold regular cycle and dry them in the permanent press cycle in the dryer. I steam them to remove wrinkles. The pants didn't shrink and preserved the colors after washing.

Fabulous fitting most comfortable sweats in the world. I never ever leave reviews but these turned out to be more than expected. I haven’t washed them yet but I plan to follow the instructions and air dry.. I’d definitely recommend to anyone who’s cold natured and I’ll be buying another pair.

Good Fit! I was skeptical about these because of reviews but honestly not a bad fit! For reference I’m 5’1, 130 pounds. Bought a Medium. Plenty of room in the butt and crotch. I was disappointed I was really excited to try these since my family literally calls me an ice cube (freezing all the time), and these looked perfect. Well, when I tried them on, they were WAY too short. I am 5’7” for reference. The leggings also made its own pudge when I bent forward, and I felt like they overemphasized my crotch area pretty much hiking up either side of it. I know the wrinkles might come out with a wash, but with all the things I didn’t like about them especially the length which can’t be fixed, I didn’t want to wash them and then be unable to return them.

A lovely pair of nice, thick, warm leggings! Based on the sizing charts I bought these leggings in a L. For reference I'm 5'6 approx. 145lbs, generally a M in tops and bottoms, a 6/8 in jeans. The waist measurements provided made me think I should prob err on the side of caution and go up one. These "work" but I may have been slightly better off with a medium as the crotch is a little long and baggy if I don't pull them absolutely up as far as they will go. But otherwise, these leggings are nice and thick and warm and the fleece lining is divine in these colder months. I do maybe wish they were slightly thinner so I could wear them as a base layer to some jeans or athletic pants for hiking, sledding etc, but as-is they will just be a nice pair of leggings to pair with boots, sweaters, and tunics!

It fits! Was surprised to order skinny jeans online and actually have them fit but these were almost perfect. Only downside was the front pockets are fake and sewn shut. Why don’t women ever get real pockets?! I’m 5’4 (27” inseam) 140 lbs and the size 30 was perfect.

FINALLY FOUND REGULAR LOW-RISE JEANS YAYYYY Delivered yesterday, already ordered another pair. These jeans are perfect…the length, the feel, the quality. 5/5 stars! Ordered size 30 regular, I am 5’3, 155 lbs and wanted low-rise jeans, so I could wear a blouse and a belt. Not a big fan of high rise jeans with belt and tucked in blouses. Call me old fashion IDC! LOL

They’re OKAY — good for spring/summer 👖 Pros: Comfortable fabric Deep front pockets (standard 5 all around) Great look for work or play — dress em up or down They fit well in the thigh/hip/butt/leg despite the waist band gap for me Cons: I don’t find them to be for those like me who lack in hips and are more stacked in the back. Be prepared for a waistband gap. Inseam (appx 27”) is a bit short for my (5’ 8-9ish”) liking, but that’s obvious from the pics. I plan to wear with flats/skimmers.

Order 1 size up if in between sizes I ordered these because I needed a black work pant and I am so glad I did! I am between a 31 and a 30 size wise, and ordered the 31. They fit PERFECTLY but there is no waistband stretch so if you are comfortably a size 31, then order a size 32. I loved the fit, the cut, and the pant length. Also, it goes up right to my belly button, so this is ideal for a business appropriate pant and can accommodate tucking in a blouse. See (unflattering) pictures for front and side perspectives. These hit right at my ankles (for reference, I am 5'8) so I didn't feel like they were high waters like the other pair of dockers I've owned. Material is soft and smooth. 98% cotton, 2% elastane based on the tag. Value on these? Awesome! They retail for $58 and the price was $39 on Amazon.
Wash is weird 5’ 4”, 155 lbs, size 12, grey wash. Fit is good but the acid wash is a weird slat pattern

Great fit! I usually NEVER order jeans on-line; they are all made so differently and can fit so differently, but I already had a pair of Democracy jeans so I thought staying with the same brand might help. I love these jeans - they are SO comfortable! I am 5'9", 155# and the 10 fit perfectly.

Nice! Runs slightly big though. I am size (US)4. Normally I will order a small, and once in awhile my voluptuous bottom is a little too big for a small. But with these cargo pants it seems they are quite baggy and loose on me overall. I think there is definitely room if you order a small and you're somewhere between a small and medium. A medium would have been way too big for me. I'm 5'6" and these are almost too long for me, so if you're shorter than I am, you will probably need to roll them up. They are very well made from very durable cotton. I love the huge side pockets. The front pockets are also decent in size. The zipper works smoothly and the closure button is solid. Overall, I'm pleased with them, especially since they're 100% cotton.
